These are wonderful little containers for travel. They are very easy to fill and dispense from, when you are ready to use your lotion or shampoo or facial cleanser, but they are also the right size to give you enough for a trip. The bottles come in different colors, so you can sort of color-code your products, however, they don't include enough colors. As a result, there are really only four bottles that can be used for travel, without labeling. Given that three are the white color, all of those will be for hand lotion. They are really convenient for attaching to a hook on a bag, which is great for me because my hands dry out so easily. Since I know what color the original bottles are, for my products, I love that they are different colors, but, again, they need more colors. My shampoo is blue. My body wash is white. My facial cleanser is green. My lotion is orange. The list goes on. I am giving these five stars because they work very well, have a nice thick top portion to hold that clip, so that they don't break off, and the clips themselves are nice medium-duty clips that really hold the little bottles in place. These are high quality for a great price and made with food-grade silicone, so they are non-toxic and won't leach any chemicals into my products, which means I won't be putting anything bad on my skin. Even though you might have to separate them out, unless you use products with color additives like pink-colored strawberry scented shampoo, and only use four for travel, while the other two are for different use, they are still really well made and easy to fill and dispense and attach and carry around. I highly recommend these. I look forward to seeing more colors and more multi-color sets. That's basically what we all need isn't it, for everything to come in different sets with more choices as to customizing the variations in the sets that we purchase. Some would want all pink.  Some would want all blue. Some would want a rainbow. I need the rainbow, and still these are five stars, because they work so very well.

I looked up the ingredients for this product at the Environmental Working Groups (EWG) Skin Deep data base & the following ingredients either rated as not green or I couldn't find them (0=green or safe, 10=red or dangerous):

CYCLOPENTASILOXANE (3) - Persistent, bioaccumulative in wildlife, classified as expected to be toxic or harmful, uterine tumors in female rats, increased anogenital distance observed in rats at a moderate dose, moderate dose in rate suggested dopamine D2-receptor behavior on pituitary cells, suspected to be an environmental toxin and be persistent or bioaccumulative.
POLYSORBATE-60 (3)
DIPEPTIDE-3 AMINOHEXANOATE - Can't find this ingredient on EWG.
ACETYL ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN - Can't find this ingredient on EWG.

I looked up DIPEPTIDE-3 AMINOHEXANOATE on DuckDuckGo & I guess it's a new skin ingredient. It might be why it & ACETYL ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN are not on EWG as they haven't been evaluated for safety. I really don't know though, it's just speculation on my part. As for safety, who knows? I rely on EWG to let me know. This is frustrating.

I've used this several evenings as a night cream & I like it, but I don't love it. I heat my home exclusively with a wood stove. Even running humidifiers & essential oil diffusers 24/7, the air is painfully dry. So far this cream seems pretty good on my middle aged skin. The funny thing is, out of all the various brands I've tried on my face, a little known handmade brand has by far served me best. It's a brand called Eruzile or Progress in Health. They have two names. I don't know why. It's really annoying. They are on Etsy & last I checked, have their own website but for some foolish reason, it seems to be more expensive to shop at their website. And to be clear, I am not affiliated with them, I'm just a long time customer. Another brand I like but it is ridiculously expensive, so I hardly ever buy it is Juice Beauty Stem Cellular. You can get it right here on amazon.

Okay, back to this brand. It is a light/thin feeling cream. Not greasy. It seems to absorb well. My skin does feel a tiny bit tight when I get up in the morning. I find a few spritz with rose water helps. This is packaged in a glass pot that you use your fingers to apply. A little plastic in the lid, so not Zero Waste, but not bad. It is not scented. It does have a smell as is normal because the ingredients will have their own smell to them, but when applying I don't notice the smell, so calling it unscented or fragrance free is more than reasonable. I only notice its smell if I'm practically sticking my nose in the pot & inhaling deeply.

For me, this isn't moisturizing enough for the dead of winter, but for spring & fall I expect it will serve me fine.

Overall, I would have rated this higher if not for the ingredient concerns.

I wanted to know what Jaborandi is, so I looked it up. It's a plant and the active ingredient is pilocarpine. I looked that up too:

"Pilocarpine has been known to cause excessive salivation, sweating, bronchial mucus secretion, bronchospasm, bradycardia, vasodilation, and diarrhea. Eye drops can result in brow ache and chronic use in miosis.

Systemic injection of pilocarpine can compromise the blood brain barrier allowing pilocarpine to gain access to the brain which can lead to chronic epilepsy."

Yeah, no thanks. I'm 50% sure this conditioner wouldn't make my scalp sweat more, but why risk it? I'll pass. This is why we google first, class.

I was pleasantly surprised and extremely satisfied with this brush. First a note on the pics. I took the before pic on the left after hair had been washed, dried, brushed with a regular brush, with no product (oil, conditioner, balm, etc.) put into the beard. I then used the brush and took the pic before applying any product to style it. For something to straighten your beard quickly on days you don't wash it, this was very easy to use. It heats up fast (about a min) and within about 1-2 minutes, I was done.

As you will notice, my beard is shorter on the sides and even my chin area isn't super long. I found the shorter bristles on this brush compared to bigger brushes meant for women's hair to work better for my beard. Also, I found it did a great job if you go slowly and comb UP from under the chin first. Then go back and brush it slowly down the other side. The extension cord is plenty long. The only downside of that is that it can make storage a challenge. I don't like wrapping the cord around the brush itself. So I had picked up some cheap velcro cord wraps from Amazon a few years ago and added one to the cord to make it easier to put away.

I bought this to keep at the office. But for the price, I think I'll go back and pick up one more for the house and another to keep in my gym bag. I'm very happy with it and would recommend it to any guy with a short to medium beard.

For the record, I am a longtime Physicians Formula wearer, and their products can be hit or miss. I am not being paid to leave a review, or representing anyone but myself here. This is the first time I tried PF's countouring powder, and I've used it for about three seasons. I like it. I don't love it.

I have very pale skin with pink undertones that is uber-sensitive and I have to be very careful about sun exposure. Anything can make me break out in hives or eczema, at any time, even if I used it daily for years. Thankfully, PF products have never done that to me, in almost a decade.

As for this product, the shades are where I take some issue. They worked best for my tone in summer, but now, in winter -- I'm shopping for a different product.

* The dark shade is really mahogany-like, so I have to blend, blend, blend in daylight or risk looking like I smeared mud on my face. It's good for shading jawline and cheekbones with a light touch at temples if you need it. Beware, though. If you are very pale, you want a very light touch with this one.

*The medium shade is very subtle and helps blend and soften the dark. It also works well as a matte bronzer, around sides of the nose, and wherever you need just a little definition. This is the best shade of the bunch, honestly. At least for my skin tone.

* The light shade is, frankly, worthless to me. It gives my skin a pasty, chalky look and looks heavy. Yuck. I occasionally use it in tiny spots as a concealer, but as a contour powder, it just looks like I put on way too much setting powder or something. Instead, I use my lightest shade of matte eyeshadow (also a PF product) and a higher-end illuminator for highlighting. Those results are beautiful, frankly, even on my 38 year old skin.

All in all, PF's contour colors are suitable for pale skin, but may be better on other slightly darker or warmer skin tones, or on summer tanned skin.

It is a little more narrow so it's a teensy bit less comfortable to hold. I have carpel tunnel. I've been grooming cats furever & grooming English angora rabbits for well over a decade.

🐱For short & long haired cats, this brush works great. All of my cats love this brush & my Doodles brand. I can comb all of them except for my 14yo with a surprising amount of pressure & they roll around purring. Tons of hair gets picked up into the tines. Unlike some styles of brushes & combs, no hair is left behind around the tail area.

Removing the hair from the brush is very easy. Start at one side from the bottom to loosen & then move to the other side. Keep working your way around & it comes off in a nice clean loose mat. I actually prefer these types of slicker brushes over self cleaning brushes & also over brushes with the little plastic beads on the ends. If you groom your animal properly, you don't need little balls on the ends of the tines. Those balls also make it a little harder to get the brush fully clean of hair.

Cosmetically, I like the look of the brush much better than my Doodles brush. This is all black with a more classy branding in small white text on the head. We Love Doodles is blue & loudly brands itself on the head.

🐰I don't recommend this style of brush for English angora rabbits. I have found that my bunnies seem to be more comfortable with smaller slickers with shorter tines. You'd think with their dense long coats, you'd want something large with long tines, but it really doesn't work out like you'd expect. At least for me it doesn't.

🐰Recommended for English Angora Rabbits:

🐇Doggy Man sells a small soft slicker brush that my angora rabbits love. It's plastic with a little blue ring on the handle. I have purchased/replaced many of them over the years (the handles tend to break over time) because they work well.
🌐 https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B001NLVFH2
🐇I also recommend if money allows, a small slicker brush by Chris Christensen. I've never needed to replace it.
🌐 https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B00GU7NKWW

Personally, I'd shop between this brand (Rita's), We Love Doodles, & Chris Christensen. Chris Christensen is top of the line. They are worth it if you have the money. We Love Doodles has been the middle ground for me. They were originally priced at $50, but the price has been a lot lower for a while now. Rita's is currently the second most expensive & probably the lowest quality of the three brands. However, I've been using it for the least amount of time, so it's too early to know for sure. So far I like all three.

Chris Christensen: Made in Germany $75.99
https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product//B00KAJTPZ2

We Love Doodles: Made in USA $29.99
https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B09G981NXV

Rita's: Made in China $36.99

I've used tepezcohuite for years and purchased creams and ointments in Mexico with truly amazing medicinal properties, but I was absolutely disappointed with this product. It is clearly a petroleum base that smells and has the texture of machinery grease. It doesn't have the color of the real bark, and since there are no ingredients listed, my guess is, it's petroleum jelly with some coloring. I'm surprised Amazon sells it without any information at all.

The positive reviews are either fake or people don't realize that if you put any type of oil or grease on your skin it will keep the moisture in. But this product will never do what real tepezcohuite can. I'd never put this grease on my skin, so it was a complete waste of money.

In summary, I like the comb even though the quality could be better. A wider, longer more comfortable handle made with a stronger wood would have been pawsome. But for a casual grab to comb a visiting fur baby while I'm on the couch - it's purrfect!

I have sensitive, combination skin. My forehead is dry, my cheeks are normal, and my nose and chin are oily. Talk about a headache... Over the years, I've been searching high and low for a "one size fits all" primer that can smooth out any dry patches, keep my natural oils at bay, and of course, be grippy so that my makeup can actually be long lasting! Unfortunately, I've yet to find one that can cater towards all of my skin's needs...this primer included.
As a primer, the product is honestly so-so. A primer, to me, needs to provide a great base for your makeup but also let your makeup be long lasting. This primer is great for sensitive, dry skin as a BASE. First off, it has the loveliest of scents and a very lightweight texture that seems to make your skin feel very fresh and bouncy. It smooths over dry patches and seems to fill it in to the point that your texture is nice and smooth. On it's own, it provides a beautiful layer of hydration and glow.
Though the hydration is nice for normal skin, I don't find it good as a primer as it can make you feel borderline oily, and that's something that I simply don't like.
For oily skin, BEWARE! This will make you look as if you have spent the day running a marathon as it has the ability to pull out all of your natural oils, even if you do set your makeup with powder!

[[VIDEOID:c049c5d22edce6fa891d4093e384169f]] THE most beautiful shoe I have ever owned! I have a variety of unique shoes (most especially animal face shoes) from mice, to cats, pandas, and puppies. These flats have got to be my favorite above them all! The quality of the shoes are AMAZING! The flats are sturdy and so far, they haven't created any bent marks from the start of the toes, to the end of them. The animal face stitching is stiff, which is a good thing, in my opinion. No loose pieces of thread sticking out from either side. The animal face patch/stitching doesn't hit you on the inside of the shoe. There's this fabric on the opposite side that protect the top of your foot from the stitching itself. The inside of the shoe is just as kawaii as the outside. It's branded, "Cute to the Core" and the sole is surrounded by gold trimming. So pretty and so effortless done. I have wide (W) feet. I have somewhat of an arch, but not enough to where I could fit a regular standard shoe comfortably. These flats accommodate regardless. There's great support and I've yet to get any blisters or have run in to areas of the flats that are too tight or too snug. The underside of the shoe (this is specifically for people with wide feet) or the outsole basically... Is the only one that touches the ground. I have had and still have many flats where the "vamp" or the edges of the shoes have touched the ground as well, because my feet are wide. These shoes, however, have not. I have worn them a total of 6 (six) times in 6 different occasions. I have received countless amounts of compliments! I wear character stockings with anything midi or mini so I have no experience wearing these flats barefoot. With stockings on, however, they fit perfectly and molded to my feet charmingly and comfortably. The only concern with these shoes that I have, is that they run a half size too small. I am a true 8 1/2 (US WOMEN'S) and I have had my feet sized with a Brannock Device at multiple shoe stores. Is this a problem to me, personally? No. As a first time buyer? It was a little concerning, but thanks to all of the reviews pointing this out, I'm glad I overcame the uncertainty. So for those who are in the "half" sizes, I do suggest sizing UP. My "Cute to the Core" animal head flats came in a black shoe box. Even the shoe box is stylish and trendy! Overall, my experience with these shoes have been an amazing one! It isn't so often that I find shoes or flats in general that fit me well, and fit me comfortably. These flats hit every positive check mark on my list of what to look for in a shoe. On top of the stylish and fashionable look these flats carry throughout every trend season, they are, "100% VEGAN LEATHER: These premium flats are an earth-conscious product" (as stated in the product description. This claim is not on the box though. The only information on the shoe box were the size, color, and style.) There was no product guarantee in the product description during the time of my purchase. EDIT: I've tried to visit their website to add references in my review but it is currently unavailable at the moment.

[[VIDEOID:c00536a031089a270c567f676bf9a934]] These shoes are a size 9. I am a size 8 1/2 wide (W). If you generally wear a wide like I do, and are a half size, I would suggest sizing up. The width of the shoe is definitely not the problem. The arch is so minimal, I think those who do have an arch would find these slip on shoes somewhat too big (in my opinion). The only concern I have with these slip on shoes is the material folded on the inside of the shoe, underneath the seam. This is located at the heel. The seam is folded inward and a very small piece of fabric beneath the seam irritates my skin with or without socks. The fold of that panel lifts while I am mobile. Whenever I lift my feet off the group, the panel follows. This irritates my skin to where it feels like a blister will form. My forth attempt in these shoes, I had to switch out. The shoes hurt my heel too much. There was too much friction. I'm looking for ways to minimize this, so if you have any suggestions as to what I could do, I will appreciate them. Other than that, the fit is extremely comfortable. The sole of the shoe is about an inch and a half in length, and will add height so keep that in mind. The cat print on the front side of the shoe is even more pigmented in person. The video I provided for references does the cat print no justice. There is no irritation on the inside of the shoe behind the tongue or the elastic. The fold you see in the front of my shoe is from walking. It really depends how you walk if you're worried about a "fold" creating in the front of the slip on shoe (the fold is not from the shoe being tight. like I said it is comfortable and loosely fitted in the slightest, especially for someone who wears a wide (W), generally). Overall, besides the panel lifting from beneath the seam inside the shoe, everything else seems to be spot on and perfect! I love these shoes, and YRU never disappoints when it comes to beautiful and unique shoes.
There was no product guarantee during the time of my purchase. EDIT: Please refer to the video for more information on these shoes.

On the other hand, the screen seems more delicate so I'll have to see how it holds up over time. I clean it every time I use the dryer and vacuum it at least once a month.

The plastic the new frame is made of appears to be a little lighter density plastic than the original frame . The reason I'm replacing the original filter is because the frame cracked in two places. Based on other reviews, I opted to select Amazon to fulfill my order. I've now had 2 separate wick filter orders come from Amazon, and both do appear to be Vornado brand filters, so I am pleased.  They come in a Vornado box, and are wrapped in plastic. Unfortunately, both filters are in the same plastic bag, so once it's open, it's open. No weird smells, or anything weird or funky about them. I uploaded photos of the box.  The filters fit my Vornado humidifier just the same as the ones that came with it originally.  I have the Evap2 humidifier, so you do have to bend & squish the filter at first to get it to bend to the curved shape of the humidifier, but it then pops up and fits perfectly.  The filters themselves do not last as long as you would hope. There are plenty of tricks out there for extending their life, usually relying on vinegar, citrus, baking soda, etc.  Of course, Vornado and others sell bottled solutions too if you'd rather go that route. The price on Vornado's site has come down to be about the same as Amazon's.

We bought this as part of a major kitchen remodel. In general, we've been very happy. We love having more countertop available. The reputation of induction is well documented. Couple of issues: with only one large burner, it is tough to have more than one large skillet or pan going at once. I wanted to love the bridge element, but when I put the cast iron griddle to make pancakes, the area in the middle (the bridge) shows serious temperature differential. Also, attempting to put two elements on a high setting (8 or. 9) you can watch them cycle back and forth with one pot boiling while the other slows down, then switches back. I would buy induction again, but I would rather have one less burner , but two big ones and two small ones.
The flavor of the ice is crisp and clean, no after taste. Very easy to use and maintain.

THIS spin dryer is a larger version of the ones they have at country clubs that you can put your swimsut in and it will wring all the water out of it in record time. Only this holds quite a bit more. It's quieter than a regular dryer.

I got this to use with a 5 cup Chemex to replace disposable paper filters. I am a big proponent of eliminating one-time-use goods as much as possible and this seems like a high quality, stainless steel filter that will last a long time. Hopefully, for the life of the Chemex.
This is a really great filter for a lot of reasons. First of all, it looks nice and seems to be well made. The surfaces and edges are all smooth with no scratchy or bumpy cut off or soldered bits. The rubbery ring at the top more or less matches the wood on the Chemex and fits perfectly and snugly in the top. I think it's really strange that the other reviewers just put this in a mug - doesn't the coffee come out of the sides of the filter and leak all over the cup?
Secondly, the mesh is very fine and does not let any grounds through. When I ordered it I expected it to be a wire mesh, but this is actually more like perforated metal with very small holes. You can see in the picture I included how small they are. No coffee grounds or silt ended up at the bottom of the Chemex of coffee. Amazing!
Thirdly, this replaces paper filters. Paper filters are compostable but you are still creating demand for single use paper products, something I strive to reduce from my life. If you make coffee every day, you are using 365 paper filters which had to be treated with tons of water and chemicals. The manufacture of this metal filter obviously uses some resources, but it is a one time thing and will last for years (I think). Also when you use paper filters, you should not pour water directly on the paper to eliminate the paper flavor. This eliminates paper flavor :)
I love doing pourover coffee. It's very satisfying to watch the foam develop when you pour in hot water and I think it makes for a very flavorful and clean tasting cup of coffee.

Updated 2/26/2014:

After using three seperate WSS-2 filters with the fix described below, I was not able to completely get rid of the noise when dispensing water and ended up buying another brand (which does not have the noise problem). What is described below may work for some people experiencing the noise but for me my water pressure is too high to fully mitigate.

Here is my original review...

This review is focused on problem w/WSS-2 filters causing loud buzzing/rattle noise when dispensing water and/or ice maker cycles on some Samsung's models, most frequent if you have high water pressure. The fix detailed below addressed the problem for me intially but then air periodically builds up and noise occurs every few days for 5-10 seconds and then dissipates. The Engineer I emailed said that the higher the water pressure, the more likely the noise will persist. The fix is better than without but not perfect.

Water Sentinel Engineer shipped me prototype "ring" to address problem. It is a plastic ring that snaps onto the "handle area" of filter (added to customer images a close-up picture of ring). It is simple to snap on and fits securely. For folks who care about looks, this addresses gap on front of refrigerator (filter w/out ring has narrower diameter than OEM). Ring covers the gap and looks as good as OEM (IMO).

Engineer explained that noise is due to horizontal mounting of filter and small amount of air remaining trapped in filter (even after following purging instructions). Does not harm filter/refrigerator but annoying. He said that purging another 2-3 gallons may lessen the potential for noise.

I asked about plans to make ring available to others and here is response "... we will ship one of these rings free to anyone experiencing a similar problem. We may even include a Ring part in each product box, but that [has not been decided yet]." You can contact Water Sentinel via email if you do not have a ring and they will ship you one.

Customer service was outstanding. Response <24 hours to my email and I received rings next day! Engineer followed up with me with additional details and answered all of my questions.

Other reviews also covered fit of filter, filtering performance, and longevity. Fit is same as OEM I replaced (with the ring of course). No issues with leaking after installing. I believe performance/longevity will depend on water quality/pressure. I cannot comment on whether these are better at filtering or how long they last compared to OEM. Given the price, even if they lasted ½ as long, they are a better value for the price. I have good quality water and high pressure and I see no degradation in flow and my water tastes great. I have been using WSS-1 style filters on my other refrigerator for years and they have served me well.

[[VIDEOID:142907a9d94c6de3d373a3f3d9b4d0fa]] This washing system really works! You can see it agitating your laundry just like an electric washing machine! And it's good exercise!

There's something soothing about using this thing. You can develop your own rhythm, and switch over to "high speed wash" anytime you want! Also work smarter- use the "soak" feature and let the water do a lot of the work.

It's not a small washer- it's about the size of an outdoor garbage can. It fits easily in my RV shower and I just take it out to use the shower.

The more water you use the easier it will be to churn your laundry. But you can use the same wash water for several loads, and then dump that out (I put the water in my toilet to help clean out the black tank), and then use the same rinse water a few times over. Just scoop the bubbles of soap off the top, add a little more fresh water, and you can easily do three or four loads w/o starting over with new water each time.

This works best in an RV site with full sewer hookups, so you can just empty the used water in your grey or black tanks. If you are off grid, then there are environment friendly soaps you can buy so that you can dispose of your wash water safely.

I would recommend this for anyone who wants to avoid laundromats. They are so expensive these days!

They're cheap, the paint isn't bad. You just have to be ridiculously delicate when closing them (or good at making little aluminum foil caps).

Yes, they will "work" if you're finicky, but even then half of them will smash up into un-usable shapes that couldn't possibly snap into anything, because the metal is just too cheap -- it bends up too easily. If you do manage to get everything to work properly (and this takes a lot of effort, all the more annoying because you're thinking the whole time how stupidly simple it is), the "ideal" snap is still too weak!

Altogether, it's junk.
All 3 leak

Worth the price and can save you lots of money by preventing a lost of an expensive earrings . It's a must for fish hook earrings !
It is amazing to me why such a thing not always included with some fish hook style earrings !
Glad Amazon offers such a variety of items ! Thanks Amazon !

I am not an artist. But I do love crafts and I love painting. This kind of paint was a bit intimidating, but I figured at the very least, I might end up using this set to touch up some old artwork I have. But first I had to try it out and I used the smallest paintbrush that came with the set.

I love this paint and I wish I'd had it all along. Mostly I've used acrylic craft paints, I haven't painted anything too difficult mostly because it just is really hard and I always felt that my dabbling was beyond amateurish, it was sorta ugly, so I stuck to simple stuff; stenciling a box, fixing scratch with some paint, I never did more that wander down the art aisles at Michael's because I wasn't qualified to use anything there.

But this paint is so forgiving and it isn't clumpy like acylic, it is thicker, but more puddingish and you can thin it if you want to layer some colors. All of the colors in this set are metalic and I though it would look good to layer some gold on top of the coppery paint I'd already used.

I should also mention how fast drying this paint is, fast drying could be a blessing or a curse, I mostly think it is amazing, I was able to finish a small artwork in an hour because of this feature. Any mistakes you make are easily remedied because you can simply paint over them!

Something else about the metallic paints in this set, they are really, really metallic. I've bought several metallic acrylic paints and to be honest, they are not very impressive, especially in the darker colors. But each metallic color from copper to pink is shimmery with metallic sparkliness, I love it!

Now for my only misgiving about this set. Each little paint pot has an adhesive cover, the covers are surprisingly difficult to peel back and once you have it is super unclear how you store the unused portion of paint without a replacement cover. I made sure to keep the covers partly attached, but it made it ackward to remove tape while simultaneously holding the lid peeled. Also I do not know if this exposure to the air will fully dry out this paint when I have barely used any, it is a major concern.

It won't turn red, only magenta - no matter how much I use. I see searching google this is common for this product. Epic fail.

This may work with transparent resin. My results are from the white resin.

I've abandoned this products and now only use oil paint to color my resin.

Update: Giving it 3 stars because I can get pink now by using the tiniest amount possible on a popsicle stick and stiring it in. And I meandthe TINIEST amount.

It did the job, I got about 300 6" cuts in thick suede leather out of one blade before I needed a lot of force.

But, it's not a super-quality handle, and not worth $20. It's not falling apart, but it's definitely not better than the other rotary cutters on Amazon for $10.
Some of the product came damaged. Not every piece in every section but a few in each are broken.
It really pretty feels great but comes unwrapped in anything.
Same as headline
Not sturdy. No matter how careful or gently worked, the crimp covers kept breaking.
Arrived dirty and had a number written in pencil probably from the factory. I went to wash it before I put it into the holder and the whole image came off.
These are very poor quality. I will never buy again. At least 25/30% of the pieces are malformed and not useable.

Making fabric bowls is intriguing. I have not made anything directly from this book, but the information is clear and there are a lot of variations to try.  A good book for the reference shelf in my craft room.
Hated it!
Tiny and not worth the price.
I bought these for under my Grandson's Christmas tree. They are really cute and will add to his Polar Express theme. They appear to be as durable as can be expected for the price.
A lot of colors but dries very chalky even with very lite application OK for kids but not for a adult who wants vibrant colors. The paint dries chalky!
It's bad when I get the colors really small high price

For some people, that might not make a difference, but if you're doing projects based off a 6x6" square, you're going to come up short. I had to waste a second sheet to get the number of strips I needed for my layout.

As the other reviewer already noted, this is a nice reference but hardly 'Master classes'. The print is light, and the font size is 8 or 9 - very difficult to read. I love "Knitter" magazine and had been hoping that this would comparable.

Watercolor

There is a lot packed into this zippered case, perhaps a little over full. Most of the tools are adequate, similar to what one would find in sets at a craft store. The chain pliers and round nose pliers are pretty good. When I tested the wire cutters on a head pin, they didn’t snap it off, had to bend it slightly to break it. Suppose that they would be ok on thinner wire. All of the rest of the tools lined up ok, and the small size of everything makes it good for travel to craft shows for repair, etc.  There are lots of findings in the plastic compartment case, which is also handy. Probably good for beginners or as an alternate set.
Pretty, but very, very thin, like tissue paper.

First, I ordered the Peacock with the head facing right. I like some things and did not like some things. First I was unhappy with the color of the background. In many pictures it looks pale grey, that's what I was hoping for too, but I got a background that looks too dark and almost like a little grey/mauve tone. It's ugly against the blue peacock. It looks dingy next to the peacocks blue tones. The kit came with all the colors and was numbered okay. I did finish pretty quick because it was only a partial drill, and had plenty of dots. The packets had some random tiny pieces of squares and leftover (too tiny) dots, excess waste mixed in but the worst was some were stuck together and impossible to get apart, that kept happening, so annoying. The background had plenty of tackiness to stick the dots down, and the peacock is pretty when finished. The opening for this is not standard size (19x11.5) not sure I want to pay for custom framing for this. The peacock is pretty some are not bothered by the things that bothered me, but I will probably go with a name brand company if I do another one.
The roller is fine but the picture makes you believe you're going to receive two rollers.
Only kind to buy. Durable and the price was better than the local store.

Amazon offered me lightning deal forfew bucks off Norberg & Linden XXL 125 colored pencils. I never heard of them so I took a chance for another budget friendly set to try out. I was The pencil barrel says the are the Creyarts. If I knew that I would not have bought them. To get the vivid colors I achieved in my diy composition swatch book I had to bear down hard forever and kept getting breakage when I sharpen up the tip. I only colored flowers with them because they did not do well on Amazon ceatespace coloring book paper. I gave up and switched to my Black Widows do rest. I think they are going to OK for my young grandkids when they color with grandma. I do not recommend. The 3 pencils I used for flowers are now much shorter than rest for barely any use

Very dissatisfied with my purchase of the Money and Kadinsky sets . Eight duplicate colors that overlap. I read description and it was not mentioned anywhere that some colors overlap each set . Cadmium yellow , yellow ochre , flesh deep , purple Lake , mauve , ultramarine , mulberry and Cobalt all were in both sets ! set was nice and low in price but one was bent and hard to get it straight again

Cute decorative sewing set, with several small tools included in a gift type box.  Since I am very familiar with sewing items I was interested to see this. The needle threaders are cute flower shapes, the thimble is an open top, ring type which is not my preference. The measuring tape is just a flat, white one, not retractable. Round container of a hand sewing needle assortment, standard style. Probably the most interesting items are the scissors, a stork and a unicorn.  They are quite colorful and fun, and good for small projects,  light use. Obviously unusual and perhaps even collectible, or certainly a nice gift set.

PanPastel really needs to work on their outer packaging, it is ineffective for mail-order shipments. My set arrived with Burnt Sienna powder coating EVERYTHING. All of the sponge blenders are already full of Burnt Sienna and the knife tool and each of the other pots of color were coated. What a mess. The way the pots stack and screw into one another is pretty cool, but the "palette tray" is just flimsy molded plastic I don't see any utility in it, including keeping the pigment in place during shipping.
So far I've only tried the Ultramarine Blue to put in a background on a mixed media piece. Blending it on Bristol didn't work too well but I expect it's better on more textured papers.

These really are mediocre. At first these caps looked pretty good, but when I tried using them, the shapes just didn't seem to fit my 6mm beads they just didn't look good - too pointy looking and cheap. You get what you pay for.

The kit came complete with all the colors, the flamingo looks great. The background for the flamingo is white, and because this is a partial drill and it was supposed to look like a watercolor in the background I wasn't sure. Well it's not very good. At the bottom left no dots go there and it's not really legible that it's supposed to be a watercolor/loose style, strange. The pros, everything comes with it, had plenty of dots and all that was supposed to be in the kit. Flamingo is easy to do for a first timer and it was fun. Looks cute when your done. Cons, as I mentioned the non dot areas of the design, also this is an odd size so have to do some calculating for figuring how to frame it.

I purchased all three sets plus both set of skin tones . Love the stunning coordinate colors ! On a y one complaint. I received two Flat Black and missing the Cobra color in the Cobra set . How can I get missing pencil ?

I received these beautiful watercolor tubes today and just LOVE them. A big shout out to all the wonderful artists and crafters that gave such positive reviews for the ShinHan watercolor. This is very nice ribbon. The color is pretty true to the picture. Just wish it came in 2" wide size.
Sticks to anything, holds like hell

This book has some pretty examples of art dolls in the gallery.  I got the book for the mermaid pattern, and I do like the shape of the basic doll pattern, it makes a very well-shaped doll.  It is a little tricky to get the darts positioned properly to shape the torso, however.  If this author's books are lacking, it is in instructions.  Two piece legs and arms? How to join the pieces together? It is not explained.  Fortunately, I have made many dolls and do not rely on someone else's directions, but for someone who wants more information, it is not clear.  I know that this book is about beading, and that is pretty and nice, but I do not personally like the beaded face, or understand the need for a beaded tatoo.  All in all, these books are good eye candy and the patterns serve as a jumping off point for creating one's own dolls, and adapting and making some of one's own patterns.
This is probably a good learning book for children. I got it to teach my grandchildren, they took off with it, and I have not seen it since.
Better quality than most lower priced fans- recommend.

So far so good, these are great dots of adhesiveness and I have found so many uses for them around the house;

I'm using one above the light switch so that I don't accidentally flip on the garbage disposal. I was thinking of buying one of those light up lightswitches, but having a scratchy side out dot above lightswitch is doing just as well.

I used a few to hang decorative christmas lights from my fence, outside, they are still there and its been a couple of days now.

I've used two for repairing a christmas wreath and another for fixing a hook in my pantry that was listing sideways.

My next plan is to use it on a shadow box, I think the dots are small enough that they will be unseen from the front or sides of my shadow box.

The possibilities are endless.

I’m just learning tatting and find this product very nice to work with. This Monet colorway is beautiful, and I also really love the Pastels colorway. I’d recommend this product to all Crochet and Tatting crafters. Arrived as promised, and in great condition. I’d purchase from this vendor again.

This is a phenomenal poster that I bought for my son. The colors are vibrant and the characters are like the ones in the comics, creepy and detailed.

It isn't a giant poster, but then again, I'm not sure even my kiddo would like a larger version of this. Some of the lanterns in the poster are downright scary, I would never have wanted a poster like this when I was his age (I had Strawberry shortcake and Holly Hobby). I'm unable to get the gold and silver lobster clasps to open.  As others have mentioned, the open jump rings are impossible to close correctly.  I bought this to make simple repairs to the jewelry we sell at a thrift store so it will probably fit that need.  You get what you pay for.

Jessica Smith is my new favorite walking instructor. She has a lot of energy and makes each session of walking fun and entertaining. In this DVD you will find four main workouts, each 15 minutes long.

The Fast Fat Burn is high energy and it whittles the waist. Jessica is giggly and talkative but gets the walking done fast.

Belly Blaster has footwork that is a bit fancy and Jessica's shoes are bright hot pink. The standing crunches in this workout are excellent.

Power Pick Up is energizing and gets the lymph flowing as you are doing a lot of arm raises.

Dance Party doesn't have any complicated steps and is not overly sensual. It is more country music inspired.

With addictive moves and motivating music you will be inspired to do two workouts at a time. This is great if you only have 30 minutes to exercise. Highly Recommended!

~The Rebecca Review

I have tried for three days to make the machine work properly.  I assumed there is a learning curve and I tried over and over again to get it to work well.  Just when I thought it was working properly, it began to drop stitches and finally the entire project - a scarf - only a few inches from being finished - dropped off the entire machine.  Tensioning the yarn is a real problem.  Alignment of the needles is a problem.  Even if they are in the proper position Before the carriage is moved across the yarn, the needles move out of place, the carriage jams and then much time and hand work has to be done to complete the row.  Then, the tension on the stitches is too tight across the row and the next row is difficult to knit. The machine makes a very distressing noise as the it attempts to knit.  It is NOT a relaxing process.  It is not an easy process.  Hand knitting is easier.  I have almost finished ONE project which will cost me the price of the entire machine.  A very expensive and not very exciting scarf.  I am going to put the entire thing away and either throw it out or give it to someone who has more patience than I have.  I wonder if the sliver reed machine is less frustrating and works better than this knitting machine?
Only buy this if you like cheaply made, ugly findings. Wish I could return it. Garbage.
It dries soft and bends with the clothes' takes about 24 hours to dry.
I ordered a size 2-3-4 so I could sew a size 2 shirt for my great grand son. But I received a size 4-5-6! So very disappointed!!! can't use this for several years.
Students love it. Wish they were a bit less expensive.
Too expensive, but worked well for art projects

This is a really great selection of brushes, everything you need to do some fine, detailed painting.

There is even a tiny liner that I think will be great on a mosaic painting I'm planning for the inside of a drawer.

The case is pretty nice, my only complaint is that I have several small zippered cases that are black, I just wish they came in other colors.

The only brush that's missing is a round flat one for stenciling.

I checked all the bristles and none were loose or falling out. So all in all this is a pretty great set.

This is a fantastic product that has been around forever. I use it to glue puzzles and I have found that one coat on each side of the puzzle is more than adequate. For an extra layer of hold or protection two layers of glue on each side will be far more than you need. This is the matte finish which I prefer,  but many other finishes are available from satin to glossy.

This isnt quality watercolor paper. Its decent but not good enough for those of us who layer. It peels badly when you layer it. If you dont plan on laying then this is pretty good. But stay away if you love or need to layer for your art
I saw it, but I wish it was all stainless steel because I live near a beach and the metals turn and tarnish before using them.

I had bought the same brand in 5 lbs so i was hoping the 10 lbs would work out.
It must have been stored improperly or is nearing its expiration maybe?
Compared to the 5 pounder, it didn’t melt well in the microwave, was clumpy, formed films on the surface, formed a lot of clumpy crap when pouring into soap molds, and ugh the smell is awful!!
I am so disappointed. This product ruined my project that i had set up with my transparent glycerine base for the two tone soaps. Very poor quality .
I will try my local soap supplier who sells for a similar price, and at well controlled storage conditions.

So disappointed in the lack of quality. Some of the pencils had damage, as well as one of the numbers 'fell off' so you could only tell the type by process of elimination. Returning for something worth it.

I have done many diamond paintings from different companies, and the quality on this wasn't good. The cut around the picture was really sloppy and uneven. There was no clear plastic covering the picture, it was solid white with the company logo on it. You pull up the white sheet to view the painting and you had to work to press down the adhesive which was not attached evenly on the painting, and stuck to the backside of the white sheet. The adhesive was so sticky that it created ripples and wrinkles on the painting that in many spots did not ever even out. I decided to use another clear sheet leftover from a different dots painting I had completed. Every time I pulled that up, the adhesive was so strong it was ridiculous and took two hands to do it. There was a lot of symbols, not easy to read without a lightbox. Even though I did manage to finish it, you can still see some ofthe adhesive wrinkles behind the dots because the gaps are a little larger and back color doesn't match dot colors.

I recently bought some watercolor paints and the brush that came with them was not very good. Some have blamed the paints for not being good but I discovered it was actually the quality of the brush. This set of brushes produce much more professional results. I love them. They have long handles and are easy to use.

The case these come in is very well made and will keep your brushes safe for years to come. I have only tried these with watercolors as it is just easier to wash the brushes off with water. But you could use these for oil painting too.

Every imaginable brush shape is here with fifteen brushes to choose from. I'm very happy with them and generally use lots of the smaller brushes for small artwork. I may get adventurous at some point and start using the bigger brushes.

If you know an artist this would be a great present. For yourself? You will fall in love with them.

Highly Recommended.

~The Rebecca Review

I received one free set of paintbrushes for review purposes. This review is my honest opinion.
Some of the tools are good quality, but a few are very poor quality. I actually returned due to this. The case was nice, though. A bit expensive for the quality.

Used shea butter melt and pour soap.
Pouring in is easy, its the unmolding part that was a little bit challenging. Pulled the edges first then peeled down like a banana. Then jiggled it out while pushing on the bottom of the column. Overall, detailed final prduct
, i am pleased with the result

The brush does not say any number, the '3' is completely worn off? Or missing. The bristles were not to a point, some strays. What's even worse is the brush retails at any art store for half the price I paid here. It may be legitimately Winsor, but I think it was the bottom of the barrel or used.

Right out of the box I pulled the zipper to open the case and the zipper broke.  OK, it still has another zipper.  The next problem is that the teeth of the zipper easily separate and that is with nothing in the case.  The case is nice, but it has the cheapest zipper on the planet.  I returned it immediately.
Noice
Kemp, which is nice but much thinner than I expected. Disappointed.

There are several sweaters in this book that I would like to make. A couple of the cardigans are on my list. However, I have not yet made any of the designs from this book. The instructions seem to be clear and accurate, and there is a good variety of garments in this book. Nice designs.
they are bigger than I expected- they don't clip on to the necklace I wanted to use them with.  It's got a dainty chain and these are pretty big.
Exactly what you want if you need padding underneath desk things. The adhesive is very strong, good luck trying to get it off.

Monstera cutter needs full redesign. The cutters aren’t that sharp either.

I’m picking up watercolor for the first time in decades and loving this little starter set. I wasn’t sure how I’d like the water brush, but turns out I love it. I started with a standard brush first, then got brave, grabbed the water brush and was pleasantly surprised at the amount of control I had with it. Thanks for a great product that will serve me well as I begin my journey back to traditional watercolor and away from doing everything on computer. The feel is so great putting hand to paper again. I highly recommend this watercolor set. The photo shows results from the water brush. Boy, am I rusty, lol. But I’ll get back there again. Blessings to you!
ok
Nice , small and neat , fit my small studs perfectly . And it's good to have some spares on hand since I tend to loose them ones in a while .
Too cheap to be used in a press, to weak to work properly even if you manage to not smash them up when you're applying them.

Was looking forward to getting these.  Expected paper quality to be as good as the Arteza 5-1/2 x 3-1/2” books I bought last month. Both say “premium”, acid free, cold press.  The small ones are only 110 lb and I had none of the problems I have with these that say 140 lb.  They buckle with very light wash, the paper pilled and came apart in chunks just doing a simple 2 color gradient.  It was destroyed when I tried to lift a little light color that bled into a white area. I say ‘white’ but this paper is not white.  Ivory at best. Pics show all these issues. Won’t even use for practice, just makes it more difficult and no fun. Very disappointed Arteza!
This is merely a tablet of blank pages with a few instructions at the beginning. Returned it.

Its a good brush but it loses more hair than I do. When I went to grab a loose hair I ended up getting a lot. I didn't even grab it harshly. I dont think the hair is tightly wound enough. It does hold a sh*tload of eater and feels great. Really large brush which i dont mind.
I have never seen tissue paper so thin... Unless using for a very small package (4x6) and then plan on using many sheets!!! Very disappointed!

Melt and pour soap Shea butter soap base.
I used a pipette to pipe in the green leaves, then poured white into the roses, piped in more green, then poured in scented blue. (waited between each application, and used alcohol spritz to make sure the layers adhere)
I am satisfied with the results, as the roses are very detailed.
Unfortunately, some of the leaves like to break off.
Use it before you lose it!

This fringe is acceptable as is, but I do think more sequins would have bumped it up in my opinion.

If you were planning a Flapper outfit using this fringe, you will need so so much of it, because when spread out to a single layer the fringe is sorta sparse, like a head covered with too few stragglers.

I think this amount is appropriate for a throw pillow or a table runner for a sexy Valentines meal.

The price seems okay and since the fringe is so long, you could trim it if you really desired.

Large set with lots of nice colors. I had only a few gel pens previously, this is a step up for me. There were only one or two that didn’t work, and color names that didn’t match the actual colors. The glitter, pastel, and metallic ones are especially fun! This set will be good for coloring books and other paper crafts.
The paper is very nice, but it didn't include the envelopes.

This is a beautiful applique, not to big and not to little. Looks just like the picture. However, it's listed as "lavender pink flowers". They are purple, lavender and green leaves. I didn't want any pink and to me the picture doesn't show any pink. I am going to use this on a pillow case hem.

This stuff is pretty cool.  I like to copy old black and white photos and attach the little faces to this filmstrip.  Think of group photos of school kids, the expressions are great!  It is necessary to glue this down with glossy accents or diamond glaze, or use brads to fasten it down.  You get a nice long roll of this, it is just over 1/2" wide.
Overall it’s a good product. I got them for my 13 year old. We had a few that couldn’t get the past off and had to use a knife to but after that everything seemed to be working as should.

Not at all as described! Dark, dull and  plastic-like beads (like the ones you see in the kid’s section at s craft store. To even say that they are AAA quality would be laughable, but the joke is on me.
These watercolor brushes do not blend That well with water. Use a light touch when coloring to blend with water later.

The glitter is most noticeable in the black sheets of paper. Which was a bit of a downer, not many kids want to use black paper for their craft projects, luckily, my son was going through a Star Wars stage and he used all the black and red sheets first.

But the other sheets have a pretty glitteryness to them and the crafts we used them for, chopstick dragons, pinwheels and a paper mosaic benefited from the slight bit of glitter in the paper.

But, like I said, these aren't super glittery, they are pieces of construction paper with a small amount of glitter thrown in. So if you were hoping for a sheet of glitter, you'd be better off buying something from a specialty papers store.

This kit is fun to use to make three beautiful bracelets. The string is elastic but easy to work with. I had no problem inserting the string into the beads.

What I did notice is that I was dropping a few of the smaller gold items on the floor due to them being rather delicate to handle. To prevent this I put a washcloth on the table and that seemed to help so the items didn't bounce when I dropped them! You don't need any jewelry making tools but remember to tie double knots in the string at each end of the bracelet.

What is convenient about this kit is that there are good instructions that are easy-to-follow and the beads for each bracelet are in their own plastic bag. So no sorting is needed.

I enjoy making jewelry and these bracelets are really pretty. I would however advise people to secure the knots on these bracelets in some way. I don't know what the durability is going to be if kids wear these.

~The Rebecca Review

I purchased the 24 Original Jar Set. The photo shows a little more vibrancy in color than in person, (camera photo). After reading another user had successfully used on polymer clay, I decided to try it and made button testers on plain white Sculpey III. I applied each powder to raw clay and baked at 275° for 30 minutes. When you open the door the fumes were not visible but potent, burns your eyes and heavy smell of burnt crayons, so I had to open windows . So I came back to read, the Rolio seller said they are not to be baked past 248°F. Well that explains the fumes. Should post that in the description...
Most were not that fine, a few had too much glitter, a few were flat, some colors are way to close in tone, almost identical. I tried mixing a couple into the clay, but it just did not have enough pigment or smooth enough to notice any color. I would use these strictly on already baked clay, perhaps in resin, nothing that needs baking unless you try maybe Fimo Polymer Clay (temp is 230°F). The front, non-watercolor paper page is intact, but the watercolor paper pages each come loose from the glued edge as you turn to the next page no matter how carefully you do it. I like it. Okay, the big plus is it only takes a bobbin and that threads into the tension then into the needle. You can't use a spool - won't fit on the bobbin peg. Get this - there is NO way to thread the bobbin itself, on it. You have to have a real machine to thread the bobbins for you.

The good news is you never have to put in a bobbin underneath the foot. It only uses the bobbin on top to feeding the thread to the needle. All that grief I kept getting from my expensive Singer that I finally threw out because I was sick of fighting with it, is gone. I don't have to fight with the jammed bobbin under the foot because that doesn't exist on this. I can just quickly thread and sew in a straight line.

- It doesn't go backwards. you have to stop it- put the needle down and turn the material around to get it to go in the opposite direction. You need to do this to start and stop or the material will pull apart a few inches after where you started and finished.
- to insert fabric you have to unplug it from the battery(or you'll bump the on switch and it will sew), then you manually pick up the metal part under the foot and slide fabric in and out.
- It is consistently tight when you stop like it's jammed - you can't pull your fabric out and have trailing thread like you do on real machines. You have to unplug it from the battery, lift up metal piece under the foot and pull the material hard enough to free it and take your fabric out. That's everytime I stop. But it comes out, the needle stays threaded and I can start again with no fuss on the next thing I want to quickly sew.
- it takes 4 double A batteries and I see no wall power for it. Batteries are expensive.

This is not for any real sewing. It's hand held. It's only for quick hems. For what it is, I like it and will use it.

Update: I no longer use this and I suggest you don't either. for the same price you can get a Lil Sew and Sew and nearly have an actual machine. I'm happy with my $17 Sew and Sew but I'm going for a Brother XR3774 now because I want a stable machine real machine.

I've now painted all of the sheets of this watercolor pad, some on both sides. The paper itself is not super high quality; water tends to puddle, it buckles some even when glued to the pad, and masking fluid can't be removed without tearing the surface. It's also impossible to get all the little glue bits neatly off the edges of the piece when it's removed from the block. Even by inexpensive paper standards, it's only okay: the Canson watercolor pad is much sturdier.

That said, the shape is fun, it was cheap, and I had fun using it to essentially play and doodle with pieces I knew I wasn't going to care too much about. I bought it because I wasn't sure I would ever use round paper, and I definitely do reach for it. It's not like it's unusable--the absorbency is okay and it doesn't buckle badly. I just wouldn't plan to use it for anything you're hoping to treasure, or anything too technically demanding.

I bought a package of wooden beads several years ago and about a 1/4 of them were damaged, usually the hole in the middle didn't go all the way through so I had to carve it out myself to make them usable, but almost all of these are perfect and, as a Bonus, they are all primed with a layer of white paint.

The paint is thin, it looks like the old fashioned milk paint type, so if you are into that shabby chic thing, you could leave these undecorated. But for me, it just means I can use less paint on each ball, yay!

So I am painting these and using some pipe cleaners and a small block of florist foam to hold them while they dry. I am using cheap acrylic paint, the kind that costs under a dollar per bottle and comes in like a million shades.

The balls come with two wodden tags that say Home Sweet Home, so cheesy, I will probably paint over these and use them as garden tags if I get my act together and plant something this pring.

There are several bundles of twine that could be used to make a garland, if you have trouble threading the balls, tie a piece of twine onto a wire twisty tie (like the kind that come with produce bags) or with a toothpick.

Since you can use less paint that means you have a shorter dry time, I don't know if everyone else likes instant craft results like me, but this has turned into a 2hour craft, yahoo!

The balls, tags and twine arrive in a decent sized ziploc, I am storing the extras in this, no reason for them to get lost in the bottom of my sewing bag.

All in all, these are a great crafting item.

I deleted my previous review on this. Mine works but had tension problems out of the box. Still I bought another sewing machine and didn't take it out of the box for months because I kept using this. I like the size! I don't like the tension needing constant adjusting.

- it didn't have a light - I taped a dollar store led light on it.
- it doesn't have reverse - you have to stop the machine, put the needle down and turn the fabric around. (Always start sewing and afer 1/2 inch go in reverse then after 1/2 inch go forward again to lock you stitches at beginning and end of your sewing.)

However, it works for me and I've sewn a lot on it. I recently bought a Brother XR3774 for $118 that sews perfectly and has no tension problems. But it's a boat. The Lil Sew and Sew is tiny and easier to grab. I finally pulled out the Mint Janome because it's smaller than the Brother and tried it - I'll use it since it's smaller. But I really like the size of this sew and sew which is the much smaller and lighter of the 3.

Crazy as it seems, I've considered spinning the roulette wheel for $15 bucks on this and buying another and hopping for no tension problems - because this is so utterly light and easy to use. Update: I'm not going back to it. My mint Janome is my main machine because of size and my Brother is hanging around with black thread in it to use instead of re-threading the Janome when I need black. If you don't have money, fine buy this cheapy but get ready to learn to adjust tension every foot you sew. It has no light and no reverse. The foot pedal is so small I used it as a hand pedal and placed it next to the machine. I did sew a whole lot with this and I adored it until I got my far better machines that require no tension adjusting, have reverse etc. If you can, spend the extra - ear mark your next gob of cash - pay check etc. to buy a decent machine and pick something that gave 5 stars in 90 percent of the reviews. This one is spinning a roulette wheel and doesn't work for half of reviewers.

When I was on the design team of a rubber stamp/scrapbooking store, this is the kind of product that they would hand me, and ask that I make samples and show customers how to use the item.  At first appearance, it seems that there are gears that turn and somehow interlock.  This is not the case.  The gears are positioning devices to place on paper in order to stamp in a circle or oval.  The first concern, however, is the packaging.  As others have mentioned, much of the inspriation and instruction is on the fold out package. The plastic bubbles that enclose the gears and stamps are not easy to remove without destroying the package.  I ended up cutting the plastic bubbles off with an exacto knife, keeping the packaging intact.  I then had to use another container to store the stamps and gears in.  Back to the product.  I would recommend that a couple of small register marks are made in pencil at the arrows on the gears in case they slip a little. They do have a non-skid type of bottom, but if they move even slightly, it can cause problems with lining up the design. I did find that using the same stamp all the way around was ok, but a little boring. The stamps are largely floral, and somewhat Victorian.  It is necessary to ink the stamp between impressions if a consistent color is desired.  I used them with chalk inks in various pastels. It is possible to vary the design by using alternating stamps. By way of explanation, the cling stamps are placed on a holder, and there is a point on either end of that holder which fits into the cogs.  According to the instructions, all can be used interchangeably, and that is another reason to make a small register mark, in case one wants to position another element. All in all, an interesting product, all of the stamps can be used independently of this system, and it is possible to use the positioning cogs for partial symmetry, as desired by the consumer.  It seems to be well made, the stamps cling well, and the instructions are pretty good, all parts are easy to clean. Intriguing.

But these brushes are so nice and I have a lot of stenciling to do, so I figured I really needed them They do work very much like 'spouncers' except that you can get a lot more interesting texture with these brushes, which is great for the ravens I have been stenciling, it gives them a wilder look.

Something else to know is that these brushes are a lot easier to clean. Sponges take a lot of effort to clean and they don't last very long if you are planning on cleaning them after each color coat.

If you have never made soaps before this soap base is a good base to start with. I cut the bar in half and then cut the half into two more places to make three small pieces of soap. Then I melted them in a double boiler using a spoon to stir everything around and around until it all melted. Then I added in rose essential oil and two drops red food coloring. Once everything was all mixed up I poured the melted soap base into flower soap molds. One half of the soap base makes enough for one Something Fabulous soap mold. So I made six flower soaps and I poured the rest into a plastic bag which I set in a stainless bowl. So half this bar made seven soaps. Getting the soaps out of the molds is the hard part. I'm going to look for silicone molds in the future. But using the soaps was a pure joy. The lather is super foamy and the feeling is that your skin is left with a protective layer of moisture. I'll definitely buy this soap base again. I loved the whole experience of making my own soaps and this really is the easy way to go. I can see myself making more soaps as gifts for Christmas. You can color this any way you want to and put whatever essential oils you like in it. A great experience!

~The Rebecca Review

Well, like everything these days you're choices for buying something is from China or China. The right side does not fit as tightly on the sleeved arms as the driver's side. This causes the right side to slowly slide out from wind pressure and road vibration. I'm positive these are all made in the same or same two or three factories and are simply resold by every Tom Dick and Harry (would that be Lee, Wang and Wong over there?) under 50 million different company names. The plastic does not even seem to be as solid as the last pair and I suspect the Arizona sun will destroy them in a few months both with sun fade and physical degradation of the plastic itself. I forgot to mention the wiring harness is so flipping short it's nearly impossible to follow the factory wiring channel to the switch pack. Another example of being cheap.

Hello ,
Over the past several months I had the brakes and rotors replaced on two cars both front and back using your products. I was extremely pleased with the product and decided to have the brakes on my 2000 CLK replaced as well. So I took the liberty to order the front pads and rotors from Amazon as I did in the past.
To my surprise and disappointment when the mechanic called me after I delivered the package to him he explained the package the pads came in and the rotors had been previously opened. He noted both pads and rotors had grease on them. He was not overly worried however he did explain sanding down the pads and using solvent to clean the rotors would solve the problem.
Since he already had the car prepared for the installation I went along with his suggestions and he completed the work. The car has been returned to me and the equipment works as expected without any known flaws.
The delivery is not what I expected and I am disappointed that I would receive a product that has either already been used and placed back in the package or some other explanation I cannot account for.
After contacting the manufacture they assured me the package left their warehouse in new condition. They also believe if any discrepancies exist the most likely source may have been a return to Amazon by another customer and the package thus being shipped to me.
Regards
James Radle

First off I recommend using a small flat head screwdriver with the install. let’s start off by taking the factory brake pad off, just grab a corner and pull, poof it’s off, grab your new brake pedal pad, put the top of the rubber gasket over the top of the pedal and pull down. Once the top is in the gasket take your screwdriver and go from just under the top right corner  and guide the gasket alone the right side of the pedal, now repeat on the left side, then finally do the bottom, run your fingers along the entire perimeter of the back of the pedal to ensure it is completely seated. Last thing you want is the pedal cover to come off in a hard breaking situation. If yes move on to the gas pedal if no use the screwdriver and repeat the side or bottom that isn’t seated. GAS PEDAL: DO NOT REMOVE THE FACTORY PEDAL COVER ON THIS SIDE. I started with the top right corner, then moved down the right side “since there’s not really room for the screwdriver trick on that side” get the entire top right corner/right side under the gasket. Then move to the bottom, use screwdriver as needed. Then pop the gasket over the bottom left side and move upwards towards the last corner. Again run your fingers along the gasket and make sure it’s properly seated around the gas pedal, if not use your screwdriver to correct it. THIS ALL SOUNDS RELATIVELY SIMPLE BUT IT DID TAKE ME A LITTLE WHILE TO FIGURE IT OUT AND TO PROPERLY SEAT THE GASKET

I'm crazy about frogs, and my home and autos are decked out in frog-themed accessories and collectibles. I purchased these in 2013 so I've had ample time to test these out, and this is my assessment after significant use:

Pros

*Cute tree frog designs compliments my rearview mirror charm and matches the customized tree frog license plate border I own
*I'm self-employed working from home, and other than bi-annual road trips, the mats haven't been used nearly as often as they'd be by the average commuter in my area (hence the reason they still look pretty decent)
*Semi-decent quality and good price at time of purchase justified the splurge, but the asking price now is exorbitant
*Frogs are still nearly the same color as when I purchased them without fading, but this is only due to keeping my car in a garage from the sun's rays

Cons

*Flimsy, cheaply made mats minutely offset by the cuteness of the frog designs
*Were I a frequent commuter, these mats certainly would have shredded and needed replacing within a short timeframe
*Most annoying feature is the mats never stay in place, because the bottoms are cheaply made and the texture intended to anchor them don't adhere properly to my auto carpet
*Although I love frogs, these aren't an overall savvy consumer splurge when most people commute at least five days on an average work week

The frog mats are cute, but this feature doesn't negate unreasonable pricing, semi-decent quality, and overall cheapness. I noticed some people loved the mats, and it's easy to see why when viewing photos, but as a professional reviewer with the best interest of fellow consumers, I simply don't recommend them.

Install was easy to follow using the included instructions. Install on a 2019 Honda Accord (Hybrid) involves removing the mudflaps (if equipped, not part of instructions), the bumper cover, and then the rear impact bar. Installation is reverse of removal, with the hitch installed between the impact bar and vehicle. The bumper cover did require slight trimming, following the dimensions in the instructions it worked perfectly.

The hitch itself is very straightforward, a few pieces of metal, some tubing, etc, all welded together as expected. Unfortunately, the product quality is lacking in the actual finishing of the product. It appears to have a coating of powdercoat or similar paint over raw metal. The raw metal likely was not cleaned, etched, or otherwise processed, as the paint was chipped and had flaked off in a few spots. I do not expect this to remain rust free for more than 6mo. I expect a majority of the product to show significant rust after 2-3 years, and we will probably just replace the entire thing in 3-4 years.

AS Amazon apparently can't keep Upol Raptor Liner in stock, I tried this product. This is really a rubberized undercoating and not hard, bed lining product as is claimed. I use the aerosol cans of "truck bed coating" to refinish faded plastic trim parts. Surface are was properly prepped by using de-greaser, washing with soap and water, drying, scuffed with a scotch brite pad (shouldnt be needed as it's textured plastic) and I used Dupli-color plastic adhesion promoter.

The results are not even close to a hard bed liner. Applied on a hot low humidity day (10% or less in the desert) and left to cure for several days and the finish is soft and rubbery. I can indent it with a finger nail. The final stop coat left a granular texture that rubs off into fine particles. This was applied following the prep and application directions of several light coats 10 minutes or less part.

I am thoroughly dissatisfied with this product, do not recommend using it as anything other than rubberized undercoating.

There are a lot of quick-car-wax products on the market. Most of them are spray bottles of wax product that you spray on your car, then wipe/buff off with your own towels. This product has the towel built in so you don't have to provide your own and wash it afterwards.  None of these quick-wax product work as well as a good paste wax (don't look as good or last as long), but they do apply much more quickly. This Armor All quick-wax gives us decent results even without any buffing afterwards (always the most time consuming part of using a paste car wax). The instructions say you should buff with a separate towel on dark colored cars or if streaking is noticeable and ugly. If you're too lazy to wax your car the old fashioned way but still want it to look reasonably nice, this quick wax is a good alternative.

so after doing a total rebuild on this F150 spending $1750 on dozens of new parts as well as sending the heads out for a rebuilding. Once I got everything completely back together (2weeks) I kept having a misfire and the engine would shake under 1500 RPM. I started replacing various parts trying to solve the problem but nothing worked. I bought an engine scanner and it kept saying misfire 3 and 4. nothing i did fixed it. switching out plugs and coils (bought new on amazon too by the way) I finally broke down after 3 weeks never finding this mysterious ghost/demon issue so I took it to a shop. they looked at it for 2 days and then told me that all the fuel injectors are incorrect and not working for this truck. They started replacing the fuel injectors with ones that they got from the Ford dealership which obviously cost a lot more but they work. Lesson learned cheap ain’t good and good ain’t cheap. I will thank Amazon for the full refund. Have a blessed day folks enjoy your rebuild

So what's my problem? Although I've tried to be very conscientious about screwing the fittings onto the hose, I've had several catastrophic failures! In the immortal words of Forrest Gump, that's all I'm going to say about that right now. Even though the ends of the fittings rotate where they connect to the outlets, the hose works itself loose somehow, requiring more vigilance than I like to engage in with poop containing pipes.

Also, after a couple years of use the fittings themselves began to leak. I understand that you can get new O-rings for them to remedy this, but I decided to let this go and try a new system instead. I selected the "Dominator" brand, based solely on the aspect of the fittings being permanently connected to the hose. Not only is this true but if they ever decided to come loose, which doesn't look likely, you could clearly see where that was happening and not be 'surprised'. It has the same rigid construction as the rhino flex hose, it appears just as heavy duty. I will miss having the clear elbow, but of course the small fitting of that sort could be added.

To not kill the extra vitamins from your breast milk maybe? Never used the timer feature/ used it on accident when I first got the item and wondered why it took 15 minutes to heat my milk up!

Minus: You need 2 free hands for it to work. You have to hold the button down for 3 seconds to turn it on requiring you to hold the back of the machine. Most busy moms are holding baby so this machine does NOT allow that.

There are too many buttons to press which may be good or bad as you can set it to whatever you like. You choose the bottle size, 3 oz, 5 oz, 8 oz, etc. There are 4 bottle sizes you can choose from causing more or less heating.

You then have to choose what temperature your milk is at, room temp, fridge temp or frozen. Why can't it default to one of them?! Making it NOT user friendly. You have to set this ALL the time.

This is a great idea but very poor design. The headrest does a good job in allowing kids to fall asleep in the car without falling over or banging their heads on the car door window. However, there is an extremely high chance of the product failing at the elbow joint where the wings attach to the back bracket. The elbow is held together with nothing more than friction of a metal pin in a hollow molded plastic cylinder. After some use, the metal pin can either lose friction with the mold and keep sliding out or the plastic cylinder can break off all together. I have used 2 of these over time and both failed for the same reason. I am not an expert, but for $50, this is a poorly made especially at the point that takes the most stress when in use.

I love the look, style and size of this storage bin. But calling it a "storage bin" is a misnomer.
