Want a quick, easy to unpackage and eat treat that offers some decent nutritional value? Most snacks don’t fit the bill, but one that does is Fiber One Streusel Bar Strawberry flavor.

I like a quick snack and I like that these bars have more nutrition than others. They are made with real fruit, healthy whole grains, and a good dose of fiber. The added fiber is completely lacking in other snacks and does help set this snack food above others.

As for the taste, well, these bars are decent although not quite the burst of natural flavor I was hoping for. The ingredients list concentrated strawberry puree and it’s nice that they use this real ingredient. But I wish there was more of this natural flavor and I’m a little disappointed that the product contains artificial colors and other additives that contribute to the less-than-stellar flavor. The taste is, therefore, not much different than that of other strawberry flavored cereal bars and, while certainly not bad, it isn’t as good as I had hoped.

Still, when I compare the overall product to other, similar snack foods, I have to say that these are a notch better than most and the reason is the fiber and whole grains. Could these be made even better? Of course they could, but they easily measure up to or exceed other snack foods and they have enough good qualities to earn my recommendation.

Everyone should drink more water, or so we are told. The problem is that water is plain and boring. It needs some flavor, and many companies have responded to this demand. O.N.E. Coconut water with splash of pink guava is one such product. It contains coconut water along with guava juice for a unique taste.

I like the taste of coconut but I am only so- so on the flavor of guava. Still, I decided this drink was worth a try because as long as it emphasized the coconut, it would be fine. Unfortunately, this isn't the case. O.N.E Coconut Water with a splash of guava is misleading in its naming, because you taste more guava than anything. Even then, the taste is rather unexciting. Even lovers of the flavor of guava will likely find this product a letdown.

Nutritionally, O.N.E. Coconut water is very good, with low calories and a large dose of vitamin C and electrolytes in each serving, and more potassium than a banana. The sugar content is a little higher than some would prefer, but it isn't too bad and, unlike other sweet drinks, you get a good deal of nutritional trade- off for the sugar. Each time I take a sip and feel dissatisfied, I keep reminding myself of the nutritional benefits.

Along with the bland flavor profile, there is one other problem I have with this drink, and that is the packaging. It is sold in a small foil box, which is ordinarily not a problem, but there is no included straw. There is an opening at the top that must be pushed- in to open, but the manufacturer doesn't include a straw. Thus, you have to use a fork or some other device to forge an opening. Then, you must carve out just the right shape for drinking from the box OR have another glass handy in which to pour the liquid.

I wanted to like this beverage but when all factors are considered, I must rate O.N.E. Coconut Water with a splash of guava as a below average product. The coconut flavor is masked by the guava, the taste is unexciting, and the packaging is flawed. Yes, you get a good deal of nutrition so the product isn't a complete bomb, but there are other, better tasting products on the market with equally good nutrition, better taste, and more sensible packaging.

Lipton's Orange Blossom Hibiscus is another one of Lipton's pyramid herbal teas- products with a pyramid- shaped tea bag and added herbs for a unique taste. This tea product contains herbs and real pieces of orange for a supposedly authentic flavor.

I like the pyramid teas from Lipton in general, but I have yet to find one that really grabs my attention and this product is yet another that seems okay overall, but that doesn't live up to its potential. The taste is rather mild overall and while you can certainly tell it is an herbal and orange flavored tea, there isn't much to the overall taste to make it memorable.

Besides the mildness of the orange flavor, the other taste that is lacking is, well, the other one in the title: hibiscus. There isn't much in the way of hibiscus taste and the flavor seems most dominated by the taste of herbs, however mild they may be.

I will continue to enjoy tea and sample as many new products as I can, but I don't know if I will try this Lipton product again. It needs a boost in the taste department to make it a viable product, but I admit that it does have just barely enough good qualities to make it worthy of trying. Don't get your hopes up too high and you just might like this orange and herbal flavored product.

This is a decent soap. It smells quite nice and has good lather. The main reason that I'm rating it one star is that there is false advertising with this item- it is made from a melt and pour soap base, NOT cold process.

I was initially suspicious when I saw that the bar is transparent rather than opaque. Additionally, the loofah was entirely encased in soap, as if it was sliced into discs and put into a mold that the soap was then individually poured onto. There shouldn't have been soap that thick on either side if the soap was cured with the loofah in the center of the mold and then sliced.

I then checked out the ingredients and, instead of listing the oils used followed by lye, it says "glycerin soap base".

When soap is made by cold process, the oils are mixed together and saponification happens when lye is mixed into the oils. It then needs to cure in a mold for a number of months to create a solid bar that lasts for a long time.

This is also how soap base is made, but glycerin in added in as well. This base is then sold to the soap maker, who uses it to create melt and pour soap. This soap is easier to make and can be prettier, but isn't as hard and doesn't last as long as true cold process.

There isn't anything wrong with selling melt and pour soap, but it is misleading to label this as cold processed and market it as "made the old fashioned way". Additionally, while almost $8 would be a fair price for a bar of true cold processed soap, this is far too high for melt and pour.

Do not support this seller by purchasing this soap until they are upfront about the product that they're selling and lower the price to reflect its quality.

Tylenol Sinus Congestion and Pain Non- Drowsy Rapid Release Gels are one of dozens of over the counter health aids from Tylenol, a company with products that seem to work wonders for some people while having almost no effect on others. This particular product falls into the latter category for me, with barely noticeable changes in sinus congestion, even after taking a couple of tablets.

On the good side, these tablets are easy to take thanks to the gelcaps that, while a little larger than average, slide down easily and effortlessly. The manufacturer recommends taking one or two of these caplets every four hours and it claims that this product can provide temporary relief from headache, sinus congestion, sinus pressure, nasal congestion, and minor body aches and pains. Based on my experience, the relief is only slight. This product is so- so at relieving pain, and it deserves at least an average rating in this area, but the main reason I take a medicine like this is to get relief from sinus congestion and pressure. This Tylenol product does almost nothing. I expect at least a little loosening of the congestion, but there is practically no noticeable difference at all.

A large part of the reason this Tylenol product is ineffective at its intended purpose is that they are made using phenylephrine. This is a newer decongestant substance and while it might work well for some people, it has almost no effect on my sinus problems. However, I have noticed that other sinus relief products, containing the same ingredient, work a little better. So, the problem with Tylenol Sinus Congestion Rapid Release Gels must have something to do with the other ingredients, even though the phenylephrine is the main issue.

Sinus problems are irritating, but Tylenol Sinus Congestion and Pain Non- Drowsy Rapid Release Gels do little to eliminate the problem. They perform at an acceptable level in the pain relief category, but their inability to reduce sinus congestion is inexcusable and when you add to that the fact that a dose is good for only four hours, you have a product that cannot be recommended.

The photo shown, the product description, "Thick-It 2" is what you believe you're getting. So when you get what is labeled as "THICK IT ORIGINAL" you'll think you're getting the regular Thick-It. It's not, if it is in the blue lidded with white and blue labeled container. Somehow, dropping the "2" and adding the word "Original" above smaller, less noticeable lettering that says "Concentrate" seemed like a good idea to the manufacturers. So identical almost to the actual "Original Thick-It", except for the actual "Original Thick-It" is in a yellow lidded container with a white and blue label that also says "Original".

If you're like me and have only ever used Thick-It2, never have you ever bought the actual "Original Thick-It" (less concentrated), therefore had no idea that the sole difference now would be in the dropping of the number "2" and the color coding of the lid, you might get your package and think you got the Original Original. Yep... it's THAT confusing. I had to call the manufacturer. The employee who answered the phone got so confused they had to put me on hold to clarify with someone else. I'm not exaggerating.

All of the confusion aside, I also asked (when I phoned the manufacturer) if the formula had changed. They responded with a VERY QUICK, VERY ABSOLUTE "No". Couldn't recall which formula came in which color lid, but knew resolutely that the formula hadn't changed. Mmkay. All I know is I could thicken my kid's 2 cups into pudding consistency with 2-3 tablespoons of Thick-It2 when I last bought it several years ago and now it takes 2/3 - 3/4 cup of the supposedly unchanged product. (His swallow issues are solely with liquids [and are both physical and psychological]; he's stayed fairly hydrated for several years but he's had a flare up [of the psychological aspect] recently and I've needed to start adding über thickened fluids back into his diet to keep him hydrated.)

My star rating doesn't deduct anything for the confusing labeling issues. I just detailed all of that for any other consumer out there who, like me, has never bought the Original Original and might not realize they're getting the same product as they ordered. My star rating is because, despite the manufacturer's claim they haven't changed the formula for the Concentrated Thick-It2, it is not as thickening as it was 10 years ago. BUT it is still better than the other brand that some hospitals use when you need to thicken water/fluids into pudding consistency.

I have light beige carpet.  Some idiot (I mean me) got 4 puppies over 3 months; as soon as 1 puppy was housebroken, I got a new puppy and the first one would have to be housebroken all over again. I tried EVERYTHING in my carpet cleaner (it's a Kent, which is like a Rug Doctor) and nothing worked so I decided "That's it!  I'm tearing it out!" - BUT before I tore it out, I decided to experiment.  My experiment was to see what 3 cups Clorox bleach to 3 gallons of water would do to my carpet:  If it ruined it, well, I was ripping it out anyway.  Low 'n behold, it worked!  My carpet looked great AND it wasn't splotchy or overly bleached.  Fast forward and I have a litter of 4 puppies and they had 3 accidents on my landing.  These pups are less than 3# each, so the wee stains were less than 3 inches each.  Instead of toxic cleaning the carpet again, I got the BISSELL OXYPRO PET and followed the directions to the inth degree.  Now, I have 3 huge (8-10" each) dark spots on the carpet.  I used 2 bottles of the Bissell over time, thinking I wasn't using enough to actually soak the stains (that's how the spots got so big).  Each time, I used the Bissell after bedtime and then placed a box fan on the area overnight so that it would be completely dry before foot-traffic hit it in the morning.  Fortunately, I found this stuff called GET SERIOUS and it got the Bissell stains out.  *****I should note that I got my Bissell as an 'included' when I purchased my Bissell Trilogy Pet vacuum (which I LOVE!).

I actually own a previous model of this which is nowhere near as nice. I bought this one because the older model is stuck 3 states away, and I'm dealing with a minor foot issue.

Some things that stand out:

- the heating element. If this heats from cold water, I assume it would take forever. DO NOT expect it to do some miracle heating of cold or even cool water. Start with lukewarm water, warm enough that you can tolerate it but not "hot" if that makes sense. Then enable the heating element. I estimate it will take roughly 15-30 minutes to actually "heat" the water from this point, just to give you perspective. Plus your skin and vessels will thank you for the gradual heating.

- the vibration element. There's no real "massaging" here. It just vibrates. Good for the soles of your feet, yes. That's about all it'll really do for you. What irritated me is that the center separator has something in there that rattles a bit. Not a showstopper but it was annoying. (Yes, I removed the attachment piece.)

- The bubble element. I wasn't impressed. It's good to help the water heat up faster by introducing air into it, but you literally can't feel the bubbles at your feet with the vibration element enabled. Not nearly as dispersed or "awesome" as what the picture shows.

- Some of the nubs under foot feel higher than others. This is especially true near the pinky toe. A bit annoying, not a showstopper.

- I do like the separate compartment for storing the attachments.

- I also like the way the pour spout is designed. On the older model you could only go so high before water was spilling out the front. Here, it's small enough to pour out but not so large as to cause spilling.

- I did not use the rollers, I took them out.

- I use a tea tree soak and they dissolve nicely here. My feet are already looking and feeling much better after only a few days of sessions.

- There are four attachments, one a regular brush, which you can use to completely pedicure. I recommend following up with a foot lotion for the best results, or at least some cocoa butter lotion or coconut oil.

Hopefully whoever reads this review does their research on probiotics in general as I didn't do mine hence why I'm probably giving this such a poor review.  As you may already be aware, different brands have different strains of 'good bacteria', and cheaper brands especially can cause problems such as an upset stomach.  When I first started taking these at the minimum dose (only 1 a day, always with a meal) I would notice my appetite would go from relatively full to very hungry almost immediately, where as before my stomach would feel more empty over a much longer duration.  No big deal, I figured my body was trying to adjust.  Then around 6+/- days into taking these I started having a cramped stomach an hour or so after going out for a beer or two, possibly what could have triggered the stomach ache since I've heard some craft beers contain probiotics in the yeast (correct me if I'm wrong, I've also heard this is filtered out in the brewing process).  I did not take my probiotic the same day I drank and that night, I went to sleep hoping my stomach would feel better in the morning.  I got up around 1am and went to the bathroom, I could not go and wanted so bad to throw up but couldn't.  I was having cold sweats looking super flushed and almost passed out on the bathroom floor.  The stomach ache was so debilitating, it was VERY similar to the time my appendix ruptured, and I was afraid this time it could be something unrelated to the probiotics, like my spleen.  I didn't know what was going on at the time and stumbled into the other room to wake up my gf.  Slowly over the next 3 days I was able to go, only ate a bare minimum of 1 meal a day with water and a small cup of coffee which is very unusual for me as I always have a big appetite.  Finally I woke up today and things seem to be back to normal.  I read up on stomach aches and probiotic uses online and they say this is why it is so important to get the right brand (I heard Align at Costco is a good brand).  Several people who had this problem (who did not cite drinking) said these stomach pains lasted a week or more.  I find it hard to believe that just ~1L of beer could cause such a reaction but I guess it's possible this was due to the bacteria in the beer.  Anyway I am scared to take any more of these, so do your research especially if you drink.

Clean burning carbs are non-existent in today's protein powders and Meal Replacement Drinks. It's always maltodextrin, for the most part (which is a VERY high glycemic sugar/starch from corn--not good). What I do, is mix 2-3 scoops of Pure Whey or Raw Brown Rice Protein, along with 1-2 scoops of Yammit, and make it into a Meal Replacement Smoothie. Low-fat, calorie rich, great for replenishing after a workout, or replacing a meal. It's great.

The only thing I wish would change about this formulation would be having actual Yam powder, rather than the Nuggets. In a smoothie, the Nuggets don't dissolve very well, and tend to settle at the bottom. Not a MAJOR problem, but it's why I gave 3 stars instead of 4.

I bought this eye mask for my occasional headaches and daytime naps.

WHAT I LIKE:
- the cord is quite long - 72" or 6 feet which is perfect if you fall asleep, so that it gives you enough leeway tossing and turning and not getting entangled with the cord
- the cord attaches to the mask magnetically - in case you DO get entangled, it easily snaps off (but it is also a big CON... read below)
- the material seems to be of high quality and it is padded, which feels very soft to the touch

WHAT I DISLIKE:
- the mask comes only in a box, not a pouch. I do not like stuff like this to be in a box that could get smashed, waterlogged or otherwise damaged. A dedicated pouch with a string as a hanger would be much more practical
- the temperature switch (on the cable) is very touch sensitive - this might be a defect of my particular eye mask: when I set it on HIGH, and I just lightly move the cord by the magnetic attachment, it will turn off entirely. So in order to not disrupt the heating, I have to set it on MEDIUM or LOW for it to work (because everyone moves around a bit....)

AND MY BIGGEST COMPLAINT:
- the mask stays on 15 minutes at a time, which in my opinion is a very short time - it should be longer!
- THE DESIGN IS WRONG! No matter how I shift the mask, the cutout shape for the nose is too small meaning the mask is sitting either mostly on the top of my eyes and I can peek from under the mask and see daylight (and my nose is nicely cleared), or I push it over my eyes, and then the mask and its padding pushes onto my (rather large, I admit) nose and sinuses and makes wearing it VERY UNCOMFORTABLE.
I have to say, my head is truly somewhat larger and so is my nose, so this discomfort might be due to the proportions of my face features, I don't know....

So all in all, mostly because of the awkward design and therefore the uncomfortable fit, I hardly ever use this mask.
Wasted money for me, but this could probably be a good fitting mask for someone who has a more delicate face with a smaller nose, and whose cord isn't too touch-sensitive where it just shuts off....

I bought two of these, one for my kitchen faucet, and one for the wet bar upstairs that gets a lot less use.

Within two weeks, the green indicator light on the kitchen one stayed on all the time. A few more days went by, and it went off altogether. So I returned it, and Amazon replaced it n/c in November. Fast forward to the first week in February, and the same thing has now happened with the replacement. Of course, my window to return it expired on January 31st. :( It really stinks that there is no way to contact the manufacturer to ask why this keeps happening, or if there's anything I can do to fix it. I tried cleaning the unit with white vinegar. The flow is good, and the water quality seems to still be okay, but the indicator light is still off.

The upstairs one is still working just fine... although it gets used much less than the kitchen one. This item is currently unavailable, not surprisingly. I might just wait til the kitchen one goes kaput, and switch the upstairs one to the kitchen, and then try a different brand when these are both history (which will probably happen by April).

Beyond Tangy Tangerine's label claim says 1g sugar, 7g carbohydrates. The 6g of carbs must be from maltodextrin, which is in essence, a sugar as well, because it's off the charts on the glycemic index. When I take a serving of BTT, I get a sugar reaction, as if I just mowed down 5 chips a'hoy cookies. It's really uncanny. So, I honestly question the label claims. Maybe it's the maltodextrin that's causing a reaction, I'm not sure. But this product gives me bloat, like I said, worse than if I ate a gigantic pastry.

What I do know, is that while I'm sure this product has benefits, if you can digest it properly, for me, it causes awful digestive reactions. I have been taking Dr. Wallach's Ultimate Classic, which is basically the liquid form of this product for years with NO negative reactions... and that has 7.5g of sugar per serving! Also, I take Youngevity's "Rebound FX" product, which too has 7g of sugar per serving, and I digest that just fine. Something's funny with this product.

If I had to guess, it's the maltodextrin giving me a reaction. But, needless to say, I can't tolerate it. I will go back to Youngevity's Ultimate Classic, Colloidal Minerals, and I'll stay with Rebound FX. Beyond Tangy Tangerine is NOT for me. This was an extremely expensive failed experiment. I still have a container that's half full, that will unfortunately be going in the trash, or passed onto someone else.

I’ve used a handful of espresso machines over the past few years ranging from cheap to expensive. This machine falls in the middle of both taste and price.
This machine is easy to use once you find the settings and pods you like, which took me about a month of trial and error. It heats up quickly and is pretty portable. You can get pretty consistent results, which is also nice. But the taste of the espresso and the milk foam simply don’t compare to my manual Gaggia Classic. I can get much better results from an extra 5 minutes with my Gaggia and quality espresso beans. Most pods I tried tasted bitter or weak until I found the single origin Rosabaya de Colombia, which has a decent flavor. I also struggled with the size of the Nespresso shots and the volume of milk. Whereas it’s simple to make a 16oz latte with a few tablespoons of espresso beans and my Gaggia, I found that using two Nespresso pods is a must to make a drink that size with the Creatista. I do one Ristretto shot and one Espresso shot to get enough espresso volume for a 16oz drink, but that makes the cost per drink upwards of $2. I was actually fairly impressed with the quality of the milk from the Creatista, but my main issue was the temperature. Even on its hottest setting the milk only got to 155 degrees. I typically manually froth my milk with my Gaggia to 160-170 degrees, which is about as hot as possible before giving it a burnt taste.
Overall I did find this machine superior to a Saeco super automatic machine I’ve tried that was three times as expensive. The display is simpler and the milk was better with this Creatista. If you can get over the cost of the pods and absolutely need to save another 5 minutes for each drink, this is the way to go. For me, I’d rather put in the extra time for a superior taste and lower cost.

Talk about disappointment! These containers are TINY! Not at all what is advertised on here! I was expecting an assortment of sizes, with the largest being able to hold a box of cereal. Pfftt! It’ll hold maybe a bowl’s worth and that’s it! These are completely useless and are being returned. Oh, they’re incredibly overpriced as well. Save your money and look elsewhere.

This does everything I expected.  I wish I would have done a little more research before buying, to realize the reusable pads are a waste of money; just buy the single use ones (they contain the cleaner).  It takes a long time for it to clean a room, but it is very quiet, so it doesn't interfere.
I was so excited to see that simple human offers metal trash cans in colors other than stainless.
Do not recommend
They work as advertised.
This has been great! I love that it locks on to cans and stays on. Our last opener was so hard to turn! This is smooth and easy to use.
I love this rug! Looks great in my laundry room. Exactly what I wanted.

I have been enjoying this cup for the past week and like the bright coral color. For such a large cup it isn’t too hard to hold since it’s rather light and the bottom is tapered, and it fits nicely in my car cup holder (unlike other liter sized bottles). It’s easy to clean and I love the stainless interior. However, I’m not overly impressed by the length of time it keeps my water cool. My double insulated stainless camelbaks or Yetis seem to insulate much better. This is made from a single layer of stainless steel so doesn’t seem to hold temperature as well. It certainly is cute and fun though if your main goal isn’t keeping a drink cold for as long as possible.
I thought it was stained glass, it’s all metal 😂😭 chain wasn’t even connected. It’s still cute but not what I was expecting.

This crock is a crock alright!  A crock of...  the inside paint was all chipping off.  The most I wiped it with a damp paper towel the more paint came off.  Never even attempted to put butter in it!!! Started a return and they gave us our money back. Told us not to bother to send it back.  Went in the trash!
Very nice quality love the design

I opened this new set of bags, and ended up using 6 when 2 normally would have worked.  Four of the bags ruptured along the seam while piping.  I was only trying to decorate two dozen mini cupcakes, and have never had bags fail like this before, even when decorating much greater quantities.  I have only used Wilton decorating bags, so I'm not sure why I'm having issues with these.
Very Cheap material and a lot smaller then I expected. Does not open or close very easily. Instant return.
This is awesome! Perfect size for shoulders or a lap. Gets really warm too. Just makes watching tv or reading more cozy. My pup loves it too!
Very nice shaker for the price. The accessories that come with shaker are quality made and add to an already nice deal. I haven't had the chance yet to use but appears that it will do the job nicely.

I have mostly aqua colored appliances. This is Sky blue not aqua. If color matters to match you will be disappointed.I hope the seller/maker of this product changes photo to show true color or they should paint it the color aqua.The true color is more sky blue than this picture Someone said this is Tiffany color it is not.
This thing is a good size and it’s quiet.  The air output is average but my goodness, it DEVOURS batteries - and not the cheap kind.
No fragrance but when it lights it can be very pretty (lighting only lasts 1/2 way thru the candle).
Will do a great job with cleaning sides of mixing bowl

Slides all over floor, feels mushy and soggy when wet. Does not dry quickly.  Cat litter from the cat box in the bathroom sticks to it and WILL NOT come off, it is stuck like cement. Tried everything to get it off. Will be tossing it in the trash!

Well that was disappointing. I eagerly received two different scented candles from this company, and I’m returning them both because they produce no scent when lit (which defeats the purpose of why I got them in the first place.) Otherwise, they seem like nice candles.
Looks totally fake. Advertised as 2 sets but please be aware that there are 2 sets of leaves only, not 2 sets of grapes.

I bought these because it is summer and what is the best thing to make in the summer? Sangria. Stirring a pitcher of Sangria however is a pain. I thought to get some plastic pitchers so I could just shake to mix & voila! Sangria at the ready. They are a good size for a single wine bottle with room to spare for the fruit. The only problem is the lid. If you make a red Sangria you'll want to pour over grass or a sink because the spout isn't the best.
A little over a year old and all of the legs have bent outward to the point it is not usable. Not sturdy at all.

This served me well for a while, but one day the lid got stuck and things went downhill from there. There were many times where I locked the lid and couldn't take it off no matter how hard I tried, or the top of the lid would just come off, leaving the rest of it still stuck on the blender. When it happened the other day, I saw there was were layers of old caked up milk all over it. (I often make frappuccinos,) This is supposed to be an easy clean up where you put hot water and soap in the blender and run it for a minute. Well, it gets the outside, but clearly not the inside. I had to wash all of the pieces three times before it got clean. I was able to put it back together and it works, but I ended up buying a different blender to replace it. I'll use this one as my backup, but that's only because I don't want to trash it.

The mirror that arrived looked as nice as advertised.  The back of the mirror is cardboard, so you'll need to use metal hooks on the back to install it.  The mirror is textured; like an orange peel wall texture.

Update 8/22 after a few months of use 4-5 times per week:
The pan is still very non stick, the pan hardly has signs of wear. I’ve only used wooden/plastic utensils in it and there are a few light scratches on the bottom of the pan, but they haven’t damaged the non stick coating and are not even apparent enough to be captured well with a picture. I have noticed browning underneath the handle from the gas flame. It doesn’t bother me and doesn’t come off, but it may bother some!!

Original review:
So far I’ve made fried rice, eggs, one pot pasta, and cooked chicken and sausage in this pan. Not a single bit has stuck even though I like to take minimal oil while cooking! Also cleaning the pot has been super easy, both because nothing has stuck to it in the first place, and because the pan is pretty lightweight. I like the fun colors and have been enjoying this pan. It doesn’t feel top quality like the few Staub pieces I have, but for the price it seems like a good value!

My package arrived in perfect time, but ended up not even mattering...I open the package to find the main basket, the hanging parts for the door, and the entire right side were smashed in, bent, uneven, and completely damaged. What a dissappointment..was looking forward to this!

Temperature seems to work well but be aware the bottom is sharp metal. I used to use a standard candy thermometer with the bulb on the end and I wasn't worried about scratching my non stick pans when clipping it in. With this one I have to be way more careful to avoid scratches.

It seemed to be a great product I have used it once. But since using it we are unable to get it unscrewed and use it again. My partner is very muscular and strong and despite his best attempts could not get it open this rendering it unusable.  I am saddened by this.

The idea of a fully automatic machine really appealed to me. I have a gaggia classic espresso machine and a rocky rancillio grinder that have seen heavy use over the past 4 years. I was interested in this for speeding up the process of making my morning lattes.

I tried several times to use this machine consistently, but I could never get the espresso or the frother to work as well as the manual machines. So I always ended up frustrated and went back to my trusty old manual setup. Specifically, I couldn’t get good creama on my espresso and the frother barely made any microfoam. No matter what I tried, I couldn’t get this to make lattes how I like them.

The assembled chair is a decent quality, rolls smoothly, and is comfortable. It feels solid and looks like a pretty typical office chair. It did take about an hour to assemble and some of the screws were hard to align with the pre drilled holes. Once it was assembled though, I was happy with it.

When I purchased these, I thought I ordered the set with a black top and gold sheer panel, in an attempt to match a black, gold, and copper bed set.  I was wrong.  I received curtains with a dark brown top and sheer tan panels.  I hung the curtains in the room anyway, and was beyond amazed at how well they worked out!
A bit stiff and doesn't yield at all when reclining.
Softness n/a as they are pillow covers.

The hangers look nice and sturdy; should work for heavier garments.  The only complaint is some of the hangers are off balance so a couple in each set stick up above the rest by a few inches when hanging.

This clock is very cheaply made and it arrived with scratches on it as though it were used. I would have returned it but I bought it at the last minute for my child to take to a Summer program. I'd but something else. Not worth the money AT ALL.

I know the gel is supposed to make it cool.  This blanket is soft and holds my sheet down over me so that I don't need a heavier blanket, so I guess in that way it is cooler.  It could be a little larger.
Arrived with frays and color is more dirty white or possibly pale gray -- while pearl ends are smaller and true pearl white.  Returned this one.
Looks nice, that's about it. Microfiber pills and isn't soft.
I purchased two sets.  One set was fine. The other, the collor of the pump bottle is wonky, and the pump won’t attach.
The knives slip over the back edge of the container.

I bought this bc I have boxes under my bed and wanted a underskirt to cover it.
Out of the package, the bed skirt was filled with dust. I had to bring it outside to shake out all the lint and dust on it.
The material is very light and thin.
Also another thing I noticed was the bed skirt tends to move a lot. I have a queen size bed. I placed it perfectly before putting my bed on it. After a few days. 1 side is long than the other. I personally had higher hopes.
The color was what I wanted and it was easy to setup.
Not as plush and I thought it would be but still very nice.

I was given a project of matching a throw to the same color of my parents living room furniture which is a sort of combined mint/sage green...almost a sage/seafoam with less blue. Very hard to find. After searching all of sites I gave Amazon a go...and found this throw within 5 min of searching. When it arrived I was pleasantly surprised at the quality of the throw based on what I paid for it. Very few times do you get such a lovely bargain like this. The color was as close to perfect as I'll be able to find without using fabric from the sofa and was very much appreciated when the gift was opened. My reputation of being the Family Retrieval expert is saved!
Knives came loose from handles. Dangerous.
Once again a disappointment in a pillow purchase. These are NOT good side sleeper pillows as there is zero support. My neck hurt more after sleeping.

Holy cats are these useless pillows. Yea, I knew they were down but I figured they'd be soft enough to use on my bed. Nope. The cotton is very heavy which makes arranging the pillow to where you want it darn near impossible. I thought ok, I'd use them for couch pillows. IMPOSSIBLE! You go to sit down and lean on them and the feathers poke thru making it very difficult to get comfy. I'd send them back but a) I won't go in to any store to return right now due to Covid & b) it'd cost too much to make it worth while to have UPS pick them up. Guess I'm just stuck with them.
This clock is beautiful. We just remodeled our bathroom and this is a lovely addition!

Bought a two pack of these and they’re working well. We bought a couch that reclines but doesn’t have a table in the middle. There’s no spot to put a drink! This product definitely solves that. They are incredibly cheap though. You get what you pay for. If you want something cheap and for short term, these will do the job.

i'm very happy with the quality (so far) of the material and feel of this sheet set.
The tip to removing the smell & scratchiness (its sizing which some people like) is to wash the set first time with a cup or more of white vinegar.
i did that in hot water and everything came out perfectly! Remember this is all cotton so Expect wrinkles (in fact that's a good test that the fabric is 100%cotton lol). (Microfiber will always be the smoothest but also the hottest.)
anyway love the mid-century vibe of the design and the material is alittle thicker so it has a bit of burlap (without the itch lol) feel--but in a good way.
For much softer sheets (but also alittle thinner too) try this set: Amazon Brand – Stone & Beam Remy 100% Cotton Sateen Bed Sheet Set, Queen, Cloud

Needed a new shower curtain and choice this bc it was on sale. The look is nice and no build up of mildew as of yet. I especially like the magnets on the bottom of the curtain to stick to the sides so water doesnt leave the tub.
The description or picture do NOT do this justice. This is the single most beautiful piece of table linen. A big downside is that it is hand wash only and you need a light iron.

Other reviews have mentioned the wrinkles in the tablecloth that don't come out. That is true, but I still give the tablecloth 4 stars because it is pretty sturdy, cleans easily, and fits the table well. It does what I need it to do. I also like the bright colors available.

Died after two uses? Returned.

I was pleasantly suprised with this rug.  I was looking for a low pile rug, but wasn't expecting it to be so soft!  We've bought a number of Safavieh rugs, which are always good quality and seem to standup to the abuse of years of use.

The cabinet did not have an unusual or even noticeable smell. It looks beautiful and seems well made. I was able to unpack it and screw the legs on myself. I put some tiered candles and a plant on top, and hung a distressed mirror on the wall above. I'm using it for my entry way. I love it!
It gets four stars because the distressing on the top could have been done a little better.

The very heavy tabletop takes two people to attach and there's a ton of small screws, so taking it off and putting it on correctly is a huge project. Someone has to hold onto the heavy tabletop while the other deals with the screws like they're a mechanic making his way under a car.

I bought this product because it was $1 cheaper than the MadeSmart product, which I had bought in the past and liked.  Unlike the MadeSmart product getting this thing to stay together as one tray was difficuly and the end result looks sloppy.
What is this? Not pure Paola Santo! It made the house smell like dill pickles. Waste of $

I used this for a birthday card. I had my sister open it outside and we are both happy about that as these are messy. I am still finding little stars however from when I opened the package. Store in an airtight container as they WILL try to escape! Lol

I was excited to get table for my bed and laptop and that excitement lasted about 25 minutes after I set it up. I noticed it swaying, the bridge support on the left side had popped out, either not glued well or should have had deeper holes to slide into. I thought "fine i'll pop it back in" well it kept happening no matter howc careful I was and its practically useless now because every small nudge could send it, and whatever I have on it crashing to the floor.

Ontop of this I noticed a screw from no where on my bed, and it wans't until I stepped on and cut my foot upen on a second one I realized they were loosely drilled in and had fall out with ease. Well now i have a bloody bottom of my foot and I'm not sure if I need tetanus shot.

This i remind you was within 48 hours of use. Who knows what would have fallen to the floor or hurt me next..

Poor or defective unit or the whole design is cheap.
It is a sharp peeler but a little to bulky for my liking. Probably perfect for someone who has difficulty gripping.
Handles were a little large and big around but still easy to hold.  Very attractive and nice looking, great for the formal dinner look I was going for.

I am not impressed. We bought two of these rugs for our bathroom. The first day we lay the rugs down, we notice one is fraying pretty badly. Quality is low.
Another disappointment is how long these stay wet. I would not re-buy these. We'll use them in our apartment and toss when we move out.
The only plus on these is that they do look nice.
The suction on this isn’t the greatest.

Product came with plastic wrapper which appeared to have melted to the product leaving a sticky residue on it. Also, inside both lids there is a plastic piece with the rubber seal. This was loose and rattling around, which seemed like the lids would not provide a tight seal. I read both positive and negative reviews of this product before I purchases and took a risk. Sadly, this product went back into the back within 5 min of opening and I processed my return. I guess from the other reviews I was expecting something different. While these are nice they aren't exactly 'finest quality'. The pairs I received had a very rough feeling at the end where it would go in your mouth. I'm not a fan of splinters on my tongue. The box is nice but impractical for storage. My daughter loved them

Works woderfully.  It light weight, and very bright (perhaps a bit too bright).

Item was a gift. It was well received.

I immediately threw all of these in the dishwasher hoping they wouldn't all melt but it was fine. These are working great so far. Glad I don't have to throw away as many straws now. I do wish there was different lengths. I have some shorter cups that I normally would cut down a plastic straw for.
I will post in the future if the dishwasher destroys them after a few washes.
It does seal tight on the yeti large containers, but does not allow to use the yeti straws. It will not fit through the hole. I had to buy smaller straws.

I was really excited to receive this record holder so I could finally have a display for all of my vinyls, however upon opening the box I saw that it had been packaged with damaged pieces.
Both of the acrylic ends are deeply scraped which isn’t very noticeable once assembled it’s still the fact they cane packaged that way. The outer box and the packaging it cans in where fine so it makes me think they were damaged at the manufactures. Also the instructions printed inside the box are so hard to read you basically just have to wing it.
Attempted to put unit together but the pre drilled holes are so off and crooked that the screws don't go in all the was causing them to stick out and the whole unit to sit a bit slanted. Honestly not worth the money or hassle should've just spent the extra $10 for a fully acrylic one.

I purchased this for my bathroom. I like how it was easy to place, self explanatory. However, when I came in 1 hour later it was found on the floor. Maybe its the wall material but other plastic suctions have worked in the past.
These are so beautiful. I got so many compliments on my mantle this year. I live them so much I don't want to take them down!!

I bought this wok for easy home cooking. I am korean and make a decent amount of fried rice with eggs and kimchi. It is extremely easy to clean. Cooks the food well. But after 1 month I started to notice very minor scratches coming off from the wok. But no big deal to me.

If you are carrying light loads with no pointy edges, these bags are for you.

If you are carrying medium to heavy loads with boxes, and cardboard/plastic packaging then these bags just won't cut it. The handles are too thin. The bags themselves are just too thin, cheap, tear easy, and fall apart due to disintegration of the recycled plastic. They literally turn into plastic dust. Do not store them in your car, they will turn into dust even faster.

Save yourself some grief and buy thicker/heavy duty bags.

In general I like this clock. I purchased it for my 3 yr old learning to use a real bed so I wanted the changing lights in the morning. Asthetically it’s the simplest and nicest looking option I found on amazon.
However I HATE the nightlight feature. The face of the clock can be set a variety of colors using the nightlight setting. I thought I’d be able to turn it on and set the color for the face of the clock permanently purple. But every morning when the wake up timer switches the clock to yellow then green, it overrides the nightlight setting and turns it off. So if you want the clock to be lit up a fun color every day and night, you have to manually turn the light back on every morning. Even though it’s not that huge of a deal, it’s annoying for a clock that cost $50 and you hope would be perfect.
Was looking for new bath tube mats. Good price. Like the different colors. Value packs. Easy to clean. good suction.
Inexpensive and works exactly as needed. No complaints here.

I have a family member with a lung disorder and bought this to keep the air clean. To better his health.
The problems I've had:
The machine keeps saying the air is not clean. And it goes in high mode more hours and hours. Never goes down. It gets annoying after it being on the high level for hours on end. Ive tried cleaning the sensor but it did not help. Maybe the one I got was defective. But this is the problem I've had with mine.

Bought this for my wife, she has friends that come over and they drink wine and have snacks for a get together. The material is well made. Looks of good quality. Comes with different cutting and eating utensils.
However takes some time to clean. Takes up a lot of counter space so u need a big kitchen counter when drying it.

I received a portable ac with no window mount. This fit great with my tubing. It was easy to setup. The design is simple yet effective. There are small gaps between the three pieces. Its easy to fix with some tape. The measurements is exact as descibed. My summer is going to be cool.
Bigger than I thought and hold a ton of groceries. Well made and strong too! Animals designs are adorable !
Don't like how thin this product is, and it is harder to keep clean.

I used this for a couple of weeks in my kitchen. I don't have a compost pile, but when I found out it takes over a year to make compost, it lost its appeal for that purpose. These came horribly tarnished and looking so bad I would be embarrassed to put these on my table.
It’s fine for a quick fix. I used two cans and my tree looked more green / gray dusted than flocked. It’s not as bright snowy white as pic. A bit messy but cleans easily. Cans clog easily.

After reading all the reviews I was reluctant to purchase this juicer but threw in the towel after weeks of research lead me to exhaustion. I should've kept with the research cause this machine is a hassle and a half.

First there is a little flip portion cup in the chute which is beyond annoying for all produce except celery. I managed to pop it out for actual use of the wide chute.
Speaking of celery, even if you do chop it down the fibers still get stuck in the waste chute and are a pain to clean out. Towards the end of making my juices I noticed the strainer was so compacted with produce bits that it stopped pressing juice and was just whirling around a medley of mashed up produce affecting the juice yield.
After my first batch of juice I rinsed out the machine and prepped my produce for my second batch of juice giving the machine time to rest and cool off. As I finished up the second batch of juice my machine began making a clicking noise which also seems to be a common issue. The machine has only made two batches of juice and its already making loud clicking noises!? I know they say you get what you pay for but $90 for a juicer that only lasts for 2 batches of juice is ridiculous. Do not recommend especially if you like to make juice in advance because after only a small amount of produce the juicer essentially stops juicing and just creates apple sauce.

I bought this for the winter time. It was easy to put on and keeps most of the air that comes through the ac vent. If I sit next to the ac I can feel a tad breeze when it is very windy and cold outside.
Beautiful and great price!

...the colors that are shown aren't true IRL. I got the green which had shown more of a lighter, sage green but when it came it was a darker Kelly green. This does the job. It looks nice. We replaced our last one because it was so grimy and rusted. The only difference from our last one is this one is much shorter! Good thing we have other spots in the shower to put our shampoo/conditioner. It still works but... I probably wouldn’t rebuy this product
It was not as advertised. These were gifts and I  had no time to retur and exchange.

These straws are fine but not as long as I was expecting. I buy the same brand and color scheme from Bed Bath and Beyond and they are maybe 1/2 in longer, which allows me to use with my Tervis cups. These straws are too short for use with large Tervis tumblers

I immediately washed this full/queen comforter in my top load washer and then threw it in the dryer on low heat to see how it would wash. I wish it was slightly more blush/pale pink as I was hoping, but that’s always hard to tell from a picture.

Overall I’m impressed with they way this washed and the coziness of the comforter. It’s a nice medium weight that is neither too thin or hot. I typically get my kids bedding from pottery barn kids, and I truly think this is comparable in quality and cuteness.
So much prettier than the picture!!!
This is beautiful. A prefect spring/summer comforter. I feel  like i brought my garden into my bedroom! I love it.

I recently bought my daughter the Thermos Funtainer 10oz with folding spoon to take soup to school for lunches, it was $16.99. It has worked well but I love all of my hydro flask water bottles so I was excited to try this. I tested them out this morning side by side. I started out by filling them both with hot tap water to warm up the metal insides while I boiled water. I dumped the tap water and then filled them both with boiling water (temp was 210 degrees). I sealed them tightly and left them on the counter for 4 hours. I then took the temperature of the water. The hydro flask water was 137 degrees and the Thermos Funtainer water was 151 degrees. I could actually see a small bit of steam coming out of the Thermos container and couldn’t see anything coming from the HydroFlask. The HydroFlask feels more substantial at first (it weighs 13 oz and the Thermos weighs 9oz), but the lid for the Thermos is bulkier and I guess that’s why the Thermos retains heat better.
Overall neither leak and my 6 year old can take off and put on the lid to both, so they are both easy enough for small kids to use. But when it comes to heat retention and price, Thermos wins.

great customer support!
they sent me a new firmware, and a replacement awair glow device to make sure the device itself isnt the problem.
the device itself worked the same as my original awair glow, but the new firmware they pushed to my device works much better (version 1.2.3 versus the original 1.0.0)
all readings are MUCH more accurate now with the new firmware!

temperature and humidity readings are still slightly too high (~1C and 8%), and the co2 readings get more and more inaccurate the higher the CO2 concentration is in the room (150ppm too high @~1000ppm) , but overall its more or less accurate now. good enough to get an idea of the air quality.
its still not as good as my other cheaper temperature and humidity and CO2 sensor, but this one has an app and allows me to track and log and access everything via my phone.
i hope they can improve sensors even more with better firmware updates in the future.
right now, in my opinion, the temperature and humidity readings are not accurate enough to use them to control a fan or dehumidifier via the built in power plug relay switch... but fingers crossed, hope they can improve this further.

Like all such devices, this handy device measures the conductivity of water, which, at a given temperature, depends in a nearly linear fashion the concentration of dissolved salts in the water. The conductivity (which is one of the measurements it will display) is converted into parts-per-million (PPM) of dissolved solids (mostly salts) through a correction factor (which is usually in the 0.5-0.9 range). This device also measures temperature, presumably to use it as a corrective factor. It's easy to use -- just press a button on its face and stir the business end through the water for 5-10s, then read the value -- you can cycle through the values by pressing the "mode" key.
My reason for giving it three stars is that its conversion factor from electrical conductivity to total dissolved solids seems to be very small -- less than 0.5. I use it for my saltwater pool: it shows 4950 for electrical conductivity, but gives me a clearly wrong 2330ppm for TDS. A chemical test for NaCl (one of several varieties of salt in the pool, but the most important for a saltwater pool) shows 3000ppm -- and the TDS includes the sodium chloride and so should be at least as high; indeed, another brand of TDS measuring pen gives a TDS of 3300ppm.
If, however, you consider only the electrical conductivity and use your own correction factor to derive the TDS, things look a lot better: with the most common correction factor (2/3), I would get 3300ppm for TDS, which is what that other brand gets.
So, good for measuring EC (conductivity) and temperature, but you need to derive the TDS yourself from the EC -- don't even bother reading the value provided by the device.

I have experience with 3D printers, so I was able to overcome the obstacles that this kit provided. My main issue is with the hot-end. Put simply, it doesn't work well at all. Jamming is a very common occurrence with the stock hot-end. Yes, I assembled it correctly with the bowden tube fully against the heat-break. I replaced the hot-end with an E3D-V6 which required printing a new mount/effector because the supplied hot-end is a different shape. Once this was done, it worked much better. But I did a little more.

The Repetier firmware that this comes with is... not the greatest. It generally works, but all of the square-root calculations (a requirement of a delta printer) it's asking of the board is a lot, and makes for very jittery printing. I was going to put Marlin on it, but it requires the same sort of processing power. That's when I discovered Klipper. Klipper does require a Raspberry Pi which does all the heavy calculations, then sends the basic movement commands to the printer over USB. The printer runs buttery smooth now. Klipper was very easy to install and configure.

After several failed prints I wrote a nasty review. After some more trial and error, I determined my bed adhesion issue was operator error. So here is the revised review.
The magnetic sticker went down smooth as glass with no bubbles at all. I cleaned the bed very well and followed the instructions provided with the QR code in the packaging. Take your time and go slow, your patience will be rewarded.
As for the bed adhesion it took a few attempts, but I determined my Z height needed to be a good bit lower than with my glass bed. In order for PLA to stick to the textured surface you really have to squish it into the tiny crevasses. Once I figured this out all my prints stick and get a lovely surface finish from the textured side. I didn't have to change any of my temp or 1st layer settings other than the correct Z height. So far I am pretty happy with it since I figured out what I was doing wrong.

Let me start by saying that I already have an Airwolf3d 5.5, which I bought completely assembled. I then bought an Airwolf3d 5.5 kit which I assembled myself.

I purchased this kit to build a Prusa i3 from scratch, and it works, but there are a lot of things I don't like about this kit:
The RAMPS board had a short across two pins on the back of the board. Thankfully, I noticed this before I plugged anything in and was able to clean it up with the touch of a soldering iron and a solder sucker.
The heatsinks are not attached to stepper motor drivers. You have to buy special glue to attach them. Without the heatsinks, the stepper drivers will overheat. Without the glue, the heatsinks move around and can easily short out the RAMPS.
Two of the stepper drivers were bad, but were replaced free of charge.
The kit has a separate SD card reader which is unnecessary because the LCD has one built in.
They claim that the extender board for the fans is "plug and play". But you have to solder pins onto the RAMPS because the aux 12v power does not have pins already soldered on. There is very little online support at geeetech regarding how to configure these fans. Turns out they are on pins 6 and 11, but figuring out the sw config was time consuming. They only work correctly in one very specific config that is contrary to the marlin settings. Incorrectly configured, it causes the X and Y travel to stall and jump.
The belt is not the standard belt for reprap machines. You need to tweak the X and Y motor step values to account for the different belt. Belt clamps also have to be modified to hold them correctly. The belt is rather ridged and causes some inconsistent movement.
There is no thermistor for the heat bed
The hotend is not assembled very well and plastic slowly leaks above the nozzle
I couldn't get the limit switches to work at all. These mechanical switches are board mounted, and I suspect the board mimics opto switches. I never figured them out so replaced them with standard micro switches.
The NEMA 17 motors were smaller than I expected and I wound up replacing the two for the Z axis with larger motors

I do like:
The price
The LCD with the built in SD card reader.
The hot end has two nozzles, .35 and .4 mm
The heat bed and borosilicate glass
Lots of wires to connect things

I did construct a fully functional 3d printer using most of the parts in this kit. But there is very little support or reference documentation to help you. The printer makes very accurate parts, within .2% tolerance.

If you are skilled in electromechanical device repair and maintenance, skilled in software configuration, and you have plenty of time to research and figure things out, you might have fun with this kit.

2014.10.01 UPDATE

They have tick marks every 2 ml, which is inconvenient if my most common dosage is 5 ml. The tick marks they use are too wide to have them every 1ml. The tick mark style used in these syringes makes it harder to measure the liquid. ½ inch refers to the needle length. Most people are better off with a shorter needle since injections do not need to go deep. I use these for peptides. 30G is a bigger needle size than 31G. 30G is harder to jab into the skin and will leave a bigger hole. I prefer the 31G size. 30G will fill faster if you are mixing the peptides with BAC water, and it might be better if you are injecting a full 100 ml since it will be faster. Thinner needles are more prone to bend.

After having used these for a while I can conclude that it is significantly harder to pierce the skin than other brands of needles. The tips seem dull and I am having to use blunt force rather than piercing to get into the skin.

I prefer the EasyTouch syringes due to their thinner more accurate tick marks. The current price for these Vaxxen Labs syringes is $17 for 100, which is $0.17 each. EasyTouch costs $15 for 100, although prices vary.

I bought this robot vacuum based on reviews I read. It is the first robo vac I’ve ever bought so my review may be based on what I thought a robot vacuum should do. First off, it does not map to your floor plan. It says it doesn’t in the details but I didn’t know any better. It operates in a criss cross pattern, which may or may not get everywhere you’d like. We do not have any carpet and our floors are dark so everything shows up on them. We have two cats and it does a good job picking up hair but I don’t feel it really gets the larger bits as well. Because it operates in the crisis cross pattern it seem like it misses areas.

The app is just okay. I’m very tech savvy and I was able to easily program it but I can’t for the life of me figure out how to change or stop that program. My biggest beef with this vac is that it gets stuck ALL THE TIME. I’ve found myself not using it because it’s just too much hassle to keep picking it up and moving it every time it gets stuck. I have it programmed to start cleaning at 4am each day and there has not been a day yet that I haven’t come down in the morning to find it stuck. It gets stuck on doorways, under furniture, and especially on the bases of our bar stools.

After my experience with this product, at least I know what to look for in our next one.

For DIY'ers fixing a 5200, it's pretty easy: Pull off the rubber gasket (where the jar/blade assembly sits), and remove the 4 screws. Remove the drive spline using a 5/64th allen wrench (find the arrow and a tiny hole to insert the wrench into). Separate the two halves of the body. Cut the old wires off the old pot (as close to the pot as possible). Strip and solder the wires to the new pot and switch them out. Be careful to put the vibration dampeners back on the body if they came off. Reattach the drive spline and fit the rubber gasket back over the top. Done.

There were actually two books in between “At Peace” where we meet Frankie and Benny and their book but the book takes place right after “At Peace” ended where Frankie is shot. Benny comes to the hospital and Frankie pretends to be asleep every time he is there. I cannot blame her with the way the Bianchi’s treated Frankie after Vinnie was killed. I actually feel like she lets them all off the hook too easily but that probably says more about my poor character huh?

The ‘Burg series is supposed to be more about crime and (although I haven’t finished #4, I am having a hard time getting into a heroine with the name of Dusty) the rest are about serial killers and the mob and a human trafficking ring. What is going on in this book is not nearly on the same level as the other books and in fact Frankie isn’t involved or actually ever in any imminent danger. Which brings me to my next point. When we meet Benny I felt like he was a typical Kristen Ashley hero (meaning: alpha, macho, tough guy, etc) but I didn’t get that feeling from Benny in this book. In fact when one of the Hot Bunch (I won’t tell but it spurred me to read his book again) makes an appearance it is abundantly clear that they are not on the same level.

The best part of the book was that Frankie got her happily ever after. But when I read a Rock Chick book or a ‘Burg book I want action, danger, kidnappings, near misses, and angst. The angst was present at the beginning of the book, but that is all I can say. I was disappointed for how long I had been looking forward to their story.

For more Kristen Ashley reviews, visit my blog at: http://bibliobibulireviews.blogspot.com/

This is the second book in the Unfinished Heroes series. As the author says, this series is a little different than her other series in that it is about the “anti-hero”. Knight is a much better anti-hero than Sylvie as he is basically a pimp (although that word is never used). Sylvie works for Knight sometimes so I guess that is where her “anti-hero-ness” comes in. Oh, and this series has more of the smexy stuff than her other series and by that I mean it is more graphic and…hmmm how to say this…more adventurous? We will go with that…more adventurous. This story is about Sylvie and Creed. They were childhood sweethearts and were torn apart right when it should have gotten good for them.

Throughout the story we get snippets of how Sylvie and Creed started. I actually think those snippets were my favorite part of the entire book. Usually, I hate when authors do that and I can’t wait to get through passages like that, but I could. not. wait. to read those sections. We get to see when Sylvie and Creed meet when she is six and he is eleven and get to read about most of the major events in their young lives.

Anyway, they were separated when they were young and it was tragic for both of them, and by that I don’t mean that their little hearts were broken (although they most certainly were), I mean that they were both abused/tortured is terrible ways. Neither has known what the other went through and they haven’t seen each other for sixteen years. That is pretty much the beginning of the book.

Those are my favorite parts of the book. I loved reading about little Sylvie and little Creed. I think my absolute favorite is when Creed realizes he likes Sylvie but she is still too young to be with him. And I loved hearing about the tragedy that took them apart. That sounds weird because they were both seriously abused and almost killed, but I liked that it wasn’t some stupid “I thought they would be better off without me and so I left”. It was well thought out most people would react the way that Sylvie and Creed did.

What I didn’t like about the book was the rest of the storyline. There was a little drama at the beginning with Knight needing some help and a little at the end with Hawk Delgado (from Mystery Man, whom for the record I love, love, love). Besides that, nothing much happens and it is all about Creed and Sylvie working through their demons and I am sorry but it was b-o-r-i-n-g! I actually skimmed a couple pages and I NEVER do that.

The other thing I didn’t like was that Sylvie was too much of a KA man character. She dressed like a guy and talked like a commando. Yuck. But I get that it was part of what happened to her to make her hard, I just didn’t like that she spoke like every other KA hero. You with me? (That is a joke. That is Sylvie’s phrase in this book.)
As I said in a previous post, I pretty much read this one to get to the next one. Raid is supposed to be out February 28th and I am getting a little worried that it won’t be released then…but I guess I can’t complain because it popped up on my radar unexpectedly. There is a couple free chapters on the author’s website.

P.S. Don’t google “Creed by Kristen Ashley” images. It is not PG…or PG 13…or even R…in face, it is probably XXX. Now that I said that you are probably all going to do that, but I am telling you…just don’t. You can’t un-see that.

This was a time travel romance that takes place in Scotland. Some of my favorite aspects right there: I love books that take place in Scotland (I wonder if Scottish people realize the obsession the rest of the world has with them) and it is about time travel. I compare all time travel books against the Outlander series so my expectations are high. This did not meet those standards, HOWEVER, when compared to pretty much every other attempt at time travel romance, this was extremely well written.

I think the hardest part of time-travel books is writing in the correct response by both the person who does the time traveling and those they eventually try to convince. There must be a certain level of disbelief on both sides because…it is time travel, but on the other hand if there is too much denial it is just plain annoying. This author struck the right cord on this topic which is a HUGE part of the storyline.

Altheia was likeable as was Malcolm. I actually didn’t see where the book was going, although I knew that Black Hugh would come back to play some part of the story line. I liked the little bit of extra that Altheia brought with her visions and I liked that Giselle was responsible and it wasn’t some mystic portal that no one has found until now.

The jury is still out on whether I want to read the next one although I read the first chapter with Robley and found that engrossing so that is a good start.

Permanent Jet Lag is a little different from most 'romance' novels out there - it's a cross between a 'love' story and a coming of age novel, I suppose, which really isn't doing it justice. Lucas has just finished his first year at university, having chosen to to away to San Francisco for four years rather than a local university in a desperate need to get away from his family (he's the oldest of five kids), and what you find to be a tragedy in his senior year. His parents are loving if a bit stifling, his siblings care for him if a bit involved with their own lives, despite being gay he didn't suffer in his hometown, and he had a good group of friends. It wasn't a terrible life, but he had his reasons - I think some people can find themselves relating very easily to Lucas as a person trying to find their way in the world, to dealing with growing up and facing 'real' emotions and relationships.

He goes back home to find that one of his best friends is getting married to someone she'd met in the last few months, and is drawn toward her soon to be husband's best man - Chris. The author, A.N. Casey, does a really good job of portraying the relationship and Chris in general, the problems Chris is facing with his health issues, Lucas with his 'permanent jet lag' (his disconnect from other people, his struggle with emotions and relationships). This isn't the happiest of reads and there was one point where things felt a little contrived, but on the whole, despite some sadness I thought this was a really good book and one I enjoyed reading, that had a good feel over all and I felt dealt with things realistically in that people made mistakes and did things they might regret, they weren't always perfect in their intentions but you understood why they were doing what they did. I really liked these characters and wouldn't mind finding out what happens to them in a few years. You got the feel that they were genuine and growing up and moving forward as best they could. I'd like to see more from this author in the future, too.

Hyena is raw and real. Rude Jude's life story (some of it) written by him and clearly in his own voice. Plenty of drugs and sex. But there are moments you can see beyond that and tap into his insight on certain aspects of life that's applicable to most of us, one way or another. Even if it's not, you'll definitely like getting a glimpse into someone else's past. Found myself completely shocked... Dumbfounded! ... Shaking my head, laughing and agreeing (or disagreeing) with him at times. Simply put, "Hyena" is worth buying and reading. Appreciate Jude's raw honesty. Thanks for sharing!

In the third book Lily senses that something is wrong with Tallis and goes to find him. What she finds is “Donnchadh” (not sure of the spelling, but this is the warrior that inhabits Tallis) is in control of Tallis. It takes Lily waaaay too long to figure out that Tallis isn’t in control. I knew it almost immediately and maybe the author was hoping to cause some suspense here, but it just makes Lily seem daft. I still really enjoyed this scene as it shows Lily being resourceful and in control. Eventually we get our Tallis back, but only right before Lily must leave for her dinner with Alaire. You can imagine that Tallis is none too pleased about this news.

Lily ends up getting another mission while she is already in the Underground City and makes her way to the Toy Store. I remember from book 2 that Lily’s friend Saxon (from the gym) said it was his least favorite place. Tallis and Bill both seem to enjoy themselves immensely there. I will admit I am not really sure how it fits in with the rest of the Underground City, except maybe the author needed an excuse to put some sexy ladies in Tallis’s path to make Lily jealous. There ends up being more drama than usual while Lily is in the Toy Story and what is left over of the book leaves Lily almost dying (more so than is typical for Lily). Alaire ends up being the problem and conveniently the solution. Through this Lily learns some stuff about Tallis that may end their friendship.

I really enjoyed this book. I hope I don’t have to wait too much longer to read the next one. We got a lot more alone time with Lily and while I don’t always agree with her logic I can see where she is coming from in making her decisions.

Chase, Dave. The Opioid Crisis Wake-Up Call: Health Care is Stealing the American Dream. Here’s How We Take It Back (Kindle Location 2). Health Rosetta Media. Kindle Edition.
AS a Program Evaluator for a county-level opioid dependence and overdose prevention initiative I HAD to read this book. What I found was validation that after 35 years of assisting chronically ill populations and surviving an ever-threatening environment as both healthcare executive and person with a 52year history of living with diabetes: I hadn’t lost my mind!
Mr. Chase deserves accolades for “Telling the story”. This entertaining and articulate book was surprisingly fun to read. The story of how American Society gave birth to such dysfunction and the Opioid Crisis as a breathtaking health outcome that proves we are “suckers” for a good con is not a novel or even a fact-based work of fiction.
For C-Level executives looking for actionable information the book describes the motivation of the business stakeholders and their marketing strategies and provides both tools and methods to restructure the care for one third of American lives (100M people). Perhaps the most important take-home however is the Author’s claim that experienced guides with knowledge of the “shell game” is critical. If you read nothing at all, please enjoy the case studies such as The Rosen Hotels and Resorts story and equip yourself with what he refers to as The Rosetta Principles. As you review them keep in mind that Openness, Transparency, Peer to Peer Networks, Care Teams, Member Engagement, Data Ownership, and Value-Based Primary Care are not new, but terminology for older concepts such as the Patient Centered Medical Home. However, in this book they may for the first time be introduced as critical links in a chain that might save the next generation of health care consumers.
As for the Opioid Crisis, you will find a nice allegory that dates-back to the 1970s when America first became involved in Employee Wellness Programming through pioneers such as Control Data Corporation. The Lesson? “If we do not keep our eye on the health of our employees, understand the array of physical, social, emotional and behavioral health variables that give rise to a stable, content and productive work-force we will lose our largest asset.”
New systems of population health could emerge from the Health Rosetta. They would be owned by communities who have studied what they have, disposed of wasteful business processes; examined their values and established a new definition for health and wellness as the measure of value and metric of exchange in the new economy. As we progress we will need new monitoring systems that are valid, reliable and sensitive to change in the stability of the system. I can only hope that we have a willing society of benefits consultants, public health experts and patient rights advocates to lead the charge.

This is the third book. By the time I read this there are still two more books out. That is the way I like to read series, otherwise I forget things and it is agony waiting for the next installment. This series is a spinoff of “The Big Bad Wolf” series and follows the 13 Kings of the supernatural realms and their quest to find their queens while also dealing with supernatural disasters.

The Warlock King is Jason Alberich (spelling?). He was present in one of “The Big Bad Wolf” books but as a bad guy. He kidnaps his childhood friend gone love obsession, Dannai Cage. Since then he has seen the error of his ways, but there is still a lot of tension between Jason and the wolves.

Jason’s queen is Chloe who is apparently one of the original Akyri. Even after finishing the book I am not quite sure how that makes her different from the next generations of Akyri, except she is mucho old. Chloe knows Jason is after her and she wants nothing to do with him. She is running, but he continues to keep an eye on her from afar. The 13 kings are heading into deep waters with the mysterious evil that they are only just beginning to understand.

Not a lot happens in this installment except Jason and Chloe have a meeting of the minds. It was kind of anti-climactic. I am anticipating good things from some of the other kings. I hope the Seelie and Unseelie kings are good as well as the Shadow King (very mysterious!) and I am not even sure we have meet the rest of them, but I hope there is more to their stories.

If you are looking for an awesome UF series try the Rylee Adamson series by this author. It is one of my top favorites (along with Ilona Andrews, Patricia Briggs). Since I love Rylee so much I decided to give this a shot.

I like the idea of the story: two sisters, one destined to fulfill a prophecy. Irish lore. One sister is taken and the other is trying to get her back and to do this she must accept the help of creatures she didn't know existed before. There are two factions of the fae in this and I really like the descriptions of the "bad ones". Quinn has a fear of the water and this drives many of her actions and reactions in this book, which is annoying but more realistic than making the heroine suddenly overcome her fears.

There is a small amount of romance or what might turn into it, but right now it appears to be the beginnings of a love triangle, which I happen to despise. We will see how that plays out.

The main character Quinn, was a little annoying at times, making stupid decisions. Hopefully as she matures, that will fade. It was over half of the book before the reader finds out that Quinn is 26 years old. I was reading her as younger than that so that clarification should be earlier in the book, but I am happy she is older.

Overall a good quick read. I will definitely read on.
