I didn't try them right away.  When I opened them for the 1st time, it has been about one month since purchase, and 8 of them were completely no color left...
It's just ink. But I have to say it doesn't leak when it's injected into the printer. Not much else to elaborate on.

I think it is on the expensive side but I like it anyway. It is intuitive to use and the keyboard and screen are large enough that it will age with me if it lasts. It prints fast. I did not buy the case. It, along with accompanying cassettes, fit well in a $6 Sterilite container from Walmart. The only thing I would change is to be able to dim the back-light.
It is soft and pillow-like and has no framework at all. It smushes down completely from your weight. It is just a flattened wedge between you and you chair.
The clip is super cheap and bulky, a lower profile clip would make this better. It broke the first day as well.

This is relatively comfortable, and the magnifications seem about as advertised, but no amount of adjustment, including bending the bridge slightly with the aid of boiling water, would allow me to focus both eyes through two lenses pointed at the same, necessarily small, area, hence my title. In practical terms, for many uses I found it easier anyway to select my target area with an unaided left eye and then use my right to look at it closely, switching after the right got tired. If I could give it  2.5 stars, I would.

This product is a great idea for one who reads books and may want to jot notes in a dim-lit environment.  My experience while using the first pen has been somewhat 'pretty good' in that, it lights up a whole page however, after approximately thirty minutes of use, the light in the pen gradually becomes dim.  Since quite a few of these come in a package, I use another one and have learned, when any of these become dim, click it off for a few hours for a somewhat recharge.  After it is clicked back on, the light remains bright for a shorter time than before.  Again, the price is right for the number of pens, however.

For printing photos, my preferred printers are Canon. I don't use this one or the little one I've had for a very long time for documents, though, because I have others which employ inks that aren't as costly. With Canon, I always go with their cartridges, never taking a chance with generics or off brand. The TS9020 gave me no problems during setup and since mine is the white version, I am especially careful to keep it clean. I hope the white doesn't yellow over time as many plastics tend to do, and will note that this printer is heavy on the cheaper plastic components. I tried the duplex printing to assure that it works, but won't use it often since I use the printer typically for photos. Because I like the ink quality, I also use this for inkjet label printing. I've only used this for a couple of weeks and my ink levels are still good, after printing photos on both glossy and matte photo papers. I enjoy printing wirelessly from the phone but prefer to use a computer with a larger screen and more editing options. The cost of the 6 ink cartridges is in line with other Canon cartridges, I guess, but with the greater number, the total cost is higher. Like most home printers now, all parts are delicate, so I'm cautious, even when touching the easy to read screen or using the card reader.

I currently use a magnetized pocket organizer that is about twice the size of this one on my refrigerator door, but the magnet is not very strong so it consistently slides down with every opening and closing of the door. I was intending to use the [[ASIN:B00JJRMK9Y Five Star Locker Organizer, Tri-Pocket with Dry Erase Surface, 7 x 7.5 Inches, White with Cobalt Blue Pockets (72622)]] for our front metal door, however because it is much more to my liking, this one will replace the sliding pocket on the refrigerator and the other will be donated. Our front door will remain pocket free for now.

This was made in China of very white plastic with blue accents and has a rounded symmetry, making it very attractive in person. All lines curve with the exception of the flat backing and substantial rear magnet. My only criticism is that the molded five star emblem and stars in the larger right sided pocket interfere with writing when I jot down a note with a dry erase marker. I think the current price is very high for this item, but do find it to be a good quality which is worthy of about a dozen dollars at most. If I were Five Star, I would include a marker.

This would have been great in the 1990s or 2000s. For 2016, it's very slow. The keyboard shape might be okay for little kids but it's just ridiculous for adults. I do like the pronunciations. But it takes way too long to navigate through the various menus (in 2016) to get to the dictionaries. I think I'd prefer a device that does less but does it better and more effectively.
They fit but chair won’t move/roll so probably sending back 😕
Stuck well to my wall. Covered the hole that the door knob was making in the wall and will adequately protect the wall from further damage.

For any Mac OS Lion 10 x beware.  Loaded the drivers from ESPON's web site with no problem (a note inside box from EPSON required it).  Later I had a malfunction with Apple Airport so I had to reload the passwords and drivers to correct the problem. Was never able to reload the 730 drivers because for some reason the printer would not accept the router password where as it did before. Perhaps my situation was an anomaly, but most printers allow the user to load and reload the printer drivers as many times as they want.
Thought this book would be great for me but it's not the print is to light and to small it maybe good for someone else waist of money for me

I like labels and have recently received several types and sizes for review.  The [[ASIN:B00JVUQ52C Avery Kids Self-Laminating Labels, Assorted, Pack of 48 (41433)]] may very well be my favorites of right now, despite their hefty price tag.  Made in the US, each of the 4 sheets contains three sizes in two shapes and their colored tabs are attractive.  Write on the label using a permanent marker, remove the backing sheet from the laminate, press down on the laminate.  Now you have your laminated label and it should live through your dishwasher or microwave, they say.  I am reluctant to try, so I cannot tell you if this is a true statement.  I've used the skinny labels for cable identification and am prepared to use the quarter sized dots on jelly jars after using a very fine tipped sharpie.  At over $3 per sheet, these are more expensive than I would pay, but I would look for them at half that price.  They are labels.

Kai scissors are just the best. Those serrated blades cut so smooth and effortlessly. For a big treat, try a pair of the "pro" models for cutting out garments. So smooth and easy on your hands.  I have several pairs of both.

My favorite pens! Great feel and smooth flow of ink. The fact that the color gel ink matches the color of the pen is a fun touch. I use them in my home office so even pink ink is okay! The ink colors are dark enough that there is absolutely no issue seeing what you're writing. The only reason I didn't give them 5 stars is that they run out of ink faster than other pens. Good thing there are 8 to a pack!

I am not a student but love paper, pens and ink. The [[ASIN:B007CGUHHG Five Star Composition Book, 100 College-Ruled Sheets, 9.75 x 7.5 Inch Sheet Size, Lime (72861)]] is a nice carrying size, fine plastic cover, sewn binding and black tape spine reinforcement. The paper is standard for five star with the blue lines and red line down the left margin. The rounded corners are cut well.

This book is fine for pencil, ballpoint, rollerball pens, however I would not recommend it for fountain pens because most will bleed through the paper to the reverse side. Some may actually bleed onto the page beneath. I use Rhodia pads when I want a high quality paper with perforated sheets and to my surprise, some can be purchased for the same price as this one. One thing to note is that the plastic cover on this is flexible, so care must be taken if you are writing in it without resting it on a flat surface for support.

Wilson Jones hit it out of the ballpark with the View-Tab Transparent Dividers.  Sold in a package of 5 sets of 8-Tabbed dividers, it's a breeze to assemble what you need quickly.  Just use the online template to create a sheet of your tab names and print it, thereby allowing you to view each tab from the rear sheet without actually writing, printing or sticking adhesive labels on the physical dividers.  For me, the only weakness is that unless a reversed page is created for the front of the binder, the identification of the tab requires an additional step to figure out.  The curve of each tab is more comfortable for me than angular tabs and I really love being able to also have a clear view at the first page beneath each divider.  This opens the door to creativity in designing the pages.  I'm certain these will hold up well and am also impressed at the affordability when these are purchased in a package of 5.  I also really like the colors.

The boxes in which I've been storing and using hanging file folders aren't designed with lowered sides, so they haven't been the most satisfactory. I had high hopes for the [[ASIN:B00G476NR2 Bankers Box Hang'N'Stor Medium-Duty Storage Boxes, Letter, 4-Pack (0078403)]] and for the most part, they are suited to my needs. They are easy to assemble whether or not you view the diagram printed on one one of the hidden folds if you are intuitive or have ever assembled a Bankers type box. I had no problem with the medium weight cardboard tearing or not folding along the fold lines. It took less than a minute to put one together and it looks good. The "Fastfold" technique truly is really quick. One thing I'd suggest is to fold in the hand opening prior to folding the larger sides over inside the box. That was my only burp with the first box. I assembled a lid as soon as I removed it from the top of the flat package and didn't realize at first that the lids are designed with a hand flap that slides into the carrying cutouts on the box for additional support. I initially folded the flap into the lid ends prior to constructing the lid ends. There is no additional material included in the package, with the exception of a little orange sweepstakes entry attached onto the rear interior of my package.

I've owned and used assorted Bankers Boxes throughout many years including those with lift off lids, fixed lids, and drawers. This is my first for hanging files. I am disappointed in the smoothness by which the brand new hanging files I'm using move to and fro. Because the box is medium weight, not extremely durable and there is no additional reinforcement or railing, I don't think they will hold up to daily use. I'd recommend these attractive boxes for storage with occasional file movement. On the other hand, because they are lighter in weight without any reinforcement, it's easier to lift the boxes for transporting them. I'm not expecting these boxes to last forever, but for now they are fine and I can recommend them for cases where hanging folders are needed. For other uses, I'd suggest using boxes without the shorter sides, particularly the heavier weight boxes.
Easy to put together and looks great

Got these for my parents and for my at home office needs. Kind of disappointed with this shipment of tape corrector. They did not seem to work and would NOT roll correctly. Very frustrating. Had to throw some of them away since they were such a mess.
Garbage. It didn't even power on.
I sent these back to replace them with tags that included strings on them.  My mistake.  They are sturdy and a good size for my craft business

With only one review of 5 stars I thought it would be a good way to file and organize my papers. Wrong! The folder itself is like an accordion but it does not stay open to get the papers out and in. The inner file folders in various colors is a good thing except that they are extremely thin. I tried it to see if I could make it usable but there’s no way I could get more than a few papers in one slot. The description says it’s a ‘large capacity’ organizer but it isn’t. It’s also said that it can be used with ‘legal’ size papers - this is something I can’t imagine unless you actually fold the document in half. I only received it yesterday and it’s going right back - there are much better ones to choose from and that have a decent price as well.

Yes, the Avery labels print to edge, but only if you successfully align the sheets in your printer and have adjusted the template document you are printing.  I used the Avery on line program and had a lot of difficulty getting it right, but fortunately, printed on plain paper prior to inserting the label sheets.  The rustic Kraft Brown paper is good for a number of purposes, is too plain for others.  the scalloped oval shape is similar to others I've used but they look totally different than those.  These are not removable labels, so to speak, so once you apply them, that's where they will stay.

I'm amazed that cardboard boxes can cost so much, but I've found that when I want to protect the goods I'm storing or moving, I'm constantly impressed by SmoothMove products.  The [[ASIN:B007XPBW3E Bankers Box SmoothMove Picture/Mirror Box, Medium, 37 x 4 x 27 Inches, 4 Pack (7711201)]] is identified as containing fragile items, so if others are moving your things, they hopefully will treat your pictures or mirrors with special care.  I used a sturdy tape when constructing the boxes and think that the cardboard will hold up to more than one move after being refolded.  I retained the shipping box because that's also usable for other things although it's not adjustable like the four specially picture/mirror boxes.  Love SmoothMove.
I checked the specs and it claimed that it fit my printer. Well, it didn't. I even took it to Office Depot and the guy said that it says it fit, but clearly was wrong

To my surprise, we are using this Five Star very tall stand that is designed to be used in a school locker since it was retired from the school lockers which are on a hiatus.  It is sturdy enough to stand on its own but will not hold as much weight as when it is reinforced by the sides of a locker.  The plastic drawer slides smoothly, stopping at the rear and front so it can't be pulled out of the side rails unless you want it removed.  It looks just like the photo provided and can be folded flat if not being used, allowing for storage ease.

I thought this was lomger.
Its even shorter than my badge so u cant see the 'nursing student' sticking out at the bottom.
Utterly useless. And shipping took forever.
I was looking for an autograph pen, but this has a bit too wide a line.

The [[ASIN:B004MTDFHA Parker IM Black Gold Trim Fountain Pen, Medium Nib - 1760800]] is  a nice looking Parker pen which arrives in a soft box containing a single cartridge and warranty.  If there's a problem with the pen,  Parker requires a copy of your sales order because Amazon does not stamp the date of purchase on the warranty.  With this pen, use of the warranty is likely.  I'd read reviews abound with complaints regarding the faulty ink flow to the nib and the one I received is awful.  I'd also purchased an ink converter, so I know it's not the cartridge, as I've tried about a half dozen different inks.  With each, even if I leave the pen for an hour, it dries and I must either prime the converter or dip the nib into water or ink.  I was embarrassed at a meeting when the pen would not write and  I needed to sign a document.  Visually, this pen is five star.  In the hand, it's also five star.  If it worked properly, it would be a total five star value.  Unfortunately, a pen that doesn't write is worthless unless you've purchased it for another purpose.  I purchase pens to use as a tool for transporting ink to paper, so this one is a great disappointment.  I'd had a very old Parker fountain pen stolen inside of a bag and really wanted another, but this one will not replace the great pen I'd had for about forty years.  I cannot recommend this pen, particularly since there are so many really good fountain pens available currently.

I've been using the 3M Paper Cutter for about a week now and I'm very pleased with the purchase.  It stands nicely in a pen cup which makes for the easiest storage and reduced chance of misplacing the tool.  Although it sometimes takes a little work to get it started, for the most part, I have just begun with a slight push or tug and it's moved right along, slicing open the envelope, bag, package tape, or print article.  It does not work for anything heftier than perhaps a construction paper weight, but that seems obvious because of the lightweight nature of the plastic handle and hidden cutter.  I don't know how long this will last, but for now, it fulfills it's purpose.  It is not a pair of scissors, knife or box cutter, but for lightweight applications, it's better than satisfactory. As of today, Avery.com/print offers 111 templates which can be used for these dissolvable labels, but you can simply write on them with a pencil or pen.  I like to print my labels, particularly if I'm presenting a canned good as a gift.  Like most other Avery labels, these have a code printed on the package, but there is no clear date of manufacturing, so you would have to contact Avery to determine their coding.  I don't expect to need that information because I'm using these labels right away.  Before I applied one onto a jar, I felt compelled to test what is meant by "dissolvable."  I removed one corner from one of the five sheets of labels and stuck it on a jar.  I then ran the jar under cool water and to my surprise and delight, the label really and truly dissolved underneath my fingers.  It washed away without the use of a sponge, soap or even warm water.  It felt sort of slimy and was then gone!  Now these labels look like none other I've used.  They are not a true bright white, but they are whitish.  Printing on these is exactly like my other Avery labels and because they are fresh, the printing was clear and remained clear.  I've experienced printing that just did not take on old labels, so I am careful about using fresh labels now.  If you do not need to use FDA approved adhesive safe for indirect food contact and labels which dissolve in water, you do not need these labels.  They are not cheap and they will dissolve if you put them on an envelope and they encounter rain in their delivery to the recipient.  These are perfect for canning or food storage purposes and that's exactly what I'm doing with this package of 90.  They were made in Mexico, south of the border.

I bought this directly from the manufacturer to control mites and aphids on my Red Lake Currant Bush and my Akane Apple tree. Although I am religious about pruning them in the winter and doing at least 3 dormant oil sprays until late February, I can't seem to control the rapid proliferation of these pests come spring. Part of the problem is that you must simultaneously destroy the ants as well as they have a symbiotic relationship with aphids, doing everything possible to ensure aphid survival so they can feed off aphid "milk".

I can't say I'm fond of this product or that it's effective. Perhaps if it weren't so difficult to apply I'd have better success. But one must use 3 cups (yes, you read that correctly) per gallon of water which means wearing a mask to put in the sprayer which then clogs up. Perhaps an agitator would help? And it does no good to just cover the plant; one must hand turn each leaf to get the UNDER side where the beasties dwell or it's useless.

It's super messy too. I manage to get it all over me, the windows, the walkways, etc. And as soon as it rains or you water, you must re-apply. I've had better luck either handpicking the infested leaves and destroying them or spraying with a forceful stream of water. Less messy too. So not sure what to do with the other 20+ pounds of it I have.

Pros: They work, if you cut the shell off.
Cons: A pain to use, not actually environmentally friendly, kills plants if you do it wrong.

I used these for all my starts last year. I was trying to move away from peat, because it's a non-renewable resource. So of course it turns out these are wrapped in plastic. :-(

The outer fabric shell, which is usually paper that can be buried once the plant is started, is a tough plasticky material, probably a polyester. It looks and feels like paper, but all my plants were fine up to a point, then sad 2 months later, then dead. When I turned up the soil, the little white shells were completely intact, preventing any roots from growing. They decayed into the soil exactly none.

You have to cut off the shell, and when you do the medium crumbles, so you're basically holding a bare plant. They expanded okay and had an acceptable germination rate, hence the 2 stars, but they're (1) a hassle, (2) don't work the way others do, and (3) plastic. (Or something similarly not biodegradable, at least not quickly.)

Initially I was very impressed by the compact design and features of this CTECHi 320Wh LiFeP04 portable power station. There are many things to like about it. However, the power station I received in my order is no longer charging properly after a little over 2 months of usage from the time I received it in the mail. It doesn't charge or show any sign of charging when I use any type C cable in the PD port. I've used several different type C cables that I know work properly and tried different power sources. The power station shows a charging indicator when I plug in the AC adapter, but it doesn't actually charge since the battery charging indication doesn't progress past 1 bar like it normally would. After 4+ hours of being plugged into the AC adapter this power station shows an empty battery indicator when I attempted to power it on multiple times. This power station has never been dropped or had any major physical damage. The ports on the power station look normal to me with no sign of damage. As stated earlier I've only been using this power station for a little over 2 months and it is well within the 24 month warranty policy from the date of purchase. The power station comes with a 12 month warranty from CTECHi, but CTECHi offers a "free one year warranty extension program" as described in the paperwork that came with the item. You do need to contact CTECHi to get the one year warranty extension activated. I've contacted CTECHi regarding this power station's charging issues and will update this review later.

***UPDATES:
8/10/22 - I contacted CTECHi through Amazon messages regarding the power station's charging issues.
8/11/22 - CTECHi replied offering to replace the power station with a new one and requested more details. I provided the requested information and now waiting for the replacement to be shipped to me. I received a tracking number for the shipment, but the package has not been received by FedEx yet.
8/12/22 - CTECHi sent me a message via Amazon apologizing for the inconvenience and informed me that I should receive the replacement in the mail on 8/14/22.
8/14/22 - Received the package in the mail via Fedex Ground.
8/15/22 - I messaged CTECHi asking if this new replacement power station will have the 24 month warranty coverage. I received a reply back within an hour stating yes, the 24 month warranty starts from the date I received this replacement in the mail.

I'm impressed by CTECHi's customer service and fast response to my questions and concerns. The warranty replacement was an easy process and I received my new replacement within 4 days from the date I initially contacted them regarding my original power station's charging issues. It's great that they shipped me a new unit with all of the accessories in the box.

I'm still disappointed the CTECHi 320Wh portable power station I originally received in my order only lasted a little over 2 months. Hopefully this new replacement will last 10 years, which is the advertised product life on the Amazon product listing page. Time will tell. I'll report back several months from now and let everyone know how the power station is doing.

I've changed my original review rating of 1 star to 4 stars due to CTECHi's great customer service, their easy warranty process, they sent me a new replacement in box with all the accessories, and their willingness to take care of their customer in a timely manner.

Please DO NOT mistake these for the old ORTHO brand white plastic, catch & contain mouse trap that they are fashioned after. Those ORTHO traps worked fantastically but they are no longer made so I tried these. Big mistake!
I loaded the traps up with my favorite bait, peanut butter (worked great in the past). Set them along walls where the mice would run. And then watched them do absolutely nothing. No evidence that a mouse ever made any attempt to enter them. When I looked at the size of hole the mouse had to want to go into I realized that our local field mice would find it a tight fit, unless it was a young mouse.
Luckily my 4-pawed mouse catcher, Aspen, can still do a great job at 10 years of age. She went right to work neutralizing those lost field mice.
I won't be buying these again, or any other Tomcat product. They tried to copy something, but could not deliver a good product. They didn't pay attention to details that were important. I wish ORTHO still made those traps so I could get more but for now I will stock up on cat treats (for a job well done last night) and stick with Aspen (sorry, she is not for hire).

I'll start out with the what I like about this Dexpole 100W portable foldable solar panel.
What I liked:
- This solar panel looks nice.
- The handle bar is comfortable to hold and feels solid.
- I like the bi-fold design.
- It comes with a lot of adapters.
- The cables are a good length.
- The solar charger controller has 4 charging ports.

What I didn't like:
- After several tests on different sunny days with clear skies the maximum watts I saw using this solar panel was 55 watts. Average watts I saw was mid 40's on a very clear, sunny day in the afternoon hours. I tested the solar panel using two portable power stations. I tested this solar panel along with two other 100W solar panels and the other two solar panels consistently showed readings of high 70's to low 80's Watt compared to this panel's mid 40's to low 50's Watts readings.
- The shiny reflective clasps holding the solar panel together gets easily scratched.
- This solar panel's support legs are not adjustable. Other solar panels I compared it to have adjustable length straps on the support legs. It would be nice if this one had them.
- During testing this solar panel fell over on days where it was windy.

I requested an exchange for another one from Amazon because I thought the low wattage readings may be due to a defective solar panel. Unfortunately, the replacement Dexpole solar panel I received in the mail got very similar/ comparable results to the original Dexpole solar panel. The price was very reasonable when I bought it on sale, but the solar charging results were disappointing compared to other 100W solar panels.

Please, please, please use something else, folks!  This stuff is EXTREMELY toxic to honeybees.  Only one bee has to get it on its feet, take it back to the hive, and the whole hive dies.  There are plenty of alternatives around that are better for you, the earth, and your pocketbook, such as: insecticidal soaps for aphids, boric acid and sugar for ant control, vinegar, Diatomaceous Earth, Spinosad, Bacillus thuringiensis, etc.  PLEASE do your research!  The honeybees are disappearing at alarming rates due to us humans, including introducing the Varroa Mite and our overuse of pesticides.  Honestly folks, you don't have to bring out the nukes just because you find a couple of ants or aphids in your yard.

I wanted to do a quick update on this fish tank. I have the 5 gallon tank. As I started to purchase plants and decorative items for the tank I was purchasing them based on the dimensions that are given on here to my surprise the tank is not as large as they say that it is and it does not hold 5 gallons of water even without rock in there. The actual dimensions of the tank portion without the lid and without vase are 10 inches high X 13 1/2 inches wide X 6.5” sides without bow, going to 10” at the very front of the bow. Another thing that I found very helpful was reading through all of the questions and comments and finding one that told me how to quiet this filter down. They do not tell you that you have to fill the water all the way to the base of the filter which completely submerses it. Even then it does still make a slight grinding noise but much better having added the extra half inch of water to it that I was lacking. I do still wish that it had been the correct size that they say which is 15 x 15 1/2 x 10 essentially I would love to have been able to put a few things or a little bit taller in the tank. Just so that you know the highest that you really should put in there is about 9 to 9 1/2 inches anything else than that will be above the water line. And one last thing for me anyways the where the water flows onto this flat filter only about half of the filter actually gets used, And my tank is level. Overall I guess they just really need a better filter. I thought this tank because I like the way that it looks, however I am wishing now that I had bought one of the panoramic, tall round, or the 6 1/2 gallon tank or even go out to a 10 gallon tank, so that I could’ve had a few more fish because I’m not one to just have a single Beita in a 5 gallon tank or in this case it’s actually about 4 gallons even without rock.

I purchased these to replace the regular cap looking can lids that we kept losing in drawers around the house. I liked the paw print design because it easily marks the item as for doggy only, so the lids wouldn't be reused for people food.

These definitely work as designed. There are two grooves around the edge so that you can use three different can sizes with these lids. This is helpful if you use different types of dog food and the can sizes vary. We don't, so this feature is a little annoying because it makes it *slightly* harder to put the lid on. You have to align the grooves to snap the lid on and it takes just a bit more focus than popping a regular can cover on. This is a little nit and doesn't really take away from the product.

My other very small nit is the paw print shape. I purchased these for the paw print shape and wish I had purchased a regular circular shape because the paw takes up more room in an already crammed fridge. It's only slightly bigger, but every inch counts! Again, this is a very small complaint.

Our set came with a dog food spoon and we love the spoon! It's really long, so you can dig the food out of the can and has the heart shape to make scraping and mixing dry food and wet food easier. This spoon was something we didn't know we needed and now can't live without.

Overall, I think this is a good product and gets the job done. It's especially great if you use multiple can sizes.

I decided to replace my soon expiring Nod32 after reading many positive comparisons and reviews about Bitdefender. The install went ok until I opened my mail program. I had one complete folder deleted! I was not given the option of deciding what the product would do with it. After reading the somewhat disappointing and limited advise from the bitdefender site, I was able to find out where my mail was deleted to and retrieve it. Now everything should be great, right!? Nope! The program continuously crashes, slows down the computer (CPU + 80%), needs to "restart the computer" for the software to start, etc, etc, etc. The program may be good at stopping viruses, but it definitely slows things down! Nod32 never gave me this much of a problem, I even had a different firewall software and anti spam software with very little incompatibility. Trying to find answers to the problem on the bitdefender website has been frustrating to say the least. It appears that to get a computer to run somewhat adequately is to limit bitdefender to antvirus and firewall and not allow it to do antiphishing, privacy control etc. But, you are still left with the intermittent slowdowns and "restart" issues. On the bitdefender website a customer was complaining of the same issues and was told to return the product to the place of purchase! Give me a break, I know of no store that will give you a refund on opened software! Why can,t bitdefender do so themselves? Less rhetoric and better customer service would go a long way to solving these issues.

I have been in the market for a recipe and meal plannig software to organize my enormous recipe collection, to create a monthly menu for my family, and to automatically generate grocery lists from my menu plans. After a lot of research, I found Home Cookin on Amazon and was impressed by the consistant great reviews. I went online and downloaded the 10 day free trial. My impression of this software is that it doesn't really live up to the reviews that have been posted here. I will not spend $40.00 on this and am glad there is a trial version that helped me to make an informed decision without having to purchase first. Considering that the reviews are so consistantly high, I was surprised to find that this software is not very impressive. Here is what I found:

On the positive side:

1. There is a 10 day free trial that can be downloaded online, so to test out the claims is easy and costs nothing. I highly reccommend that anyone looking for cooking sofware, should download the free version before purchasing. What people need from this kind of software is highly individualized and what works for some folks is not very useful to others. ALMOST ALL RECIPE MANAGEMENT PROGRAMS HAVE SOME KIND OF FREE TRIAL, AND IT IS WORTHWHILE TO LOOK AT SEVERAL OF THEM BEFORE PURCHASING.

2. It is basic and simple. Actually, it is so basic and simple that it doesn't function much better than my current system of keeping my recipes and meal plans in my database and word processor.

3. The customer support of this company is EXCELLENT. They are reliably there for you and will answer any and all questions within 12-24 hours. This is far and away better than the vast majority of software publishers. The customer support is absolutely the best feature of this software.

On the negative side:

1. This software does not automaticly generate a grocery list. You have to manually enter your grocery lists, which is what I am already doing. And the grocery list function doesn't really make sense, as it tries to do things that it cannot really do. For example, it tries to price everything but really doesn't do it well and so the software is bloated with a function that is kind of pointless. Also, when you enter an item in the grocery list, it has you also enter the store and the section, as if it is going to generate an ORGANIZED list. But I could never figure out how to make that happen, because my lists always ended up as a mishmash of items that had addendums about which store and which aisle. Since creating organized grocery lists from my menu plans was a number one priority for me, THIS WAS A DEAL BREAKER. My current system is fine for my needs and the only improvement is that I need a grocery list generator.

2. The look and feel of the software is kind of dull. It might not matter to some people and doesn't affect the function, but I like a colorful display.

3. It is an incredibly simple format, and doesn't give much more advantage than my current system of recipe management where I manually generate recipes, menus and grocery lists using a database, spreadsheet and word processor. I understand that some folks like the simplicity, but why should I pay for this when I already own Microsoft Office Suite? Or you can get Open Office for free.

4. It comes pre-loaded with recipes that I donn't want, and these recipes have some kind of connection to the grocery list function that I could never undo. Consequently, my grocery lists keep showing up with items from the pre-loaded recipes and I don't know how to get rid of them.

5. The customer service, while excellent, is email only. There is no phone number, so you cannot get immediate help. That said, it is excellent in all other respects, and even though you cannot get immediate service, you can reliably expect a reply within 24 - 48 hours. This is kind of cumbersome, but still better than most companies.

All in all, I was surprised that this software wasn't more useful, considering all the great reviews. It provoked me to go back and look at the profiles of the reviewers and I noticed that most of the folks who posted reviews on this product had ONLY ONE REVIEW (making me wonder if they had created an Amazon account for the express purpose of giving this product a good review). Others had posted several reviews but all of them were positive - no matter what the product was (making me wonder if they are professional reviewers who have been paid for their good reviews). Both of these scenarios can sometimes indicate paid reviewers, and while I cannot say absolutely whether the reviews here are genuine or not, many of them are kind of suspicious in my eyes. A few seemed genuine, but most of them caused me to raise an eyebrow. In any case, I was disappointed in the overall functionality of Home Cookin, and will continue to look for something that fits my needs better than this.

Although I am computer literate (VMS, Windows), Unix and Linux are new environments for me.  The default installation from Suse boots quite slowly, and a newbie would have some difficulty improving that.  However, consistent software installation is facilitated very admirably via the YaSt tool.  Although the useful documentation seems mainly to be on line (who would not prefer print?), it is sufficiently automated (via KDE desktop tools) and sufficiently documented to introduce any new person in a kind and gentle way.  The distribution is extremely thorough, and it includes a very large quantity of very high quality software.  It seems clear to me that GNU is the path to computing enlightenment, and SuSe is a very good way to get on that path.  There seems to be no effective limit to the utility of the system you receive.  All congratulations and thanks to the GNU people and also to the SuSe people.

I really wanted to love this backpack.
I don't even like it let alone love it. A coworker has it and loves it. I'm not a backpacker but even if I were I'm not sure I'd like this style. I think it would be too tall and without structure I'd spend half my hike digging things out. I wanted something pretty to carry to work after my workhorse gave up so in I went. This pack has a pear shape and is tall. Without structure I found it difficult to put things in any one of the many pockets even with my lunch bag as an anchor. The bag just flops over. I gave up and grabbed a reusable grocery bag to carry things today. The slick material slipped right off my shoulder when I trialed that. I get it because it's waterproof. I don't carry with one shoulder but in case you do this doesn't work well that way. If you like non structured bags this would be a good deal. It just doesn't work for me. 3 stars because even though it terrible for me it seems to be a decent bag. And pretty.

I'll admit, when my boyfriend's brother first brought over P90X, I was like, "oh here we go, another 'as seen on TV' product that is going to suck." And then we started following the program. To say it is an amazing, one-of-a-kind system is to underestimate the true power of P90X.

There are several different workouts in P90X to help you train every part of your body. Shoulders & Arms, Cardio X, Yoga X, Chest and Back, Back & Biceps...so many different exercises, you'll eventually find your favorite and look forward to doing it every week. There are also three different levels of exercise. You can do the "lean" program if you're mainly looking to drop weight. Or you can do the "classic" program which is a good place to start if you are already fit and want to get even more fit. Then there is "doubles" which is as extreme as P90X will get and is for the more serious (and seriously fit). The doubles program consists of two exercises most days of the week so make sure you eat plenty of food to have the energy necessary to truly bring it!

I have always had a strong determination and Tony Horton, your coach throughout P90X, fosters that determination and that drive. If you have always wanted a better body, to lose weight, to be healthier or even just to maintain, Tony will help you get there. He knows exactly what to say and when to say it. He knows how to push you, even through the barrier of your TV screen. He gives you tips to modify your exercises so that you don't always have to keep up with the team on your screen. And the best part is there are women like me in the programs where a woman would typically think "oh, this is more of a male, body-building exercise".

If you're having a particularly weaker day, whether you feel like you're coming down with something or if you're a woman who has her period and you know you still need to work out but you just don't feel up to it that day, he gives you options and variations so that you can still work out, even if it's less than your maximum potential and feel excellent that you actually did something instead of flaking out that day. You watch the people in the programs sweat and struggle right along with you. They keep it real and that is a refreshing change.

90 days seems like a lot and at times, it definitely feels like a lot. But Tony is so motivational (and at times, quite humorous!) that you want to keep pushing play every day. On the days where you can "bring it", you feel excellent that you did, in fact, "bring it". On the days where you're a little lighter on your feet, he makes it alright, you don't have to feel as guilty--as long as you're moving, your form doesn't always have to be perfect.

It might take you a few go-rounds to complete the program. Sometimes life gets in the way. Sometimes your own mindset does. Either way, you can write down your numbers and study how you did and next time, you'll know what to match or exceed. You can even use bands as a substitute for weights or pull-ups. (I'm almost through the lean program and I can only do 1 pull up, which is still a nice change from not being able to even lift up my own body when I started.)

P90X also offers nutritional supplements, such as the tasty protein bars, (you don't even feel like you're eating healthy!), or the recovery drink which I personally can't drink because it repeats on me but my boyfriend and his brother love the effects they feel after drinking it.

I am not a Beachbody coach and I don't really want to be, to be honest. But I do want to let people out there know that if you have been considering a workout program and if you have the determination, then every dollar you invest in P90X is worth it. I thought I would never have a better body and kind of accepted my fate. But P90X has proven to me that if I channel my determination into exercise, anything is possible! And I have even started eating healthier too which I swore I didn't really want to do either--I liked my Pop Tarts and my Burger King. Now I'm eating organic cookies! And loving them! Once you change one thing about yourself, you feel great and want to change everything!

Seriously, this is an excellent product. Try it out. And if you're not satisfied, they will refund you anyway. But I can't imagine why you wouldn't be. Never have we sunk so much money into a company. And never before have we felt that company deserved it as much as Beachbody does. And I never write this much about anything unless I'm passionate about it--it's true, check my other reviews. ;)

P90X is extreme. If you're extreme (or if you someday want to be) then bring it! Do your best and forget the rest!!!

I bought this heart monitor to do some target training with my trainer at the gym. My heart rate was going too high and leaving the aerobic zone too often. I wanted a monitor that would be easy to use and that would synch with the machines at the gym so I could see my heart rate without looking at my hand every few seconds. This one works well. It sometimes takes a few minutes to find my heartbeat (if my heartbeat is resting when I put it on). But once my heart rate increases, or if I get near a treadmill that is built to synch with monitors, it seems to pick it up all of the sudden and then it stays strong (and accurate) the rest of the work out. I have tried turning it off and on and comparing it against the tread mill's monitor, and it seems to be always within a couple of bpm.

The chest strap is very comfortable. I don't even feel it on. And the part that goes against your skin is low profile so it is barely visible under my work out bra. The band also has nearly infinite adjustments so unlike nearly every watch in the world, it actually fits my small wrists! I can wear my husband's, too, and his fits me so if you have bigger wrists it shouldn't be a problem.

I do think the controls could be a bit more intuitive. You have to push a bunch of buttons to figure out how to do some things. But after a few uses you get used to it. I haven't used any of the special functions--I've only used it to see my heart rate, so I can't comment on those. But just for keeping track of how fast my heart is beating during a workout, it does great, and I've been able to lower my heart rate while running over the past few weeks of use.

I purchased this model/version because my Charge 4 stopped working after only 2 years of use. The Inspire 2 is a smaller display and, depending on the templates you download on the app, can have more details showing on the home screen. There are a few things that I would complain about but are not major issues: first, there are 2 touch-sensitive nodes on the sides that seem to go off when my wrists are bent back and this happens very often which makes me think the notifications have gone off; the second is that I can’t adjust the vibration intensity on the watch so I miss phone calls and messages many times.

This is still a great buy if you only need the basics. All the features for heart rate, steps, and (this one shows) activity time are what you’re looking for when buying a watch like this.

Fitbit Charge 4 vs Inspire 2: What's the difference?
The Inspire 2 has a plastic body so it's a little cheaper in its appearance compared to the Charge 4. It also has a smaller touchscreen display than the Charge and it has a physical button on the side, making for a less streamlined design than the other two models.

Ultimately, it’s lightweight, compact, and doesn’t use up excessive power like other smartwatches on the market. It also charges pretty fast (20-30 minutes for a full charge) and it takes about 4-5 days to run out of power (2 days for smartwatches). I would definitely recommend that someone to buy it for fitness purposes.

Overall, an economical watch that does the job.

Cons: Fitbits stop working after a couple of years for no reason. This is my 4th one in the last 6 years.

I purchased this unit mainly for the continuous heart rate recording. It seems to do that very well.

The most amazing thing about this Charge HR is the quality of the software - both the user interface and the signal processing. It's impressive that it figures out when I'm "outdoor cycling" and that it identifies a racquetball game as "Sport".

It's not so great that it isn't waterproof and that the battery lasts only about a day with all features activated. As clever as the makers of this device are, how difficult would it be for them to build a waterproof version with batter battery life?

The unit won't sync without an internet connection for your linked device, as far as I can tell. This can be important if you want to change a setting on the device and are not in an internet coverage area. Fitbit talks to your phone via Bluetooth, and you would think that would be enough, but sync won't happen until your linked device is connected to the internet.

On balance, I'm blown away with what this little Fitbit Charge HR can do and wouldn't want to give it up!

There were 1) stickers to put on a picture of a barn; 2) a red gift box that they called a barn and suggested you save some stickers for it too; 3) noses to glue to Popsicle sticks; 4) pieces of felt to practice lacing together and 5) felt carrots with holes and tops with numbers to match the number of holes to the Arabic numeral.  There was also a bonus wooden frame that a kid could paint (the nicest part of the box).  Finally, there was an insidious sticker chart, with a 'badge' to encourage the parent to not cancel the box.  The included guide just suggested questions like why are farms important?  It didn't seem to me there was any science in it, ad the quality felt disappointing.  I think it is hard to do a box like this for pre-schoolers; it looks like the grade school box is of higher quality, so maybe we'll try that.

January 2020's box was great. Then, like the rest of the year, Therabox dove downhill and shows no signs of getting better. The products are getting more and more dubious; the first warning sign was a cheap, ugly shower cap from Sugar Bear as well as Sugar Bear """vitamins.""" Uncomfortable socks. July's box, the one that made me quit, had a cheap, round blanket that could be charitably described as canvasy and fairly useless for humans. A card of tiny earrings felt more fitting for a very young kid. There was also a travel journal thrown in; the curator defends this tonedeaf choice in her letter, though doesn't give an excuse for why it feels like a dollar store journal. Nor why anyone thought a book with twee picture checklists would replace travel apps in this day and age.

In fairness, I did find some great skincare items in Therabox among the playing cards with cringey ice breakers and hair scrubs. There's also a nice, unconventional perfume that is now in my rotation. If you love masks, you'll probably find some new favorites in the box. The candles tended to be decent.

Finally, be aware if you have certain health issues, you won't be able to use some of the products. A foot peel from a previous box warned against using the product if you had a number of conditions, for example.

Update: My son is obsessed with shimajiro and anything tiger related now, that I had to subscribe back again. I am still yet to get response on my questions but I feel this box has helped the kid to communicate more .. thanks for that..

My husband and I have been married 19 years. We're both introverts but we enjoy laughing together. I'm a top-of-the-ladder professional and my husband is a "guys guy" type who wears steel toed boots to work. We firmly believe the science that doing something novel together builds our relationship and we're willing to try new things. But unless you have a lot of time and money and a reliable babysitter, it's hard to find novel activities. The point of the date night is each other.

The latest date was Hippie themed. We learned to tie dye bandanas, played a card game that had us answering questions to understand each other better, did face paint on each other that symbolized questions we wanted the other person to answer (and took photos and washed it off because we do have jobs to get to in the morning), etc.

One box lasts us multiple nights because we're tired and we only have the stamina for one activity per night. Also we use the activities as jumping off points to actually connect with each other, not just check the box that we did the activity. We have fun together. And that takes time.

At first $40 felt like a lot but a lot of thought and planning goes into each box. Yes, you could buy an expensive board game for $40 and play it many times. But that's not really the point. The activities are all designed by a therapist to help us connect with each other. After 19 years and 2 kids, that's something we desperately needed. Some of the activities keep on giving,
the card game this month has replay value. One box is worth about 4 sessions of marriage counseling for us... and that's a steal at $40! (And I do love the sturdy little boxes and use them for storage when we're done).
Not worth the price.

I have just received my first box and was prepared to be somewhat disappointed after reading quite a few reviews...
The delivery took a long time and I was super excited to finally receive my first bag of goodies.
The bag itself is super cute but not of fantastic quality, the zipper is pretty flimsy.
I received 4 full sized products (the product description on amazon said 4-5 products per month) but unlike advertised only 3 of them were lip products - the accompanying card said the company decided to switch things up with a “Monthly Mixup” - not sure I’m super thrilled about that.
The first thing I noticed is that there was no description given for the products I received or the manufacturers of those products.
I actually had to google the items to determine which one wasn’t the lip item! Since I subscribed to try out new products and companies I’m unfamiliar with I’d very much appreciate some pointers along with the products!
Now to the products themselves:
1. cougar lipgloss in cocoa - the website promises 24hrs of matte lips in “the perfect nude brown” - not a color I’d have picked but I think I’ll be able to use it on occasion
2. Cargo gel lipstick in Sicily - a bright, bold red. It’s a pretty color but certainly not fit for everyday use for me. Again not a color I’d have bought for myself.
3. Appeal gel liner in deep taupe - it’s a dark brown rather than what I’d expect from a taupe and as a lipliner it will be useless to me. The company’s website claims it can be used both as lip liner or eye liner so I’ll try it as an eyeliner and might actually get some use out of it. The color works with neither the lipstick nor lipgloss. It would have been really nice had they been coordinated!
4. Bellapierre shimmer roll in a deep bronze color - google let me know that this is the non lip product and that it is a roll on highlighter. I do not use highlighter and even if i did I don’t think this would be the color for me... I might try it a shimmer layer over eyeshadow or lipstick and see whether that works.

I’ll try another month or two to get a better idea of whether this just wasn’t the box for me or whether none of the boxes are for me before I’ll decide whether or not to continue this subscription.
I expected to not only get colors or products I’d have bought for myself - that’s part of the fun with a non personalized subscription like this.
But if the selection is so far out of the neutral, should fit most types and styles spectrum every month then I’ll not keep collecting a growing stack of useless products.

Update with November box:
Again 4, not 5 products:
1) peony lip scrub - it’s a very small tube but on the company’s website that’s the actual size offered
2) lipmonthly liquid lipstick - I think, the packaging only states it’s 02 of whatever it is, looks like a nude enough color to maybe work for me
3) lord&berry lip crayon in a statement color I couldn’t find on their website - what I could find there however is, that the crayon apparently requires a particular sharpener that can be bought on their website
4) a single eyeshadow in a very adventurous color.
I could have forgiven this, after all I don’t expect colors handpicked for me BUT it is a single eyeshadow that’s meant to be clicked into a palette and is going to be awkward to use on its own since it literally has no edges to nicely hold on to and the lid slides off since it’s not meant to stay on once it’s been put into the palette.

So essentially 2 out of the 4 products received would require additional expenditure to be used as intended by the manufacturers.

I’ve decided to not wait for a 3rd box and have cancelled my subscription.

My daughter gave my “boy” a Bark Box and we were truly impressed with the contents.
These toys weren’t something that you see in the stores, they were extremely durable for my professional chewer. They were unusual and the dog loved them all. Two of the toys that were included had additional surprises for us. Once he had finally got a spot chewed and you could see under it, there was another toy underneath it. Again we were so surprised to see a tough rubber ball inside of these other layers. It has little nubs on the outside and a squeaky on the inside. Now that’s what I call a gift that kept giving. This company says its toys are "designed" locally. Know that while this may be true, if you search for made in USA this product will come up, but four of the five  toys said they were actually "Made in China." So you can buy the same cheap toys at any discount store for about half the price. One toy only was domestically made, and it was of soft and high quality. I cancelled this subscription.

I had high hopes for this crate, but what I received was: 1) a broken coffee mug (made in China); 2) a very cheap (and cheap-looking) brass necklace that maybe would be appropriate for a 10 year old; 3) a "toy" that appears to be made out of hard plastic (not rubber); 4) 3 scented products that I won't use because I don't like making my dog sit in cloying perfumed air; 5) two small bags of dog treat (which seem fine, but the retail price on the website is lower than the price listed as retail on the card provided in the crate); and a rope toy heart that was okay, but looked more like an oval after one tug game. I kept the treats, but everything else went into the garbage.

I was really disappointed. I subscribe to other crates, and while I wasn't expecting to be bowled over, I was also not expecting to have to throw away 90% of what I received. Also, I ordered a crate for a big dog, and the only thing I kept were the two bags of training-sized treats, so my dog gets nothing special and I get nothing special. Also, while I appreciate that the idea of the crate is that I am a female who owns a female dog, I thought there would be more creativity than just giving me junk that says "Dog Mom" on it.

For what I paid, I would have loved to have seen some larger treats (appropriate for a 60 pound dog), such as jerky strips or even a larger biscuit. Maybe a Valentine's themed collar and leash set or dog socks. I would have been thrilled with that.

The room spray was just a personal thing for me, so I am not going to fault that, but two cheap car fresheners (reminiscent of the cardboard trees) cheapened the crate. I would rather you not throw in "fillers" rather than think I will prefer quantity over quality.

$40 for THIS??? Seriously??? Who are they kidding? 3 of the items I can get at Target for around $15 and then get a $5 coupon back (a face mask, toner, face wipes- these items are so cheap!). A small candle- the $5 size, a “volcano” bracelet (which, I must admit, I was a bit excited about, at first, because I had looked into these in the past and had wanted one) that was so cheap it broke within a week, a lavender pillow spray/sheet mist, and some kind of roll on essential oil. Then there were 2 books both of which were a bit on the silly side. 1 was meant for a very specific audience- ones who travel around the world, frequently and like to journal their “adventures” and want to record all the clothes they pack for their trips (why?). The second one was a scratch off book. You’re supposed to scratch one bubble a day then you “have” to do what it says and it says ridiculous things- “light a candle”, “watch the sun rise”, “meditate”. Generic things that honestly you are either already doing or really have no interest in doing.
It’s a good idea “in theory” but it’s WAAAAAYYYYY overpriced!!! I may have kept the subscription going for $25/mo, but $40/mo is SUCH a ripoff. Plus, you end up with extra crap in your house that you absolutely don’t want or need.
I can say though, the packaging is lovely and it was fun to receive a “surprise” package in the mail. So...there’s that...

Some may complain that every product isn't something they might purchase. But overall it is a great deal when you look at the overall value. No everything isn't geared to everyone but if you pass on the items you can't use you have some happy friends.

We currently have 5 adult cats and 7 kittens (only 1 over 6 months of age) under the roof at the moment.

The replacement kitty wine leaked in the box during shipping. None of the catnip kitties liked the wine. The venison liver treats that were supposed to be included would have gone over well with the foster kittens and possibly some of the adults.

The wand toy is loved by the kittens and a couple of the adult cats. However, I can buy these types of wands for only $2 all year round.

I also get a email notification from them and I can either skip the copy or get a previous copy to sell as they sell for a lot compared with most record clubs. It’s more expensive, but they make the covers which are very thick and all of the pressings are exclusive and 180g pressings. Some are even 45rpm remastered. I know many people are going to say they don’t like VMP or it’s too expensive. But I like knowing what I am getting and have plenty of time to exchange it, or if I don’t want a duplicate or something else, I will get a month’s credit.

The one thing that Amazon has FINALLY made an improvement with is their packaging for vinyl. The old method was absolutely terrible and I never purchased vinyl from Amazon because of this issue. I recently purchased two Phish LP on LP copies and discovered that they finally updated their packaging procedure!

Almost every album I would order, would arrive with either bent edges or corners, or worse, large seam splits or both! I can’t say it’s the post office every time either. I returned a copy of Bob Mould - Workbook 25 because it had about 2 inches of the spine missing from poor packaging. Another issue I have had with Amazon and vinyl pressings is that in the past, I ordered two Mobile Fidelity Sound Labs pressings and they kept saying it wasn’t in stock. I finally bought the two pressings from Music Direct, the owner of the MoFi label now, and canceled the Amazon copies. I don’t know why they didn’t have them in stock as they where not exactly low numbered repressings like some of the newer ones. However Amazon charged me for the crappy mp3 that they sent, instead of removing them. I don’t do mp3 format! Yes that sounds like a snobbish remark, but it’s a crude format! It’s why I have owned 4 Astell&Kern players over the years and I buy downloads that are in 24Bit/192 kHz or DSD. MQA is an amazing sounding format as well and I own several MQA CDs that I have ordered from Japan! You need a computer and special DAC to unfold the true sound of these releases! I have some great downloads that are 24Bit /96kHz as well. Amazon still charged me $9.99 each for the two albums they could have gotten and it was for a service I don’t even use! I don’t understand why they didn’t have the copies in stock in the first place because everyone else online had them in stock!

My last complaint about buying vinyl pressings from Amazon is that third party sellers are not listing their used vinyl pressings in the correct place. Yes, I know this Vinyl of the Month subscription is different, but their used Vinyl and CDs is a complete mess! I have sent back several that were listed as a certain pressings only to get a different pressing! Some of these albums have upwards of 200+ pressings. Yet they let third party sellers list them just anywhere! I purchased a copy of Queen - The Game that was supposed to be a MFSL copy and NM. It had a photo of the MFSL album . I even sent an email to the seller saying that if it wasn’t an MFSL issue, don’t send it! Well I got a terrible US pressing that was dirty and had severe ring wear. I tried to contact the seller and he was gone with my $75.00.

I haven’t purchased any Vinyl or CDs or SACDs from the third party sellers in a long time. I just got feed up with buying items that where listed incorrectly or the sellers idea of NM is much different than what the average person would consider NM, even to the people who have no clue about grading vinyl.

I’m going to post a question before I decide on weather or not this is a Record of the Month club I would want to be part of or is it not something I would like. So far the titles listed are copies I already have a few different pressings of. I’m also not a huge fan of picture disc, as they sound terrible for the most part. I have two copies of Pink Floyd - The Wall, one is a Japanese pressing and the other is a MFSL pressing. I have 5 copies of The Clash - London Calling. A earlier Japanese pressing, a red unofficial version, a couple of U.K. pressings and I bought a Clear Vinyl 50th Anniversary release that I have not played or opened as it is worth over $450.00+ on Discogs. I got it for about $70.00.

I’m also a little bit put off by customers saying that they got a different copy for the month than others got. That never happens with VMP! So I will have to see what response I get to my question about these albums that are being sent out before I join another vinyl club!

I have an entertainment system I've been designing in my minivan that works using an Android head unit and game console with a switch routing between 2 screens (each can display one or share sources). Without going into too much detail here, it's a complex system and I haven't worked out all the bugs. I occasionally need to just reboot everything in the back when something goes wrong or gets frozen, which tends to happen on long road trips.
I was looking for something like this, a way to remotely turn off the system if something needs to be rebooted, but all the ones I found previously were designed for 120v AC outlets. I wanted just a DC remote shut off!
This product is perfect because it can be connected to the car's 12v wiring which supplies power to the rear equipment, and the remote switch can be mounted near the driver, and a secondary one in the back for the kids to do themselves if they need to and don't want to bother me while driving. So far it seems to work, I'm pretty happy with it. The only improvement I'd suggest is that if it doesn't have wire terminals built in, at least it should come with some quick connect splice boxes or something. I don't want to permanently solder it while testing it out, and wire nuts are cumbersome and unnecessary for such limited current. But as far as functionality goes, it does what it says so I'd give it 5 stars.

I was so excited about this Worx portable mini blower, as I have a Worx leaf blower and it works amazing. However, upon opening, I realized that it was only the tool itself included. I could have sworn when I ordered it, that it also included a power supply, however now it appears that the verbiage has changed to “tool only” upon receiving the item today.

I quickly looked online to find the power supply, however I realized that it requires a charger, a hub, and a battery. All of these separate items cost approximately (depending on battery power) from $120 to $160 extra on top of the mini blower cost itself. So if you wanted this particular 1 item, it would cost close to $200. My full size leaf blower by Worx cost much less ($59.99 retail) than this and is a lot more powerful.

Unfortunately I am not willing to spend an extra $120-$160 to get this little item to work, so I honestly cannot test it out.

Another thing to add- it’s truly not even portable, as you would still have to make sure the power supply is attached with the battery, so you will be constantly hauling around a “tail” with your blower. On the other hand, where the blower component is, it is completely separate from the handle and to where the power supply goes to - There is enough room for a 3.7 volt battery with a USB charge which would be sufficient for this size of an item. If this were done, it would be 100% portable, convenient, and more cost efficient. They would just have to add a speed control to the blower itself if this were done.

You can purchase a bundle with all of the tools and power supplies for cheaper. But if you purchase these a la carte, then it becomes very costly for just this 1 item.

I will just have to use my full sized leaf blower to get the small areas I was intending to use this on.

I can't vouch for the accuracy of the color here, but usually 5000-5500k is considered sunlight color. When I work with cameras and want to match the look of window light, that's usually the range I work in (compared to tungsten/yellow light, which is approximately 2700 kelvin). The bulb is plenty bright at 1200 lumens, but unless you're using it combined with natural sunlight I don't really like the color. It is way too cool of a color, especially if anything in your house is closer to the more traditional yellow / warm colored lights. See the attached picture of it in the same lamp as a 3000k LED bulb. It is very blue next to it, and feels jarringly intense as a result, despite actually putting out LESS light (the other bulb is a 1500 lumen LED).
All in all, I think I'd prefer 3000k colored lights that put out more light versus this very blue light at less lumens. But some people might like this, especially if they've already decided they like "full spectrum" daylight colored light and have changed all the bulbs in their house to this color range. It definitely seems to be what it says on the box, 6500k and 1200 lumens, so if you know what you are getting and know that's what you want, I say go for it! If you aren't sure, go with 3000k colored lights instead.
