But I used a couple of these to replace an ugly older fixture that came with the house and some "fixtures" after construction in our hallway that were basically a single bulb in the ceiling. At least this looks nicer than that, but mostly when off. I'm kind of disappointed in how the single bulb looks in a round fixture like this.
Description says they have an adjustable nose clip, but they don’t.

Great surge protector! The 3-foot cord is just the right length. It is easy to use, easy to turn off and on with your finger or your toe :) I recommend this to anyone who needs a good surge protector.

We just finished building a larger tiny house and put these bulbs in the two outside lights on either side of the door to keep bugs from congregating around the door. They work very well and they are very pretty to boot!!! Give a lovely yellow glow around the area at night.

This is an excellent way to add lights without running wires. I like that this is rechargeable so you don't need to replace batteries like other closet/counter lighting, and the motion sensor means you can have it only turn on when needed and stay off when you don't to prolong time between charges. The magnetic base holds on pretty well, and makes it easy to mount/remount, however the caveat to me is the buttons. There are two buttons, one marked like a power button and the other seems to have an icon of a light/sun. It makes it hard to understand which button does what without referring to the instructions. I have a similar model from another brand that uses a switch instead of the dual buttons here, and that switch seems much more intuitive to understand and use. That's my only caveat, otherwise a great product!
Keeps breaking and leak cold air in the house, don't buy it.
Too wide for my gutter.

I appreciate this innovative, curly, artsy thing with light. It is intensely bright to night-light-dim adjustable. Great! But it can be annoyingly in your eyes bright. This is the kind of light you'd put on a sideboard, living room end table, or by the back door where you want light for someone to walk through the room without bumping into something. Or set it on a guest bathroom cabinet to let folks know where the lou is. I can't read by it, and while watching TV it's okay, but a lamp that is higher up doesn't get in the eyes like this one does that sits at eye level. It definitely lights up a dark corner.

It is white. That makes it perfect to go with my white kitchen decor.

AND.. it's pretty. It's unique. It could start a conversation. And it is useful to put by the back door so when I come home the house is lit rather than dark. I like that a lot.

This filter has so much potential! Pros:

+ Water passes through it faster than any other filter I've used before
+ It looks cool!
+ It comes with a sweet carrying case

However there are two major cons that I think outweigh the good:

- THERE'S NO VENT! The thing is designed as though you should put it on top of the bottle you're filling and just leave it there, but since there is no channel for air to pass through, the flow slows to a trickle unless you hold it above the container to allow air to get in. A simple notch cut into the spout would solve this. I'm going to attempt to make my own.
- It's pretty awkward to hold, which is a problem because I always have to hold it to actually use it

I just replaced a whole box of socket wrench parts with this ONE! I love the way this works- place it over a bolt, for example, and it presses in on the rods inside to form a shape around the head, thus being a custom size fit on the fly. No longer needing to trial-and-error fit the right mm size to the bolt I'm trying to work with!
It works pretty well, however there's one caveat I've noticed: If you have multiple parts too close to each other, it might be harder to use than an exact size socket bit. I was trying to tighten a base plate for a child bicycle carrier, and I found it difficult to turn individual bolt heads next to one another since the head was too wide. It bumped into the ones next to it and make it hard to turn individual ones. But otherwise, for everything else I've truly appreciated having this "master wrench" one size for all!

I really like this lamp. It arrived as expected. Unfortunately, the bulb blew in a matter of hours, making it a more expensive and hassle-filled purchase than it should have been.  No reason to buy online if I end up having to go to the store to replaced the bulb. Some items that require specialty-sized bulbs include a spare in the package...this would have been a great idea.
Light is great but double side tape/magnets didn’t hold and the light fell and broke. Slightly disappointed
What luxury to have a lamp that has different light levels. It has a neat clamp you can attach the lamp to the desk so it doesn't move around and you can't knock it off the desk. Love it.

I got these intending to use them for all sorts of things, but when they arrived they are SO TINY they won't go around anything but maybe a small cable. So beware, they are not as big as they look and will only help if you want to secure very small bundles of things.

I wanted this to turn on and off a device I have installed in my crawl space, and it works perfectly for that purpose! The remote easily connects and disconnects the plug through walls and a decent amount of distance. I love that it also comes with a keychain remote, it's handy when I misplace the regular one, and I could see this being very useful on things like garage lights and the like.
The one caveat I have, is that the shape of the receiver plug is a bit too tall to allow anything to plug in next to it. In other words, this is fine as long as you don't have anything else plugged into the outlet you intend to use it with. Otherwise, you can get around this limitation with an extension cord or power strip adapter, etc. I'm happy with it for my purposes and recommend it.

The holder doesn't hold properly. This is not the cheap one on the market, so we expected better quality than what we received. We need to buy another holder to hold it properly. Very bad design. -- The two flow settings is great. Still thinking if we're returning it.

I used to borrow a neighbor's Little Giant brand ladder for things around and outside my home. I love the collapsible A-frame style because you can make it any height you need in between the min and max, and even use it on stairs to create a level climbing area as well. This is a very interesting and different take on that.
Rather than the large metal hooks which lock the extension in place, each rung here has dual plastic levers that lock in place as you expand. The advantage here is that it is REALLY easy to expand the ladder- just lift as the locks engage rung by rung. Stop when it is the correct height. The problem, however, is that you can't lock a leg in a shorter position- as soon as you lift it off the ground, gravity will attempt to pull it out further. In fact, when collapsing the ladder, you need to use the built in strap to hold it closed and prevent it from trying to open as you carry it away. I wish there was a way to lock the legs in place like the Little Giant and its variants.
Otherwise, it is very useful to have around!

I'm trying out a Nest, And the first thing that we noticed is that the old thermostat had a wider mounting plate, so the holes were still visible in the wall with the Nest installed. Also, since our walls are white The Nest looked a little bit out of place. I did not want to spackle and paint over the holes in case we decide to go back to the old one when the seasons change.
This cover helped alleviate both problems. It conveniently covered the holes, and gives a more decoratory boost around the Nest so that it blends better into the walls.
The only downside however, is that the pattern on it reminds me very much of a paper disposable plate. Interestingly, the material seems to be a soft TPU which you can squish with your fingers. This is probably helpful to seal out any air in the cavity behind the wall for insulation.

All in all I'm happy with it, however I wish it did not have the intricate pattern.

My 4 year old son loves the hat, but he hates it when I attach the screen. The visor of the hat makes it so the screen cannot come down all the way. I was hoping to use it as an extra precaution during this epidemic, so I was disappointed that the screen doesn't really work. Also it took a long time to ship.
Perfect for timing the flooring I'm putting in. Plenty of power and maneuverability. Glad I chose this one.
This is a nice outdoor light.  It is a great value for the money and so far it works beautifully!

I bought two of these to cover my large fenced compost bin last year and it does a wonderful job. It's been out there now for a year and still looks brand new. Blends in well with my wooded acreage. I just attached it with some of my stainless steel clothespin clips and had it up in a couple of minutes. I'm going to buy two more for another bin I am building.

This takes some getting used to.  In order to get the pressure you are looking for it actually has to be set 1.5-2psi higher than you want.  Once we figured that out it was ok.  Better than waiting at the gas station for air in our tires.

This item was my mother-in-law’s requested present from my husband last Christmas as she recently had a hand surgery. She said this was the best gift she ever received. It helps her a lot in doing the dishes.

Only good for small jobs. If you want a sprayer to paint anything large like a home look elsewhere. This is from our professional painter who was painting our home recently. It does not give out a goodly amount of paint as needed for big jobs.

Phillips makes some decent brand name household equipment, and these bulbs are no different. I like the way you can adjust them but it doesn't require wifi or an app or anything like that... It's a physical dial that you set and forget, and it has plenty of in between settings so you can literally dial in the color you need between tungsten and daylight. Well made, would recommend.
I'm old and forgetful. These remind me quickly to turn off my kitchen sink faucet if I walk away. Very loud!!!!!! Highly recommend.😁👍🏻

I put this in my motion detector spotlight​ in my back yard. It isn't as bright as the old one, but it does light up the place enough to make intruders run for cover. It is not hot to touch after it has been on a while. I think this is a good product. I'm glad I ordered it.
The mask is too tight. I am looking for a suitable recipient to gift them to.

This is good securing low grade items, such as a food pantry that I volunteer in. Using this chain and set of locks we are able to secure some cabinets and refrigerators that were being raided before food distributions. I've seen more industrial locks on the market, but for light duty use like lockers, panties, bikes, etc, this is enough to slow someone down enough to not be worth breaking in.
For the asking price, I think it is worth the money.

Personally I wasn't that impressed by the quality of this tiara. The necklace a and bracelet is okay quality, but the tiara simply looks cheap. It looks like it is made from glitter foam. For the $12 price tag this was listed at the time I wrote the review, I would have been disappointed with this purchase. I don't think I can recommend this set.

You can calibrate this thing all day, and you will if you're going to try and get an accurate reading. It's cheap, but you'll waste more money in calibration solution trying to get it right then just spending more up front on a better meter. The only reason I can think this has such high rating is people aren't rechecking them.

After continued use my opinion has changed, I leave it in a shotglass with the calibration solution and it seems it doesn't require recalibration as often. Maybe once or twice a week it will begin to read .1 too high and will need to be adjusted.

7/5/2014 You get what you pay for with this product, it costs about as much as a couple bottles of calibration solution. And you'll be using alot of solution, about 6 months ago I bought a BlueLabs Combo meter and I never looked back, yeah it's quite pricey but you get what you pay for, every 30 days it needs to be readjusted and it has a icon on the display to remind you, no dial, automatic calibration. The probe needs to be kept wet, their say is "if it dries it dies" and it has a cleaning kit to use on it every month, maybe that's the issue with the Etekcity, maybe if it was cleaned and stored in a KCL solution it would work better longer. I don't know but I love my BlueLabs Combo so far.

This mask is cute and funny. However it is very thin, so you’ll want to take advantage of the filter pocket in the mask and buy a filter. It’s also a bit to big for my 5yo boy, but fits my 9yo daughter perfect.
My son returned to school (middle schooler) and needed something to help him make it through the day, my dad loves with us so we have to be very careful
It smells like it belongs in a tool shop, not our home.  HEAVY, too.  Sent back.

These were bought as a replacement for the burnt original incandescent bulb. Now, with a benefit of a hindsight, it is clear that LEDs (or maybe most LEDs) cannot be used inside a microwave. Alas, this seller never indicated that and I started an additional research after they were, one by one, destroyed by the magnetron within a few seconds of starting the microwave. Actually, it looks like most sellers conveniently forgot to mention that fact. But, a few do have a warning that they can only be used outside of a microwave (like in those over-the-oven ones). Likely, as long as these are not used in a microwave, they should be fine. Alas, I cannot comment on the longevity of that usage.  Total lifetime of all 3 in my case was about 5 seconds :-)
Came chipped but looks natural Pretty glow!

I had a Moen system previously in this shower that was acting up. For a long time now I wasn't able to close the water, even after changing the cartridge, handle, and most of the system. Eventually it wouldn't turn on anymore, so I gave up and went the nuclear approach to try a total replacement off Amazon.
I had this installed a couple of days ago, and so far I really like it. Everything works smoothly and effortlessly, on and off, hot and cold, exactly as it should. And best of all, when I turn it off, it STAYS OFF, no dripping. Perfect.
I can't speak for longevity, that's the part that I'm nervous about but so far so good. It actually has some things the name brand Moen components did not, such as a rubber gasket around both the inner ring as well as the outer one (the Moen only had the outer rubber ring). That means hopefully it is less likely to get water into the mechanism or wall behind the tiles. I was surprised the name brand one did not have this.
I'll try to come back and update this review if I ever have issues with it.

I received this lamp as a Christmas gift and have it at my office.  The first power cord I received burnt out within a week of using the lamp. Customer Service got back to me quickly and replaced the cord within about a week. Now, less than two months later, the power supply burnt out again.
Not the easiest to install correctly, but look very nice after it put together

I should have ordered a large instead of a medium, and ruined one of the covers the first day when I tried to get my shoe into it. As I stretched the silicone, my finger poked right through. Not sure how long I could walk around in these before I wore out the bottom.

I wanted this for two reasons: First, as a frequent DIYer, I figured it would be good to have when handling things I don't want to get on my clothing, such as brake fluid, motor oil, paint, etc. Secondly, I thought it would be cool to use as a Breaking Bad inspired outfit for a costume event. Well, the good news is that it really works well for the former, which is admittedly what it is intended for. As for the costume, well, I'm usually an XL and despite this being a L/XL, it was little more snug around certain sections than I expected it to be. I'm not uploading a photo for this, but let's just say I looked more like a a stuffed banana than Walter White.
On a more serious note, if you are working with corrosive chemicals or other things that would require the use of an outfit like this, you probably won't care so much what you look like in it. So, 4 stars for being what would consider functional. Minus one star because it's not very forgiving on the XL side, despite saying it should fit in the title.

This flapper and universal fill valve needs lots of adjustments.  Toto is an expensive brand.  Why a universal FIX for so many products?  This product is half plastic and half rubber.  Maybe yours will not give you nightmares, but mine did.  Soon after installing, it would leak.  The tank fills water continuously.  That is because the seal is made of cheap stuff.

I got this because I wanted a more "rainfall" shower head, while still having the handheld option. In that, this  fits the bill.  It took longer than any other showerhead to install (in my experience) due to the weird slip nut that is the first thing instructed to install.  This piece ended up preventing me from being able to fully attach the actual diverter head to the arm. Once I removed it, I was able to install the diverter piece with no leakage. My shower arm is higher than standard, and it's not very long, so this head sits almost on the wall--so if you have a short arm, might consider an extender.  My handheld would not sit in the arm due to this lack of space, but that's fine with me as the previous owners installed a holder half way down the wall, and that's where mine will sit.  I have a little trouble switching the diverter as it's stiff (and very high, but the second part is not the fault of the manufacturer). I did remove the flow limiter, and I'm happy with the amount of water that comes through the larger head.  There's not quite as great a result when you use both, because you are splitting water, but I usually use the handheld for spot rinsing or cleaning the tub, so it won't need a lot of both going at the same time.  Overall, not a bad showerhead for the price, just wish it hadn't been so difficult to install and that it gave a little more room from the shower arm.

I have to say, I'm impressed. My old craftsman drill set could use a new battery, but this whole kit costs less than a single replacement battery! I wasn't expecting much for that price, especially since I've never heard of this brand before, but I figured it would be a good backup. Turns out, I actually REALLY like this one! It is plenty powerful, and light as well as comfortable to hold too!
I can't determine if it will hold up to abuse as well as my trusty brand name drill does, but this is absolutely a solid buy for the price. I like that there is an LED arrow indicator on the side that shows how much strain there is on the battery (so you can tell before it starts getting too low), and it comes with all the screw and drill bits you'd need to get going out of the box. I recently found myself using this instead of my Craftsman and didn't miss my old one at all. Definitely check this out for an inexpensive tool on a budget that will get the job done!
Looks pretty bad. The pictures are badly smudged and fuzzy looking. Edges are all messed up.

I don't understand all the negativity around this from other reviews. Chances are I don't have to say this if you're looking at an e39 mount bulb, but just in case it needs to be said: This is NOT for use in a standard light socket (usually referred to as an e26 Edison type). Yes, they sell adapters, and I've tried them for use with my video softbox to get more light output in a portable package. But I don't recommend them. I was actually hoping to do something like that here, but generally, E39 bulbs are larger and heavier, and assume you have the thicker mount to hold it securely. The adapters rely on the smaller diameter socket to hold the bigger one which not only could have ruined my light box as it started to bend, but could have fallen out and made a real mess. That being said, this bulb IS super bright and puts out the light equivalent of equipment that usually requires several times the amount of energy. The most common point of failure in LED bulbs, however, is heat dissipation since unlike a traditional tungsten, halogen or vapor bulb, there are integrated circuits here which can easily burn out at high temperatures. The bulb therefore has an active cooling system, with vents and a whirring fan that you can hear if you are too close to it (yet another reason it would not work as a video lamp, although perhaps for continuous light photography).
That concerns me regarding longevity because it means there are moving parts which can eventually wear out and stop spinning, unlike traditional passive cooling LED bulbs. Then again, a bulb with this many diodes would not be possible without active cooling, so that's the compromise I suppose.

For what it is, it is a good balance of supreme light output for commercial/industrial level use, while saving a ton on energy compared the 1,000watt bulb it claims to be equivalent to.

I love the corn-cob style bulbs like this for the amount of light you get, and unlike the smaller round bulbs, the light output is more evenly dispersed. However, there are a couple of caveats. I was excited to order this but it arrived broken with a corner smashed in and cracked, even though the shipping boxes were in intact. Once I started examining the broken part I noticed that this quality is very low.

This is a great smart thermostat that integrates with GE's Sync app and smart ecosystem. I'm kind of shocked at the other reviews claiming they were surprised it wasn't compatible with their older wiring. Much like almost any other modern Wifi-enabled thermostat, you need a common wire (C-wire) for a constant source of power in order to keep this on and connected. In other words, if you can't install a Nest or other smart thermostat, and instead have been stuck using with AA batteries that you need to change... this is going to need a wiring update! All of them do!
Thankfully, this is already standard and any house made in recent years will have it. Also, there are plenty of kits on the market to "add a C-wire" for those who need it. When the Nest came out, my old house did not support it, however when we moved, one of the things I wanted to make sure of was that our home had more updated HVAC wiring so that I could install a smart thermostat. If you've got the wiring, it is easy as anything to install! Just follow the diagram and keep track of your color-coded wires, and everything should work exactly as expected! I've seen GE's Sync system for light bulbs and smart plugs/switches, and they tend to be rated quite well so this is an interesting addition to their lineup!
Much larger than I expected! Beautiful!!

I bought this for travel - especially needed a good bottle opener with multi tools, and I LOVE Leatherman products. I have had many over the years so I was excited to receive this one.
I was very disappointed - the quality of Leatherman manufacturing has really deteriorated over the past 10 years - sure this CS-4 has a lot of 'gadgets, but it is very cheaply made - more like made in China than in Oregan, USA.
The main problem is with the SAW. You have to open the ANVIL first to release it. That's OK, but then it is impossible to put things back in place without the ANVIL jamming against the closed SAW.
After a lot of frustration, I gave up and sent it back to Amazon.

This is a great clamp on light for bedside reading use. I really like that it is USB powered because I already have a USB multiport charger behind my nightstand (for charging phone, tablet, headphones, etc...), and it was easier to run something off of an unused port on that rather that another item that plugs into the wall directly (the only other AC outlet accessible to my bedside currently has a clock).
It has multiple modes for color, which I like because I found one that sort of matches the 3000k colored light bulbs in use elsewhere in the house, and an inline switch to shut it off and on.
It's pretty perfect aside from 2 things:
The base it comes with to make it free-standing doesn't balance very well. It seems to want to fall over when I use that, so it works much better if you clamp it to something like a headboard or desk side. The other thing is I wish the switch were located on the line instead of on the cable. If I mount this to a desk or bed side, I want to hide the wire behind the furniture. As it is now, I have to fish the wire back out to hit the switch. Its a minor inconvenience, the light is otherwise wonderful and I'm enjoying it. For most people's usage, it will work exactly as expected.
This is perfect for my back patio. It gets really dark back there and this light makes me feel a lot safer. The light is bright and reaches into the corners of the yard. Great bang for the buck.
These turned extremely yellow and hideous looking in just a few weeks. Plus, the adhesive is very weak and I already lost one down the toilet.

We like to have parties outside on the deck for friends and family, and lights like these are awesome to have around. I've tried a few different similar panels, and I love that you can dial in specific colors to match a theme, or let it cycle on its own for a crazy dance vibe. There's no program control beyond the remote so you can't really sync it up with other lights or anything, but that's to be expected at this price point.
The one thing that disappointed me, however, was the brightness. Not that they aren't bright- they get PLENTY bright! But I have 100w LED flood lights that I've used before, and they get brighter than this. I think that because this is a color changing setup, the full power is split between varying RGB levels and you therefore never get the full power of a solid color 100w LED panel. If you get a single color LED at 100 watts, it will be noticeably brighter than this (like, fill my backyard with stadium light levels of bright). This is plenty of light to set the mood, but keep your lumens expectations realistic.
I wanted to use this light in my linen closet but the magnets won't hold it. I'm testing it now to see how well the light actually work before i decide to keep it or send it back.
The filters did not last as long as Zero Water filters and were contaminated much faster ( showing escalating numbers for water testing). Disappointed because Zero Water filters are more expensive.

These aren't working for me. I ordered them to hang around a full length mirror that is on the back of a bedroom door, in an area that has poor lighting. They were really easy to install and plenty sticky. And they don't as "cheesy" as I feared they might once they were up. But as far as lighting goes some how they're not very illuminating.... yet they're incredibly hard to look directly at. A lot like LED xmas lights.
What I mean by that is the light they give off doesn't reach very far. The light extends out maybe six inches, at best, before it starts evaporating. So while it lights up the area directly around the mirror, me standing in the outfit I want to look at not even two feet away I am barley lit up. Yet because they're LED lights, they're hard to look at. Being on the mirror itself, it's impossible not to squint when looking in their direction.
Now that's not to say these wouldn't be useful elsewhere around the house. I'm thinking they might still work well for for something like under cabinet lighting, for example. Somewhere you'd like a little extra glow.
I did like that the cord was more than long enough and I loved that you could change the lumens color. But they're just not going to work around a mirror for me.

I've used a few similar looking smart plugs in my home, but this is the best one I've gotten by far. You just can't beat an actual Amazon branded product for their ecosystem!
For starters, each outlet is individually controlled. I had one previously that was all-or-nothing, but this will allow you to either group or control each plugged in device individually. Nice.
Secondly, no third party app as a layer in between!
Every other smart plug or switch I've used required an app to be installed on my phone, then you can choose to have Alexa set up with the specific skill for it. Sometimes that app is kind of sketchy, and appears to be poorly translated and/or required to get from an untrusted source. With this, no third party is required- it connects and works directly within the Alexa app already and does not require anything else. It's like getting the name brand accessory for a product! It just works, and well at that!
Some people have pointed out that it doesn't work with other systems (Google home, Siri, etc), but that's sort of obvious, I'm not sure why someone would think an Amazon product designed for Alexa would be compatible with the other programs.
Highly recommended!

Our old "universal" kit came with 2 sizes of flapper so you could find one that fits. I assumed this would be the same, but it turns out to be 2 of the same size one. I have some PEX pipes that the previous owner of my home did some weird things to, so I wanted to cut out and redo some of it. This kit was great help, it has elbows and corners and straight connection parts, so I was able to mix-and-match to get all the crimped connections I needed. I had to borrow a crimping tool for it, but it was pretty easy to figure out and use for my first attempt. Everything is holding solid and strong, and most importantly, leak free!
The only downside in my opinion, is the strange number of items in the package. You get 10 of each type of fitting, which means 30 connection fittings, but also only 10 rings to crimp it down. A single connection requires two rings (one on each side), and one of those T fittings requires 3. You will end up only being able to make a maximum of 5 connections with this, and as little as 3 if you're using all the triple T connections. Really, it should come with more rings, otherwise you have a bunch of brass fittings left over that you can't do anything with!

First the good- these lights work as expected, they appear to be standard issue RGB strips like many other brands out there, and you get a good amount of length for the money which can be cut at pre-determined points to fit. They are not covered, but rather exposed LED circuits, which is normal for this sort of product (not a problem as long as you don't hit them or spill something on them to short them out or anything).
The bad: This took forever to install because the adhesive is GARBAGE. Like, utter trash. Getting the back to come off was torture, it keeps peeling away and leaving a layer of backing behind which prevents it from sticking. I had to go at it with a razor blade to get the proper amount of backing to come off and reveal the adhesive, and even then it was only good for a few inches of length before it tears and starts leaving a thin layer again, which needs to start over with the razor blade.
In the end, I spent hours getting the first strip up, and then never even bothered with the second. It was too much effort. Maybe I'll get some double sided tape and finish, but I can't recommend this product as is, knowing there are others on the market that do NOT have this issue.

This is a great rotary tool similar to a Dremel. I've borrowed my friend's Dremel brand rotary tool before to cut some plastic wedges off something to fit in my car, so I was excited to get my own to try. The thing is, I've used them before, but I've never set one up from scratch, and this came with no instructions beyond some safety and care info. I had to google how to properly set it up and watch some youtube videos just to properly mount the cutting tool. There are a LOT of different parts and tools included with this set, some of which I still need to learn how to use / what they are for, some basic instructions or a quick start guide would have been useful.
Otherwise, it works pretty great once you know what you're doing. It is pretty powerful at a claimed 350W motor, has a hard on/off switch and variable speed like the Dremel tool too. This is a pretty cool looking lamp, and it does a good job lighting up my son's work area on his desk. It gets plenty bright, and has three different color modes to match whatever ambient light you want (sunlight, indoor tungsten colored lights, and a blend of the two). It has a little fan built in that can blow a small draft of air towards you if you like, but don't expect this to circulate the air in a room or anything like that. It is more like a personal breeze in case your desk area feels stuffy. I think it is kind of silly but my son actually likes it so, points for that I guess! The timer function means you can also use it as a sort of night light, although I think if it had more options to set it would be more useful. Right now, it just has a timer on or off setting. You can't even adjust how long you want it to be.
I also have mixed opinions about the light up control dial. It lights up even when everything is off, which is useful to find it in the dark, however this is part of why I feel it is better suited in a kids room. I wouldn't want that light always glowing at night in an adult / college dorm room, personally. For kids, it's cool and almost like a glow in dark / nightlight sort of thing.
Otherwise, as a desk lamp, I think it works well and it has a really modern and cool design.

This is an impressive amount of light for a bulb small enough to fit in a standard E26 base. I have used large LEDs inside of a softbox for photography but usually for this much output you need a larger bulb that requires a wider base for support (E39). This actually comes with adapters for that in case you want to use a a fixture made for a larger base, but the beauty here is that this will natively fit in a standard light socket made for traditional sized (E26) bulbs.
Since it comes in a set of 2, I was able to put them into matching softbox kits to light a subject with light that is similar in color to window light.
I say similar because I actually found this has a bit of the green spike that some LEDs have compared to photographer bulbs in the 5000+ Kelvin spectrum. If you want to avoid that, you might need to get more expensive bulbs made for photo/video use. But for high output bulbs that fit in a standard socket, this is a great value for the price! In my experiments with it so far, it was close enough to mix with window light in photography.

This is not a bad face serum. It has some good ingredients like hyaluronic acid and Vitamin C. It comes in this blue dropper bottle that is pretty cute.

So yes you are treating your face with some good ingredients, but neither does it look nor does it feel great to the touch.

I hate adhesive chair pads, we have hardwood in our eat-in kitchen and dining room, and the sticky felt pads fall off when you don't expect them to, not only allowing the chairs to scratch the floor, but also leaving a sticky adhesive mess on the floor that needs to be cleaned up. But not these! These stay on the legs without adhesive and therefore won't fall off on their own or leave residue!
The only thing I to note is to make sure they are the right size/shape for your furniture. Our kitchen chairs are round on the bottom but too thin for these. The dining room chairs are the right thickness but not round. We were able to squeeze them onto the dining room chairs and are happy with it so far, but I wish they fit on the kitchen chairs as well. I really liked that these lights had a 6 hour timer function on them. Worked perfectly with our other Christmas lights. Problem, each light has a separate remote. I turn them on and press set timer. There is no way to know if it worked until the next morning when you find that 1 then 3 were on all night! They take 4 batteries each so that's a lot if they don't turn off. Brightness is good and we enjoy them. Just wish we could manage to get the timer right on all 4.

This is pretty near PERFECT solution to a real problem we had! We recently bought a home that had motion sensor lights for the front walkway which frequently misfired and turned themselves on when not wanted (wind blowing a tree branch, neighbor's dog, etc). There was no switch associated with these lights, so we looked to add one.
This product is MUCH cheaper than hiring an electrician and MUCH easier than opening up the walls to run a wire to a switch! It comes with little crimp cable connections that easily attach to the existing wiring, much simpler than wire nuts and fast too... which I'm thankful for since I need to do this while balancing on a ladder! And once connected, each unit can be paired with any of the 3 buttons on the remote control switch included (even more than one- which I'll get to in a minute).
Here's the caveat, however: These are NOT pre-paired. I didn't know that. The first time I tried to wire one up, I closed everything up, turned the power back on, tried all the buttons and nothing worked. I had to actually unmount the light AGAIN to get to this receiver and press the button to put into pairing mode. Make sure you do that before putting everything back in the wall and remounting the fixture. It's annoying only because you need the power on in order to pair, and I usually only turn my breaker back on once the wires are put back (and I can't reach the receiver anymore).
So what I actually did is take an appliance plug I happened to have lying around in my garage, and I wired THAT up to the receivers, and plugged each one into an outlet to pair first. Once paired with a button, I went out and installed the other two (I learned my lesson after the first one).
An interesting feature to note is that each button on the remote switch panel puts out an RF signal, and each receiver in the wall can be paired with more than one. For example, first I paired one with the wrong button, so I did it again with the other button, only to find that now BOTH buttons would toggle power on that light. You have to press-and-hold to clear all previous programming first if you don't want 2 buttons doing the same light. Then again, this opens up some interesting possibilities, such as one button toggling all three lights, where as other buttons can do individual power if needed, and therefore make nested groups of control.
If they came pre-programmed to buttons on the remote (but could still be re-programmed if desired), I'd have given this product 5 stars. Since I had to take it out and pair it after installation, I'm removing a star.

I have a few light fixtures in my home that require these smaller bulbs, and the problem has traditionally been that they don't come in bright enough sizes. Unless you want to get the "corn cob" style LED, which sometimes looks funny without a diffuser.
This is some sort of magic bulb that resembles a traditional tungsten filament inside, but they claim is "100 Watt equivalence" out of only 8 watts. Well, to be fair, a REAL 100 Watt tungsten bulb should output something like 1600 lumens, and this is only rated at 800. There are plenty of other brands that make the same claim so I can't fault them for that, however I have another "100 Watt" LED that puts out 1400-1500 lumens, and you can tell that this is significantly less bright. That being said, I'm impressed at how natural looking these bulbs are for LEDs.
The super bright tiny bulbs usually need to resort to "corncob" style LED formations, which are many little LED points of light that look like... well, corn. And while those work, it doesn't look as nice without a diffusion cover to mask all the bright dots. This type of bulb can work in a fixture with an open bulb design, because they actually look really natural with no cover at all. That's cool. And for the money, you get 6 of them in a neat little package. I like that too. All in all, these seem like a good value and I'd recommend them, as long as you don't actually need 1600 lumens per bulb!

There's a lot I like about this plug. It looks nice and was really easy to set up! I didn't even need to install their app, as it works with the more standard Smartlife app which I already have from other devices in my home. Since Smartlife is already hooked up with my voice assistants (Alexa), I didn't need to do anything else! Once the app found it, it was automatically added as an available plug to control from Alexa. If you don't already have something like this setup, the usual process is to install their app, configure the device, then go to your digital assistant and add the skill for that manufacturer's product (Geeni has its own skill for Alexa). I was able to set up a schedule for it to automatically come on and off at desired times, and it seems to work as expected.
The ONLY issue is, it is on a circuit which gets shut off manually by a wall switch from time to time (the entire room's outlets are on switches). Unlike some other smartplugs I've used, this device will NOT remember what state it was in when it got unplugged. It will default to OFF upon power loss. That isn't so cool. Every time someone shuts the power off to the plug, even if the light was on, when you turn the power back on you'll have to manually go over and press the button on the plug too. Or use Alexa/app/etc. It's an extra unnecessary step that I found annoying.
To be fair, most people don't use it this way and have the plug set somewhere with permanent power going to it. My setup is admittedly atypical, however this is a feature which I've noticed OTHER smart switches can handle so I have to remove at least one star for not working the way I expected. I'll probably swap a different smart plug into that location and use this one somewhere that never gets shut off.

These are great energy efficient bulbs, however I hate the use of "equivalents" as they are so loosely interpreted by vendors in my experience. While I love the color (3000k is the sweet spot) and the amount of energy they use, they are not as bright as other "100 Watt equivalent" products. However, looking at the specs it is easy to see why. These are rated at only 1100 lumens, whereas my brighter LED bulbs produce more like 1500-1600 lumens. That's around 50% more light. Granted, they also consume an extra couple of watts of real power as well, which makes sense. But the numbers are confusing, since 800 lumens is considered a 60-75watt tradition incandescent equivalent. Since no one has ever heard of an 85-90Watt bulb, anything over 1,000 lumens they have to round up to say 100w.
It's all very confusing and honestly, people need to stop using incandescent equivalents and start looking at strictly lumen light-output numbers for comparison. For the stated specifications, I'm happy with this bulb and would recommend it. But don't expect it to be the "brightest" 100w bulb. It's definitely brighter than my "60w" LEDs, which is what I wanted to replace.

Well, I have to say, there aren't as many bugs around, however I can't say there are none attracted. I did notice a few gnats/mesquitos circling under the light the way they do when they catch a light source. When I turned off the bulb they went elsewhere. So it helps, but isn't completely bug proof.
However I really do like how much more light there is for less power. 650 lumens while only using 8w is an incredible value that will no doubt save me a lot of money down the road. So, I recommend it as a low cost alternative that doesn't repel bugs, but will attract noticeably less.

These are fantastic for low profile ceilings that don't have room for traditional cans. For example, a room with a cathedral style ceiling that lacks an attic space above. I also like that the light emitters are built in and don't require any additional bulb inside to illuminate, however I wonder if that's also a con. LEDs do last a long time, but I have LED Edison bulbs in other light fixtures in the house that have blown from power outages and what not. I wonder how difficult it would be to replace any of these if one blows out, since it is all embedded together. Also, if you decide you want to change the color, right now they sell 3 different k-temperature colors (lower number is more warm color like incandescent,  higher number is more blue like sunlight). There were times that we wanted to try out more sunlight-matching color and switched all our bulbs to 5000k, then went back to a mix with 3000k or 4000k, and I'm a little concerned that with these installed, I'm committed to sticking with this color since it is more difficult to ever change them. You can't select the desired color after the fact, you need to buy the appropriate one. So in some ways, I still prefer real, easily replaced bulbs. But, I do like that this can be installed in places those cannot, and they are surprisingly bright for their size. So 4 stars for being useful enough that the pros outweigh the cons!

I was about to leave this a poor review because I tried it and it never seemed to work the way I expected. Putting it into a fixture, it just turned on and stayed on the whole time. I tried covering the light sensors, I tried turning it on while the light sensors were covered and then uncovering them... no matter what I did, it just seemed to work like a normal light bulb. So I decided to just leave it like a normal light bulb, and then within 24 hours it was turning itself off in the daylight and back on at night. Apparently it doesn't respond right away, but rather takes some time to "calibrate". I suppose they were trying to avoid the frequent on/off when the light changes temporarily thing, but honestly they could have said that in the instructions. But there was no proper instruction booklet or any information that tells you to wait for calibration or anything like that. I wish it were more predictable, looking at the reviews here I see that others had similar experiences. So, if it doesn't work right away, DON'T PANIC! It should eventually sort itself out.

I have kids that frequently forget to close the garage door into our home, which has let in all kinds of humidity in the summer and cold in the winter. It's not a heavy door, I just wanted something to make sure it closes, and this is perfect for that. It's actually a big smaller of a closer mechanism than I expected, but it does the job... as long as my attic fan in the garage is off, that is!
The garage has a fan to blow out hot and humid air, and it sort of creates a vacuum effect when it is running and the door closes, so much so that it refuses to close the last inch without manually pulling it. When the fan is off it works as expected and closes it all the way. If I adjust it more to close harder, it becomes too difficult for my younger kids to open. I'm happy enough with this to leave it for now, it's very good, if not perfect.

That's how I originally programmed my Honda's built in button for opening the garage and it has worked great for years! I was hoping this one would be the same type of system, but alas- I had to get a ladder and climb up to garage opener system to find the settings and figure out how to set the remote to work with it.

I love the Trackmaster toys as an alternative to wooden railways that are more expensive, less detailed and not as suitable for power.  However, the design flaws and poor quality coming from Fisher Price need improvement.  In the case of the tender locomotives, the battery is carried in the tender and the motor is in the locomotive.  Only a thin plastic coupler holds them together and an electrical wire between the two will break when the coupler fails.  It's short and difficult to splice.  The result is a ruined toy simply because the child carries the locomotive around by holding one segment or the other.  My boys have a cousin with virtually every Trackmaster locomotive.  Most of the tender locs have broken couplers.  Fisher Price needs to fix this so the battery is in the engine and the tender can decouple without breaking the toy.

I like the Trackmaster series.  Some of the tank locs my children have a real diehards.  Still, when I went to get more at Walmart, I ended up returning several multiple times before I got good ones.  The problem with Thomas was the frame was twisted so that when he rolled down the tracks, only three wheels touched the track.  One of the front wheels kind of rode up in the air.  If you pushed down on him, he would rocker over onto that wheel.  The front axels are split.  There's two spindles instead of a solid axle across the front.  Looking at my old Stepney engine, it has a solid axle.  The solid axle requires the manufacturer to assemble it and swage a cap on the end of the pin.  With the current Thomas design, they just pop two independent spindles on each side of the plastic frame.  It's cheaper to make.  The problem is, if the plastic frame isn't straight (not a chance) the pins will be out of alignment and so will the wheels.  The design is made cheap to make as if the Chinese labor isn't cheap enough already.  If Fisher Price wants to go this cheap, they're going to have to discard a lot more of the flawed units which makes their cost go back up.  Instead they decided to make the consumer lose.

There are two zip type strips that slip through the two tops that come with the game. There is a handle in the zip type strips. The idea is that you hold the starter tool after slipping the zip strip into the top and then quickly pull the strip out of the top and place it in the arena.

The tops spin all over the arena knocking into each other and the last top left standing wins.

This game would be more fun if:

1. The arena was bigger. More room is needed for the tops to really spin around nicely

2. The inner discs were more varied in design and sturdier.

3. The tops were equal in performance. In this kit one of the tops is a dud. Plain and simple.

My family is not overly thrilled with this game- in fact we were bored. Who wants to play a game when one top is always slanted against and loses?

I may not be a proven purchaser of this game, but check out my game pieces, they speak for themselves. What I like about this game is every game is different. Did everybody put their games in the dungeon already? My box is still out and looks like it’s gone through battle itself. We’ve logged over 1000 games on it. We got so good at beating the game we had to make the game harder. So we purchased Merlin’s Company and added “some” of the bad cards from it into our original game. Plus, having the 8th guy is simply a must. Now we can play 4 players, each having 2 guys. So worth it! We now have the perfect game and we’re winning by the slightest of margins and are usually covering the 6th Black sword to get the job done. I bet we lost the first 98/100 games we played while we were still figuring the game out. But don’t get discouraged, when you play it enough, you’ll figure out what NOT to do next time. A hint: Guinevere is evil. What she does to you, you can’t recover from. She makes you waste precious Merlins needed for doing other good things, so don’t go where she can hurt you. Being forced to go home and lose a turn is hard enough to recover from much less lose all your work on a solo quest too. This game is very well done and just another Great Game from Days Of Wonder.

The product does not deserve a 4.5 star rating overall. Based on the deceptive product image (single color in reality vs vibrant multiple colors in marketing image) this product should have received 3 stars at best and most of their sales should have gone to competitors.

Well I took Three Dragon Ante to "Game Night" joining all my friends bringing various card and board games to try and play for the night. Since this was my game I learned the rules and explained the concepts thoroughly to the group and the 6 of us began to play. As much as I looked forward to this game obviously my friends, who are avid gamers (including Magic and Poker) and they were not impressed. I dominated the game that we played taking the first two "pots" and everyone quickly lost interest, stating it was boring and relied too much on chance (although Poker relies on many of the same things they complained about). Oh, well I moved on and we played other games.

As far as the game goes it does involve a great deal of strategy because there can be so many different objectives for each player. Maybe you are lying low this round to build better cards and just limit your gold exposure, or maybe you low play, waiting to play your card that makes the winner have the lowest score to steal the "pot."

If you like casual card games with a Poker type feel and you like fantasy stuff, buy it. It is a very cheap game and I can see where certain play groups would really enjoy it. I know I would like to play it more down the road.

While I can usually "trick" my 10 and 13 year old sons to play Classic Uno, I wondered would this version of the game appeal to them more because it has a computerized component. After all, the robot is cute with it's little voice, and the cards fit nicely in a holder on it's head. Especially because today's generation plays XBox, Nintendo, PSP, and the WII. I hoped that they would enjoy it especially on family night.

Surprisingly, this version was extremely interactive, entertaining and tons of fun....throwing out names and house rules, which provided a new twist to an already enjoyable game. The "new" additions to the Classic include: the ability to record the names of the players; the ability to add creative house rules where the last person to complete the task draws two cards; and the ability to add surprise commands. In other words, you can make this game as funny as you want.And while my kids initially thought it was lame, they have asked to play it several times creating silly rules and cute nicknames. I definitely think this game will appeal to some.

I thought maybe I should set this up before any of the kids came over. I got the app set up on my phone and tried to use it. No luck. After getting it paired with my phone and calibrated, I never got past the first step.

What a huge disappointment. I am glad that I didn't just hand this over to the grandson. Maybe one of our adult kids will have better luck with this. I sure didn't.

I would suggest that you keep looking and pass this one up.
The firetruck is much smaller than I anticipated. Maybe the dimensions were listed but I dont remember seeing them. The truck is about a foot long

I bought this for my girlfriend and it looks a lot cuter in the picture online (on multiple different monitors/displays). When I received the item, I immediately noticed how much uglier it looks in person. It's very similar to what you see online but the stuffed animal I received had a lighter more pale color in the face. Multiple people I have asked also confirmed that it looks better online.

That said, it still looks very realistic and has that hilarious look you see on its face. I frequently walk into the room and just from a glance, and from 10 feet away, think that there is a real dog in the room.

This stuffed animal is also very firm, so don't expect it to be very cuddle-able. It was delivered cleanly in a large clear plastic bag within the cardboard box.

this was so cheaply made.  it will fall apart on you almost instantly.  not designed for kids because its not sturdy at all.  Not like the old games we used to have.  the plastic is too think to hold up.

Really didn't like that you have to download an app in order to play this game.

I pull out board games to get myself and others off our phones, and at only 2-4 players I don't think this will be something I will feel like playing often
These are not for beginners, as the instructions/guidebook isnt included. There is a QR code on the box that should be the link for a guidebook but it does not work at all.

Really it's not so bad as far as fun for the kids, but I was a bit surprised that it was so much smaller than the picture. In the photo they Photoshopped a child on the mat, in all honesty, it's not very large at all, but maybe better for a 3 year old. My 9 year old is of average height and the keys are quite small, but still fun. I don't like the fact that I have to keep the noisy motor running in order to pull the trigger when I want.  I ordered this for the nerf gun wars that I have with our grandson. This turned out to be a great gift for my 1-year-old niece. She loved it. It is well made, and the fact that there are several activities offered in such a small product makes it a great buy, in my opinion.

Our Grandson liked this set. He loves the name brand set ( but they can be so costly ) He put this together even with the poor instructions. He liked this set but said he prefers the name-brand sets better. I do too.

This set was nice but not as impressive as I had hoped for.

As I get older, I become more determined to complete some stuff on my bucket list. One thing on that list is to learn how to play chess. This Pavillion wooden chess set will help me accomplish this goal. This set is a chess and checkers set. My son has been teaching me chess, and while right now I am not the best player, I am enjoying using this lovely set and learning the game. It has a wooden, foldable board. Inside/underneath the board, the chess and checker pieces can be housed/stored. The 56 wooden pieces (32 chess and 24 checker) appear to be hand carved. They are easy to hold and move. I like that this set is portable and you can take when you travel. Since it folds, it doesn’t take up much space and is easy to store, especially the pieces since they are housed on the back/inside. The only thing I noticed is that the pieces are sometimes difficult to pull off, which caused one of the velvet holders to come apart from the inside of the set. I had to glue it back.  Otherwise, this is a nice set for a beginner. Let the games begin!

These were so cheap. 6 of them were misshapen and unusable. You have to push them forward, but they won’t stay. My students said they fell apart and didn’t work. The foil material it’s made of was scratched up and a few of them look like an animal had chewed on them. Don’t waste your money. Go to the dollar tree and get pinwheels that work.
My son really liked this toy, unfortunately, as the reviews stated, the button broke within 2 days. Im disappointed because he really liked it, but I would not buy another one.

Assembly wasn't the easiest. The instructions are easy enough to understand, but how the item needs to be assembled requires some weird positionng. For example the grey sensors under the hoops have to be attached with a washer and screw. However, you have to hold it up out of your way while also holding up the entire backboard then use one hand to hold the screw and another to put the washer on. Someone didn't consider how difficult assembling this would be with other things in the way and how you only have two hands, but need to be doing four things. If there are two people then it is a little easier, but something like this shouldnt require two people for assembly. One of the washers was drilled incorrectly, rather than straight through someone drilled it to the side so the washer would not screw onto the screw. Therefore, only one hoop is useable because the other is only tightened with one screw and starts twisting if you try to shoot the basketball through it. I would have given it more stars if not for the assembly and the messed up washer.
This carrying case is as advertised. It's well-made, fairly durable, and will hold your toy cars well.

11 month old loves it. Needed 300 balls to fill the head part. Maybe could have gotten away with 250. Don't go less than that if u make this a ball pit. Kids sized though a very thin adult could wiggle through if necessary to coax a child through.

We pulled this game out to play after dinner on Thanksgiving. We played a few rounds and I think it was an okay time but most of the game was driven by everyone just wanting to play a board game, not so much that the game was that fun.

This game is a yes/no guessing type game. There are different categories and "help" cards you can use. To be honest this game felt like a more complicated and less fun version of other games. Bc it's only yes/no some of the items are pretty difficult to guess. For example "social media analyst". Actually we found most of the career/professions category very challenging, in comparison to the landmark category which were always too easy.

I think the headbands are kind of cool initially but they really don't add that much and are just gimmicky when it gets down to it.

In my opinion this game is better than nothing, but it's definitely not one of my favorites.

This was a waste of money. Purchased this so the kids can play with it in the pool. Ordered the 3-pack, 3 boats/3 remotes.

- 2 boats were controlled by the same remote simultaneously. Someone said I had to change the frequency...uh how?
- 1 boat wouldn't work with any remote at all.

So that's 1 working remote control boat. 2 dead remote controllers. 1 mimicking boat. 1 dead boat.

So much for 3-pack, 3 kids.

They all lost interest real quick and went back to their normal pool toys. The plan was to have boat races, and obstacle courses using their other floaties but it was a complete bust.

I got this set of puzzles as a gift for my niece and she loves them. The puzzles are well-made (nice and thick) and hold up well to being trampled and tossed around. She is engaged while putting them together, and follows the background images, so this is great for development.

The toys were smaller than expected. Cute set but not as impressive as hoped.

Everything you need is in this black box, with the exception of appetizers and wine. Enjoy.

I love the Trackmaster toys as an alternative to wooden railways that are more expensive, less detailed and not as suitable for power.  However, the design flaws and poor quality coming from Fisher Price need improvement.  In the case of the tender locomotives, the battery is carried in the tender and the motor is in the locomotive.  Only a thin plastic coupler holds them together and an electrical wire between the two will break when the coupler fails.  It's short and difficult to splice.  The result is a ruined toy simply because the child carries the locomotive around by holding one segment or the other.  My boys have a cousin with virtually every Trackmaster locomotive.  Most of the tender locs have broken couplers.  Fisher Price needs to fix this so the battery is in the engine and the tender can decouple without breaking the toy.  I still recommend Trackmaster, just get the tender locs that do not have "talking" features or RC so the battery will be in the engine.  Those locs will be durable as long as they are made properly from the start.  FP has quality issues with their China factory too.

We normally put smaller Lego sets together with the grandkids. This one we could see was going to be way too hard for small kids. So, we thought let's do this together. Let me just say that my first Lego set was 5 decades ago. Things have changed a lot since then.

This dude has a ton of pieces. Many of them are very small and the same color. I lost count of how many times I put a brick just one space off and had to remove several and start back at a previous level. I did say a few curse words. At least the bags are numbered making it easier to go from step to step. The instructions are decent. There were too many times when we could not tell exactly where a piece went. Lego does send some extra pieces BUT that does not make up for them shorting us several pieces that we needed. We had to go to the garage and find pieces from old sets to fill in for the pieces they shorted us.

Maybe we should have tried a much smaller, easier set than this one. It took us about two partial days to get this set done. She got me the pieces for each step and I put them on. Like I said, sometimes not getting them exactly in the right spot. Trying to figure out which piece was off was a big challenge for me. I found out that it was better just to remove all pieces from the current stage we were on and start over. The book of instructions is huge with over 500 steps. Some steps were extremely simple and others were very challenging. ( at least for me )

The box says ages 10+ I am not sure that many 10-year-olds would be able to do this.

Anyway, we got it done with some cussing and some laughing. Next time we will try an easier set. LOL

When I saw this product was available, I thought that I would order it for my 10 year old son, who wants to see the movie Captain America when it is released later this summer. After receiving it, I asked him to write a review from the perspective of a 10-year-old. This is what he had to say: "The Captain America Shield is really fun. It is easy to put together(he literally read the directions and assembled the parts all by himself). I just had to add 4 batteries and place the foam discs in it. When you shoot the discs you hear a sound, like a siren. But the sound can get kind of annoying. Other than that, the shield is pretty fun". From a parents perspective, I thought that (1) the easy to read instructions allows kids to problem solve and work independently. (2) The sound when the discs are fired acts as a warning system (kind of a fail safe although I don't think the foam discs could cause any damage). And if a child ever accidentally fired the discs in the direction of another child's eyes, although I don't think that they will be injured, they will at least hear it coming. (3) The armband and handgrip are perfect for holding the shield. And (4) it seems like fun. While my 10 year old (it was his birthday today) had fun, I do think that a slightly younger child (7 or 8) would have more fun and not become bored as quickly as an older one.

I have been around Barbie dolls my entire life. My mom collected them and my sister got into it. My daughter has had a few here and there, but none of them was the quality that this doll is. I did not expect it to be fantastic quality, but it even fell short of my lower expectations.

WHAT IS THE DOLL LIKE?
The doll comes in a clear plastic package which is not overly attractive, in fact it looks stark and not like the usual Barbie stuff. The doll itself is packaged with two pieces of plastic sticking out from inside her head. It is then attached to the board in the back so you have to cut those pieces to get it off of the backing. That means you have to be careful of the hair. Once you managed to do this, you have to stick the plastic ends back into the skull of the doll to keep the sharp ends from pricking your child or you.

The outfit is kind of cute and the colors are attractive. The doll itself is a rigid plastic doll- not the bendable twist and turn type that they have had in the past. The head piece has a knob type attachment which makes it able to move in almost any direction. The problem is in this case, the head is so easy to move it is borderline bobble head.

HOW DOES THE HAIR COLOR WORK?
The hair color looked like it would be a lot of fun! It has a plastic piece that is similar to a flat iron in shape. Inside the *flat iron* piece are a group of colored waxy blocks. The idea is that you comb the doll's hair and separate it into strands. You press the flat iron type piece over the hair, squeeze it shut as you draw the hair through and it leaves the color on the hair.

The hair is waxy in feel, probably to make the color work better than traditional Barbie hair. In this case, however, it does not seem to do much for the process. The hair colors are not too great. They come off as crumbly and waxy and very spotty on the hair. If you run your fingers over it, you will get some on your fingers. This will stain so if your child plays with this near anything white or light colored, it could stain it.

The color blocks are almost like you mix waxy crayons with silicone to make it a bit softer. The result is uneven kind of unimpressive color on the hair. It is not overly bright and does not look like the pictures promised.

THE BOTTOM LINE
My daughter was not impressed by this doll. The body is very rigid, making for a boring play toy. The hair is very uneven and has a waxy feel to it. The colors come out crumbly and uneven, and color blocks probably will only last for a day or two of constant play and then run out. The colors will stain some things so if you plan to let your child run free with this, you may regret it if you have anything white you don't want stained. The head is so easily moved that it is unpleasant to play with and use. The outfit is cute and colorful and the shoes are not too bad either.

My daughter kept saying that she expected more substantial color that was like a watercolor marker or something instead of a crumbly waxy substance that barely sticks to the hair in a few random spots. She kept taking out her other Barbie dolls and saying how much more she liked those. I give this two stars because it has a nice outfit, and if you want a disposable toy for your child, it will serve the purpose. If you want a sturdy toy they will play with over and over, I am thinking this might not be it for you.

I bought these to replace my Turtle Beach X41's because even with the Microsoft Adapter for $24.99 I still have a hard time chatting with folks on my XBOX One. If I'm playing Battlefield or COD and I'm not going to be chatting with anyone I go back and use my X41's because I think they sound better then these Stealth 500X.

Pros:
1) I like the surround sound aspect. They sound very realistic with helicopters flying around and bullets whizzing past you.
2) I like how clear I can hear others and they can hear me while playing COD Ghosts (the alien maps) (so far that's the only game I chat in unless I'm in party chat on Battlefield 4 with my friend).
3) I like that they are COMPLETELY wireless. Now when I get out of my gaming chair to go get a snack I don't have to take my headphones off ;o)

Cons:
1) After doing all the downloads and updates and using Turtle Beaches recommendations for playing a "shooter" game the sound has a lot of echo and they sound like you are listening inside of a tin can.
2) There is not a lot of treble in these headphones. There is a lot of bass which sound good but I would have liked to have had more treble in them. When I break a window in Battlefield 4 on my Turtle Beach X41's it sounded exactly like glass breaking. On these there is no tinkling of broken glass. Just a lot of bass. This would be a 5 star product if the software wasn't so terrible. iCue is slow, buggy, and crashes on a regular basis. Sometimes the headset wakes up and works in seconds, other times it can drag out to 30+ seconds. If you click on iCue in the system tray to open it, the software might eventually open. Keyword: Might. Eventually. If it does open, moving from screen to screen takes longer than your Grandma would take to run a marathon.

For some reason the percentage of battery life remaining is buried in the settings, and only occasionally shows when connected. It doesn't show at all when charging so you have no idea how charged it is until you unplug it.

The firmware of the headset is version 0.02. Yes, ZERO POINT ZERO TWO. Sometimes the voice prompts play when you hit buttons, sometimes you just get beeps. If you press the mute mic button it mutes, but when you unmute it the sidetone shuts off so it sounds like your mic went dead when it didn't, until you either hit mute/unmute over and over or power the headset off and back on.

Sometimes when fully charged the light on the headset turns green. Sometimes it stays yellow for days, but when you unplug it from charging iCue shows it's at 100% charged but the software was just too buggy to make the light turn green consistently.

The mic is too quiet. Even maxed out it barely registers when you test it unless you have the mic so close to your lips it tickles. The only way to make it a reasonable volume is to install a third party equalizer app named Equalizer APO and boost the Mic gain using it. This means it's a software issue not a hardware one that Corsair hasn't bothered to fix despite threads on it being all over gaming forums.

The only complaint I have about the physical hardware is the buttons for mic mute and power is hard to find by touch as they're smooth and have no ridges or anything else to identify them. This is a 5 star piece of hardware hobbled by 1 star software. Considering this product has been on the market since August 2017 there's no excuse other than Corsair being too cheap and lazy to fix the software.

Go buy something made by a manufacturer who knows how to hire decent programmers, Corsair definitely can't.

This was one BORING game. Button mashing, button mashing, some stupid dwarf i couldn't care less about, telling me to get something I didn't give a c*&^%^ about. Personally, I couldn't handle all the stupid names. In Skyrim, a sword that burns is called, duh... Sword of Burning... In this game it might be called, lewluiatnbuyhikhnn. As if the writer was saying, look how original and clever I can be... I tossed some scrabble tiles. WTH??? Fae? Is that like Fate except with no "t?" What the what the what about the fateless? What? What does that have to do with anything? You're gonna explain it to me? It'll only take 3 hours while the baddies are knocking at the door?? I have to listen to dorky Dr. Doolittle character dwarf, recite a book in monotone for minutes to even begin to care about what is what? And in the end, did I care? Not at all! Because there was nothing in what the idiot dwarf told me that said I should care because of this reason. I don't care about your stupid woman who left you! I don't care about your husband who's lost! Give me some semblance of reason to CARE! At least look attractive or something!! Why are you telling me this, anyway? I'm a STRANGER!!! Why do I have to do that??? Why the heck do I have to go there? What am I doing here?? Amensia? Really??? How original! Am I suppose to take over this world? Am I supposed to kill the evil one? Who was it I'm supposed to fight???? Mr. awhpiuyetrkj??? I couldn't get past the stupidity after 10 hours. 10 hours! I think I gave it a fair shot! I sold it back the next day for 18$. Bye bye 40$... I would've liked it a lot more I guess if I could kill all the annoying npc's. I guess people who play RPGs have a LOT of imagination and a lot of suspension of disbelief, and a LOT of forgiveness for silliness... I just didn't.
I can't believe the comparisons on websites made between this game and Skyrim. It's no contest. Skyrim is immersive because it is believable even while fighting dragons. This game is NOT imaginative, it's ridiculous, and NOT even entertainingly so. Skyrim has a given purpose, you're supposed to get powerful to kill the evil dragon, because you are destined to do so as the Dragon born. KoA? "Well, you just came back from the dead... Why don't you prance around until you find something to do?" People who liked the combat probably haven't tried just mashing "A". They'll probably kill the beasties faster... At least in games like Batman, it mattered HOW you kill your enemies; your tactics mattered. I didn't feel that in this game. I didn't see anything innovative at all in the combat, just copying of other games. I did read a review with a headline something like RPG nerd's RPG. Maybe I'm not a big enough RPG nerd? Well, I loved Mass Effect, Skyrim, Oblivion, Fallout.. Skyrim, you're in a world; in this game you are in a huge corridor full of annoying florescent things and totally-ineffectual people I really didn't care a bit about. I just couldn't handle it. The characters were too silly. The story was nonexistent or copied. Things that could've been explained in 2 words were turned into long drawn out speeches that meandered like your mom telling how she met your dad for the 551st time, except Fred, your uncle, is named Fredrickasonian, look how clever I am!!! I could've forgiven it, if it was meant to be silly, like in Bard's Tale or something. Nope. Meant to be serious, but just turned silly. Don't send me comments... I won't read them... it's my opinion.

Strange heading for a five star rating I know, so let me explain.
About the Collectors edition stuff,
The statue is really really awesome I love it, I'm confused as to why they gave the game in a plastic case instead of just putting it with the soundtrack in the steal case which has space for it. It's misses out on the potential of a great art book though.

About the gameplay,
The game still plays like core tekken, this is great, this is fantastic, it's the reason I still give it full stars. As a player that started with tekken 2 and played all right up to tag 2, I'm right at home. New is the rage arts and rage drive, the rage arts are like any modern fighting game super specials complete with cut scene type presentation, what's different is that it's accessible when health is low as opposed to fulling a bar, I welcome this feature. The rage drive allows you to crush through attacks, this I'm not a fan of because tekken for me was always about spacing, baiting and timing, not moves that can allow you to go through your opponents attacks. It does also depend on the low health rage mode and is either or when it comes to using rage drives vs rage arts.

About the story,
personally I think it was great but I hated the ending and it isn't really a "conclusion to the Mishima saga" as advertised because there are clearly more fights to be had involving Mishima blood relatives.

About the game modes,
Arcade could've been better with more fights and the cinematic ending I've come to expect, those endings are now story mode character endings
Treasure mode is basically the new go to offline mode, unlimited fights, unlocking customization options via fights and don't have to worry about health as survival modes
Online didn't seem to be working to great at the time of release so I haven't tried it.
Gallery and jukebox stuff i'm really here nor there about.

About my hate feelings,
The character roster, that's it really. I've played Michelle in tekken 2 and Julia in 3,4,5,6 and both tag games I used Michelle and Julia. Unfortunately neither is in tekken 7, this really sucks for me as the game feels so good yet I can't play with the character I want, with the style I like. they've also left out others like Anna, Lei and Zafina to name a few. I'm sure there are many whose favorite characters aren't present and share my feelings.
I now find myself in a situation where I need to find a new character to like and learn to the level my Julia was, I'm not prepared or really have to time for such and endeavor. Also since new tekken 6 characters were left out, it feels like a risk to focus on anyone completely new in this game.
I can rant on this more but I think it's enough.

Conclusion,
So yes, I love this game as it does feel like the tekken I know and love with improvements, but I pulled the unfortunate short straw with the exclusion of Julia. The right DLC would change my entire opinion and make me absolutely, wholeheartedly love this game.
