So I'm going to say right away that i'm a big fan of the Xenogames in general. It's the reason I decided now was the time to buy a switch, I'm also 100+ hours in.

That said I love this game, it feels like the xenoblade games before it but the combat does feel improved now that you no longer have to cycle through arts since they are now assigned to face buttons. The chain combos add a very satisfying feel to the combat as well.

A lot of complaints I read was that the combat takes too long, at first I agreed but when you use the combo chains effectively things feel pretty standard for a xenoblade battle.

The complaints of the map is all true, even with updates it's pretty easy to just be totally lost due to elevations mostly. If they add the follow guide thing from Xenoblade chronicles X then everything would be fine.

Another issue i saw people having was the high level beasts roaming around. I'm probably just used to it from xenoblade chronicles x because it doesn't bother me at all. Like X you learn your place in the food chain and almost like hungry shark, as you level up the usually aggressive beasts become humble no longer attacking unless provoked, this makes fetching stuff in previously visited locations easy.

I seem to always be a bit overpowered though so I tend to be able to stroll through new and expected dangerous areas without having to fight which makes things a little boring

Without spoilers, the story is great. In some ways I like it more than the xenoblade 1, X is different for me as it reminds me more of xenogears and xenosaga but its usually consider the weakest story-wise.

Obtaining the rare blades can be a pain though, 100 hours in and it looks like I'm still missing about half, perhaps they are mostly special story related/ quests ones.

Overall great for any fan of the series and new players as well, you don't have to play the others before it.

If you are comparing this to a > $200 keyboard, this may not meet your needs but for the price, this is really a great keyboard. It has become the rage to have a volume nob usually at the top right corner of keyboards these days. That is fine, I guess, but I prefer what Royal Kludge has done with this board using the nob as a switch for wired, 2.5GHz and the three bluetooth options. In my opinion, this is much more useful than a volume nob, especially when I can use the F-keys for volume just as easily. Using the nob to switch the keyboard from one device to another is not only easier than using the normal FN key + number key to switch between devices but it will provide a quick visual cue as to what device is currently active.

This is a gasket mounted keyboard. I am not a huge fan of a springy gasket keyboard. This is not such a keyboard. It takes a pretty good press to see any plate flex. What it does do is mute the sound of your keys. That along with the polycarbonate plate makes this keyboard a great option for a work environment where other cube mates may be annoyed by a loud keyboard.

The Sky Cyan switches are super smooth and creamy. They are Royal Kludge's own branded switch and a great option for those who like a linear switch with a great feel. I prefer a top loaded tactical switch so I did replace the installed ones with something of my own choice but I will likely find a good use for these switches. They really are good. One thing to note, they are really tight in the PC plate and will take some effort to remove.

I have the 2015 version, but I believe that the problems I'm listing will be the same for anyone.
You can search for this on the Web, but a whole lot of people ran into issues with the Shield 9 upgrade, especially those who are running Plex such as myself. Due to really poor version management issues on Plex, the Shield 9 launch was botched and Nvidia was forced to build a hotfix to deal with issues related to the new storage permission model while Plex had to deploy an OTA update. If you're lucky, those will install correctly and get you by, but the fixes still broke existing installations with large libraries. but for me, the installation will still crash the entire box and you're forced to hard reboot it to recover. moving my music library to a separate Plex server was my only solution.
The BIGGER problem though is the sudden discontinuation of support for certain Light Studio games such as Tomb Raider and Borderlands. Even though I have paid for those games, after forced factory reset, I no longer had access to these games. That just shows the extremely immature business model for Nvidia's first party studio and the greed that Nvidia has to try to push people towards the sub-par Geforce NOW service. Neither Google nor Nvidia will refund you for these.
To add insult to injury, the Play Games app is now broken on Shield 9, which breaks the license check for any first party studio games still left on the Play Store. You can find on the web other people who have suffered that same issue. So with these two combined, you can see that first party gaming experience should be avoided AT ALL COSTS.
If you really want a good experience after owning this for a few years, my best advice now is for you to install Emby on either an x86 tiny PC or a Raspberry Pi while getting a Premium, which provides HW transcoding support for BOTH architectures, and a real game console, whose game library support you can rely on for the long term.

First of all, I'll say like everyone else, that the game is amazing. I wasn't sure I'd like it as much as I did but most of it works great.
Back when shadow of the colossus (sotc) 1st came out I remember thinking that this is what I'd like zelda games to be. And in many ways this game reminds me of sotc. Some similarities:
*The horses have a mind of their own which you guide
*Stamina limitations for climbing etc
*Gathering of fruits and animals (lizard tails in sotc)
*The Devine beasts can be liken to colossi
But while sotc was a simple game, zelda botw has taken most of those ideas and evolved into a much more elaborate game.
Weapons and shields break, don't really love it but sometimes you genuinely have to prepare your gear before certain tasks are done.
There are tons of things to gather.
Your preparation can lead to frustrating battles or comfortable victories.
Unfortunately the game is so open that the story is given in recollected memories and early in game discussions. This due to the fact that most of the game can be considered optional content.
Exploration is the heart of this game, there's always something interesting to be discovered or do, some alternative way to defeat a group of enemies... There's always something. It's great how lively this world feels.
A nice touch is how the climate affects you and the various ways in which you counter these effects. Too cool? Spicy food and warmer gear. Thunderstorm? Put away your metal gear. Its all interesting and fun, you sometimes need to explore more and discover new stuff that would make these trips more comfortable.

One note is the learning curve, because it's so open, you can find yourself feeling pretty overwhelmed by the enemies you meet up. While this is mostly due to not being prepared, you can die a lot and meet some challenges.

Update July 2019: I've owned the keyboard a little over 3 years. Customer support hasn't answered and it's been almost a week so I am dropping the review to 2 stars. So typing is a nightmare I'm constantly using backspace. Typing passwords is almost impossible. I do LOVE a mechanical keyboard but having a 2 year warranty, and after paying $170 for your keyboard, watch it die in 3 years sucks. I did order a renewed K70 keyboard from the corsair outlet for $70, because these keyboards are fantastic once you get used them. And I couldn't really go to another brand since I've invested in the corsair mouse, and corsair pad. The CUE to make the RGB colors schemed is also fantastic. The K95 Platinum is the expensive keyboard from them right now, Rapidfire was pretty new when I got it, I'm switching to the LUX for a quieter key click.

UPDATE: Putting the stars back to 4 stars, this keyboard keeps pretty clean, I did have to buy a can of air to shoot some cat hair out ( I could have just pulled a couple keys with the key puller they sent... ) this review is going back to 4 stars because the RGB lighting is cooler than cool, it's ice cold! Thanks to my new graphics card also having RGB I'm rocking a sick red white blue setup between my gear... Keyboard the american flag, mouse white, headphones red, front case fan blue, graphics card red and white... All red is a little intense for me, but most my friends seem to like it the best. Sadly I use an Xbox 360 controller for most of my gaming right now.

UPDATE: Amazon Prime day July 12 2017, I've had this keyboard about a month, new rapidfire switches, I paid like $180, today it is $109. I wish I waited until now....

UPDATE to the UPDATE: The keyboard is nice, and being able to change the colors is cool, I don't have another keyboard with switches so I don't know if these rapidfire switches are that much better than regular MX cherry reds, I wish I saved the $100 and got a regular mechanical keyboard and used money towards a new video card.

UPDATE: After 12 hours of intensive gaming, I love this keyboard even if I need to get used to how sensitive the keys are... I find myself strafing left because my finger is resting too hard on the A key (I'm used to holding it halfway down to get a fast strafe). I am in the processes of ordering a replacement, my volume adjustment makes a horrible squeal when adjusting volume down (sounds like nails on a chalk board). I am leaving this at 4 out of 5 stars because my extra keys do not include 1-6, I received the key puller WASD in the top half of the package, then keys, Q,W,E,R,D,F. Not my most used keys, my W&S keys definitely get worn off the fastest since I run mostly FPS games, then the number keys wear out.

The first COD was epic, genuinely fun, and original. COD 2 was a step up. Everything that has come since COD2 has disappointed me. Since when did telling some half-ass concocted over-blown Hollywood-esque story take precedence over gameplay? These games used to be fun...you're thrown into a battle and you complete your objectives, hopefully killing dozens of human beings in the process. Who wants silly heroic cut-scenes every 2 minutes? Who wants a ridiculous michael bay/tony scott type story to follow? COD2 was my favorite because is is free of these things. There is no story to follow, only countless battle levels in various settings and locations with a diverse arsenal, which makes for a lot of replay-ability. This game (and the modern warfare games) are not re-playable. Once you finish it, you toss it aside. It's not memorable, or even that fun. The characters are silly, the story is ludicrous, and the gameplay is buggy and forgettable.

The graphic are mediocre.
The gameplay has gotten worse - since COD1
Too many cut-scenes.
Too much emphasis on the "story".
The maps are boring - the levels are redundant and simple.
The weapons are boring.
The sound is not very good.
I'll take WW2, Korea, or Vietnam over a future-terror-related storyline any day.

Came in today. Will update after a year of use and see how quality stands up to a k70 rgb corsair (which lasted years and is still going)

Cons:
1.Keys are wobbly; a little too wobbly for me. Not noticeable while using the keyboard but only when i actually look and move the keys around.
2. No volume control (arguably necessary for form factor)
3. Clicky aspect of the keys feels weird when pressed very slowly. I don't notice it when i'm actually using it normally. I've realized I would rather use their tactile but silent keys (i think orange) over these green switches. But i still enjoy the sound I guess, but it is a little pointless for me. The clickiness already from, for example, a k70 rgb is clicky enough without an extra clicky actuation.
4. Space bar is kind of loud, different obvious sound to the rest of the keys.
Pros:
1. RGB is better imo than corsair's.
2. I'm a sucker for the design (bought this keyboard almost strictly for the white)
3. Quickly accustomed to it, can type 90+ wpm with little errors straight out of the box (this might just mean i'm used to the key distance or irrelevant point)

Bottom line, the keyboard is "good enough" for my use. I mainly bought this keyboard for the aesthetics. Clickiness & keys feel a little cheap, so is it worth 115$ ? Arguably so. Buying my k70 rgb 3+ years ago, it was well over 150$. I would recommend this keyboard, unless the tactile feedback is what you don't want.

My 7 and 9 year old grandsons love this game. It's fun and funny.  Starts out easy so players can progress to harder adventures.  Several of the adventures are cleverly set to music and the boys absolutely love them - play them over and over, even after high scoring.  I have to admit all the adventures are entertaining to watch and I like the music too.  The graphics are fantastic and there is an enormous amount of variety in every aspect of the game: characters, situations, background designs, things to collect, rewards, etc.  The boys even found ways to bash each other and the creatures, which gets them laughing and jumping.  It is obvious that a great deal of thought and creativity went into the development.  Big high 5 to the developers of this game!

For awhile I was on nothing but destiny. I still love it. However I swayed away because I needed the change, and variety is important. I started with Dying light, side note here is that if you like a zombie game go get dying light right now. I loved the sh** out of that game. Could've been a bit longer.. I personally got rid of it after completion because I won't wait for or purchase "add on" garbage. because that's all it is.

Back to this game.
Graphically 4/5 I think theres some parts they could've improved but overall it's rather good. the system runs a little rough here and there, but It hasn't effected or bothered my game play yet. The classes are fun to be part of although I immediately changed my classes after getting through the first part with one class. If you've played DA in the past this is essentially a must have to feel complete.
The story is huge and i'm constantly picking up a new quest here and there while trying to complete another one for some fool who can't carry a sword to protect themselves.
I haven't even came to killing a dragon yet (although one killed me because I walked up on its roost and spooked it) and I'm already immersed in and loving this game. The more you get into this game the stronger it will hold you. There's endless areas to explore, things to collect, and evil doers to slay.

The menu system I think could've used a little bit of work as well, although i have grown more used to it. I get a bit mixed up gearing up all 4 of my characters at once trying to remember which one wears what can be stressful. however once you get past the rush style life we're used to and pay attention to what you have, one can carefully plan a magic defense, distance, or CQC defense depending on what area you're coming up on to attack.

I did do this game as a download through xbox live i'm not sure if the disk is any different but something I do love is that I turn on my xbox, watch some netflix or something and when I go back to the game it's loaded where I left, no boot up or anything, simply constantly ready. It's rather nice. It's made doing whatever I want or need to do easy and seamless to switch back to my game.

I've yet to get through the first map that i scouted a town at initially... i foresee many travels in my future. If you're up for a relax, tactical, strategic based game in which you can manage a few different classes at one time, watch reviews, see a twitch, check some youtube, and pick it up. this is NOT a fast paced game in any way.

One night while watching TV my husband and I jumped up because we were alarmed to smell burning plastic. We didn't know it was this heater at the time, and we tried narrowing it down but couldn't be sure if it was this heater or another (different) one we had on in the living room. A while later the smell went away.

After that I would smell it every now and then for the next couple of days, and I suspected this heater but couldn't be positive.

Today I smelled the heater again and thought I could hear a small noise coming from the heater but I didn't think much of it. I thought that something like dust might've gotten flown in there from sweeping all the time and that it'd go away soon. But then I heard more of an electrical "zap" noise and saw a huge spark come out the side of the heater. Turned that sucker off pretty quick after that.

My husband wanted to try to turn it on again to see what would happen and like other reviewers have said, it is now just blowing cold air out of it. The heater part must've shorted out.

I hope we can get our money back after only having it for two months, but I'm doubtful since I don't think we kept the receipt. If anything else noteable happens about returning this heater to the company, I'll add it here.

Update: The company wouldn't take the heater back since I didn't buy it from them, and where we bought it from wouldn't give our money back for it without a receipt. Instead they gave us a replacement. When this one breaks, I'm not really sure what we're going to do. I'm watching it like a hawk and so far it's been a month. If/when this one breaks too, I'll add to the review.

Update a week or two after the previous update, 1/7/2012: Well, it died. SURPRISE! Not. It didn't short out like last time though. This time when I went to change it from low heat to high heat, it simply made the little red light above the power buttong and high heat button stay on (sort of....they were more like wavering red lights) while the rest of it seemed to lose power. I unplugged it for a few minutes and plugged it back in since that's really all you can do to troubleshoot these things, but it stayed the same with the two little red lights and no other power.
I don't understand this since it's not even like we used a bad extension cord. The instructions say to plug it into the wall, but the cord is too short for that so I got a special extension cord specifically made for appliances like these heaters with certain wattage/voltage requirements, so we wouldn't short it out. But just like last time it didn't last. I suppose the company would say it's our fault for not plugging it directly into the wall or something, but it's not like we didn't go out of our way to help the short cord situation. And you should be able to use an extension cord with these heaters if you do your research (like for fans or vacuums or ANYTHING). There is no reason why it should just quit working except that it's junk. We're going to try to return it to the store AGAIN with no receipt and hope for store credit, but I'm not really getting my hopes up. Probably just got screwed.

Disclaimer: this is my first smart phone, so there is a learning curve. That said, I'm not a particularly picky person when it comes to this kind of stuff, so it doesn't take much to please me.

The phone is alright. My biggest peeve is that it doesn't have a shred of memory so you have to buy a mirco sd card to go with it before you plan on doing much other than making calls and texts and stuff. I should've probably known that before I bought it, but it's my first smart phone so what do I know?

My bad issues with my phone are basically due to the seller, Joycells. On different parts of the phone, like "bundles" and "channels" to choose from in the news feed area, or "device help", are all in spanish and either mexico- or argentina-based. As far as I've tried I've been unable to erase the mexican channels and bundles.
What made me most angry was that in the phone you can see the approximate data usage for the life of the phone. It said something like 13.65MB of data used since JUNE 18, 2011! So obviously this is a refurbished phone, not brand new.

I had a lot of trouble finding this phone and was really looking forward to it. I'm not very satisfied but I decided to stick with the stupid thing rather than return it just because I need a phone and it was a huge pain just to set it up and get service on it (didn't want to have to go through all of that again!). Since Joycells is an Amazon-approved seller, they gave me 20% of my money back and let me keep the phone rather than sending it back for 100% of my money back. Not sure if this was a wise choice or not.

Update 05/04/2014: So it turns out this phone really can't do much. Again, I should have known this by looking at the specs but seeing as how it was my first "smartphone," I had no clue. It is only Android 2.1 with no hope of updating it to anything more recent, so most apps aren't compatible with it. Apps such as most games, the Amazon app, Instagram, etc... so that's a huge disappointment.

On top of that--and I'm not sure, this might be just a STRAIGHT TALK issue--I am unable to receive mms (picture) messages. I can send them, but I can't receive them. I've tried EVERYTHING. I'm assuming this is Straight Talk's issue, so don't get this phone if you want to use them. Even their Straight Talk apps from the google app store are incompatible with it.

Overall, I'm pretty unhappy with this phone. It's about as useful as a year 2000 blackberry, or so it seems. I can get email and texts, and I can play a few select cheap games, but that's about it. I'll probably stick with it for 6 months or a year and then get something else. Which is a shame, because it's really cute and I get a lot of compliments on it. But for a "smartphone" it's really not that smart at all!

I will be returning this faucet because it was not a fit for my sink.  I understand that is my mistake and it has no bearing on the rating.  I only mention this so that others might double-check the measurements of their own sinks.  The finish of the oiled bronze faucet is pretty much black.  There is minimal to no bronze/copper metal shining through.  It has a slight matte appearance to it, and I can tell if you chose to, you could ‘wear down’ the finish to expose more metal underneath.  The faucet is heavy and well made, but did not come with any parts for the drain assembly.  I could purchase the oiled bronze drain from another retailer, but then it might not match this faucet.  I think if you’re selling a faucet, you need to go ahead and include the drain so that it is a matching set.  It’s great that they include the hot and cold water lines, but these are not likely to be needed if you are replacing an existing faucet.  Overall, it’s a good deal if you’re looking for a black faucet.

I've only had this for a few days, but I can't say I'm terribly impressed with it.

I've only managed to connect it to my phone once. I get a notification saying my band is recording my movements, but the app still tells me that I'm disconnected. So I click it to connect it and nothing shows up to connect it. The band itself will tell me I'm connected, but nothing shows up in the app. The one time I was able to connect it made my phone run hot and slowed it down. So the app is pretty useless. It doesn't store history, so you'd need to connect to the app every day to keep that information.

On the band itself, I wish I could pick and choose what functions to display. I do not need blood pressure or running (is this the GPS function?) or even heart rate. All I really wanted this for was for steps and distance. But you can't pick and choose what pages to flip through on the band. At least steps and distance are the first two.

I'm not really sure how accurate this thing is. I've only tested my resting heart rate twice, and I think it might be reasonable but I haven't tried it with activity yet. As for the blood pressure, I was sitting in the car yesterday and it told me my blood pressure was 151/101! I mean, grocery shopping can be annoying but I've never been that stressed about it. I've never had high blood pressure in my life. Why this is even on the wristband at all is a mystery to me since it'd be obviously very inaccurate considering how blood pressure is supposed to be measured.

I wore it to bed last night for the first time and it says I got 8.4 hours of sleep. Ok, I'll buy that I guess. But when I got up this morning it also said that I had walked 240 steps already. Unless I'm sleep walking--which I don't believe I do--that's a lot of extra steps for no reason.

I have it set for the wristband to turn on whenever I turn my wrist to look at it, but this function never works. I have to flick my wrist out and back in again, and sometimes that works but most of the time it doesn't. Usually I have to touch it to turn it on, which is fine, but it makes you wonder how accurate it really can be for anything if it can't even sense a wrist-flicking movement.

Lastly, I don't like wearing it to bed--but that's personal preference. Something bulky and hard like that on my wrist bothers me at night so I'm not going to bother with the sleep tracker. But when you take it off and lay it on a nightstand or whatever, a bright green light likes to shine and blink periodically. BRIGHT. It's from the underside where it would meet your wrist. So you'll need to cover it or stuff it in a drawer if that bothers you.

Overall I wouldn't recommend it. Look for something better instead of spending $30 on this thing.
